title: CycloneDX Transparency Expression Language
$comment: OWASP CycloneDX is an Ecma International standard (ECMA-424) developed in collaboration between the OWASP Foundation and Ecma Technical Committee 54 (TC54). The standard is published under a royalty-free patent policy. This JSON schema is the reference implementation and is licensed under the Apache License 2.0.

specFormat.title: Specification Format
specFormat.description: Specifies the format. This value shall be "CycloneDX".

specVersion.title: Specification Version
specVersion.description: The version of the CycloneDX specification the BOM conforms to.

serialNumber.title: BOM Serial Number
serialNumber.description: Every BOM generated SHOULD have a unique serial number, even if the contents of the BOM have not changed over time. If specified, the serial number must conform to [RFC 4122](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4122.html). Use of serial numbers is recommended.

version.title: BOM Version
version.description: Whenever an existing BOM is modified, either manually or through automated processes, the version of the BOM SHOULD be incremented by 1. When a system is presented with multiple BOMs with identical serial numbers, the system SHOULD use the most recent version of the BOM. The default version is '1'.

components.description: A collection of components. When a metadata component is present, this array represents the inventory of components associated with that subject, forming a bill of materials. When the metadata component is omitted, the array provides component data for interchange purposes without establishing a compositional relationship.

modelCard.$comment: Model card support in CycloneDX is derived from TensorFlow Model Card Toolkit released under the Apache 2.0 license and available from https://github.com/tensorflow/model-card-toolkit/blob/main/model_card_toolkit/schema/v0.0.2/model_card.schema.json. In addition, CycloneDX model card support includes portions of VerifyML, also released under the Apache 2.0 license and available from https://github.com/cylynx/verifyml/blob/main/verifyml/model_card_toolkit/schema/v0.0.4/model_card.schema.json.
modelCard.title: Model Card
modelCard.description: A model card describes the intended uses of a machine learning model and potential limitations, including biases and ethical considerations. Model cards typically contain the training parameters, which datasets were used to train the model, performance metrics, and other relevant data useful for ML transparency. This object SHOULD be specified for any component of type `machine-learning-model` and must not be specified for other component types.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the model card elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

modelParameters.title: Model Parameters
modelParameters.description: Hyper-parameters for construction of the model.

approach.title: Approach
approach.description: The overall approach to learning used by the model for problem solving.

type.title: Learning Type
type.description: Learning types describing the learning problem or hybrid learning problem.

meta:enum.supervised: Supervised machine learning involves training an algorithm on labeled data to predict or classify new data based on the patterns learned from the labeled examples.
meta:enum.unsupervised: Unsupervised machine learning involves training algorithms on unlabeled data to discover patterns, structures, or relationships without explicit guidance, allowing the model to identify inherent structures or clusters within the data.
meta:enum.reinforcement-learning: Reinforcement learning is a type of machine learning where an agent learns to make decisions by interacting with an environment to maximize cumulative rewards, through trial and error.
meta:enum.semi-supervised: Semi-supervised machine learning utilizes a combination of labeled and unlabeled data during training to improve model performance, leveraging the benefits of both supervised and unsupervised learning techniques.
meta:enum.self-supervised: Self-supervised machine learning involves training models to predict parts of the input data from other parts of the same data, without requiring external labels, enabling learning from large amounts of unlabeled data.

task.title: Task
task.description: Directly influences the input and/or output. Examples include classification, regression, clustering, etc.

architectureFamily.title: Architecture Family
architectureFamily.description: The model architecture family such as transformer network, convolutional neural network, residual neural network, LSTM neural network, etc.

modelArchitecture.title: Model Architecture
modelArchitecture.description: The specific architecture of the model such as GPT-1, ResNet-50, YOLOv3, etc.

datasets.title: Datasets
datasets.description: The datasets used to train and evaluate the model.

ref.title: Reference
ref.description: References a data component by the components bom-ref attribute

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: The input format(s) of the model

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: The output format(s) from the model

quantitativeAnalysis.title: Quantitative Analysis
quantitativeAnalysis.description: A quantitative analysis of the model

performanceMetrics.title: Performance Metrics
performanceMetrics.description: The model performance metrics being reported. Examples may include accuracy, F1 score, precision, top-3 error rates, MSC, etc.

considerations.title: Considerations
considerations.description: What considerations should be taken into account regarding the model's construction, training, and application?

users.title: Users
users.description: Who are the intended users of the model?

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.description: What are the intended use cases of the model?

technicalLimitations.title: Technical Limitations
technicalLimitations.description: What are the known technical limitations of the model? E.g. What kind(s) of data should the model be expected not to perform well on? What are the factors that might degrade model performance?

performanceTradeoffs.title: Performance Tradeoffs
performanceTradeoffs.description: What are the known tradeoffs in accuracy/performance of the model?

ethicalConsiderations.title: Ethical Considerations
ethicalConsiderations.description: What are the ethical risks involved in the application of this model?

environmentalConsiderations.title: Environmental Considerations
environmentalConsiderations.description: What are the various environmental impacts the corresponding machine learning model has exhibited across its lifecycle?

fairnessAssessments.title: Fairness Assessments
fairnessAssessments.description: How does the model affect groups at risk of being systematically disadvantaged? What are the harms and benefits to the various affected groups?

format.title: Input/Output Format
format.description: The data format for input/output to the model.

energyConsumptions.title: Energy Consumptions
energyConsumptions.description: Describes energy consumption information incurred for one or more component lifecycle activities.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Provides the ability to document properties in a name-value store. This provides flexibility to include data not officially supported in the standard without having to use additional namespaces or create extensions. Unlike key-value stores, properties support duplicate names, each potentially having different values. Property names of interest to the general public are encouraged to be registered in the [CycloneDX Property Taxonomy](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-property-taxonomy). Formal registration is optional.

energyConsumption.title: Energy consumption
energyConsumption.description: Describes energy consumption information incurred for the specified lifecycle activity.

activity.title: Activity
activity.description: The type of activity that is part of a machine learning model development or operational lifecycle.

meta:enum.design: A model design including problem framing, goal definition and algorithm selection.
meta:enum.data-collection: Model data acquisition including search, selection and transfer.
meta:enum.data-preparation: Model data preparation including data cleaning, labeling and conversion.
meta:enum.training: Model building, training and generalized tuning.
meta:enum.fine-tuning: Refining a trained model to produce desired outputs for a given problem space.
meta:enum.validation: Model validation including model output evaluation and testing.
meta:enum.deployment: Explicit model deployment to a target hosting infrastructure.
meta:enum.inference: Generating an output response from a hosted model from a set of inputs.
meta:enum.other: A lifecycle activity type whose description does not match currently defined values.

energyProviders.title: Energy Providers
energyProviders.description: The provider(s) of the energy consumed by the associated model development lifecycle activity.

activityEnergyCost.title: Activity Energy Cost
activityEnergyCost.description: The total energy cost associated with the model lifecycle activity.

co2CostEquivalent.title: CO2 Equivalent Cost
co2CostEquivalent.description: The CO2 cost (debit) equivalent to the total energy cost.

co2CostOffset.title: CO2 Cost Offset
co2CostOffset.description: The CO2 offset (credit) for the CO2 equivalent cost.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Provides the ability to document properties in a name-value store. This provides flexibility to include data not officially supported in the standard without having to use additional namespaces or create extensions. Unlike key-value stores, properties support duplicate names, each potentially having different values. Property names of interest to the general public are encouraged to be registered in the [CycloneDX Property Taxonomy](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-property-taxonomy). Formal registration is optional.

meta:enum.kWh: Kilowatt-hour (kWh) is the energy delivered by one kilowatt (kW) of power for one hour (h).

energyProvider.title: Energy Provider
energyProvider.description: Describes the physical provider of energy used for model development or operations.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the energy provider elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the energy provider.

meta:enum.coal: Energy produced by types of coal.
meta:enum.oil: Petroleum products (primarily crude oil and its derivative fuel oils).
meta:enum.natural-gas: Hydrocarbon gas liquids (HGL) that occur as gases at atmospheric pressure and as liquids under higher pressures including Natural gas (C5H12 and heavier), Ethane (C2H6), Propane (C3H8), etc.
meta:enum.nuclear: Energy produced from the cores of atoms (i.e., through nuclear fission or fusion).
meta:enum.solar: Energy produced from the sun (i.e., solar radiation).
meta:enum.geothermal: Energy produced from heat within the earth.
meta:enum.biofuel: Liquid fuels produced from biomass feedstocks (i.e., organic materials such as plants or animals).
meta:enum.other: An energy source that is not listed.

energySource.title: Energy Source
energySource.description: The energy source for the energy provider.

energyProvided.title: Energy Provided
energyProvided.description: The energy provided by the energy source for an associated activity.

externalReferences.title: External References
externalReferences.description: External references provide a way to document systems, sites, and information that may be relevant but are not included with the BOM. They may also establish specific relationships within or external to the BOM.

graphicsCollection.title: Graphics Collection
graphicsCollection.description: A collection of graphics that represent various measurements.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of this collection of graphics.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the graphic.

image.title: Graphic Image
image.description: The graphic (vector or raster). Base64 encoding must be specified for binary images.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of performance metric.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value of the performance metric.

slice.title: Slice
slice.description: The name of the slice this metric was computed on. By default, assume this metric is not sliced.

confidenceInterval.title: Confidence Interval
confidenceInterval.description: The confidence interval of the metric.

lowerBound.title: Lower Bound
lowerBound.description: The lower bound of the confidence interval.

upperBound.title: Upper Bound
upperBound.description: The upper bound of the confidence interval.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the risk.

mitigationStrategy.title: Mitigation Strategy
mitigationStrategy.description: Strategy used to address this risk.

fairnessAssessment.title: Fairness Assessment
fairnessAssessment.description: Information about the benefits and harms of the model to an identified at risk group.

groupAtRisk.title: Group at Risk
groupAtRisk.description: The groups or individuals at risk of being systematically disadvantaged by the model.

benefits.title: Benefits
benefits.description: Expected benefits to the identified groups.

harms.title: Harms
harms.description: Expected harms to the identified groups.

mitigationStrategy.title: Mitigation Strategy
mitigationStrategy.description: With respect to the benefits and harms outlined, please describe any mitigation strategy implemented.

annotations.title: Annotations
annotations.description: Comments made by people, organizations, or tools about any object with a bom-ref, such as components, services, vulnerabilities, or the BOM itself. Unlike inventory information, annotations may contain opinions or commentary from various stakeholders. Annotations may be inline (with inventory) or externalized via BOM-Link and may optionally be signed.

annotation.title: Annotations
annotation.description: A comment, note, explanation, or similar textual content which provides additional context to the object(s) being annotated.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the annotation elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

subjects.title: Subjects
subjects.description: The object in the BOM identified by its bom-ref. This is often a component or service, but may be any object type supporting bom-refs.

annotator.title: Annotator
annotator.description: The organization, person, component, or service which created the textual content of the annotation.

organization.description: The organization that created the annotation

individual.description: The person that created the annotation

component.description: The tool or component that created the annotation

service.description: The service that created the annotation

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the annotation was created.

text.title: Text
text.description: The textual content of the annotation.

behaviors.title: Behaviors
behaviors.description: Behaviors performed by objects within the BOM.

graphs.title: Graphs
graphs.description: Behavior graphs that model behaviour over time as activity flows, state machines, or a mixture of the two.

behaviorInstance.title: Behavior Instance
behaviorInstance.description: A behavior performed by one or more objects within the BOM.

actors.title: Actors
actors.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to objects that perform this behavior.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to objects affected by this behavior.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: The condition or event that initiates a behavior.

meta:enum.startup: Triggered during system or component initialisation.
meta:enum.shutdown: Triggered during system or component termination.
meta:enum.scheduled: Triggered at predetermined times or intervals.
meta:enum.event-driven: Triggered in response to a specific event.
meta:enum.signal: Triggered by a system or inter-process signal.
meta:enum.condition-based: Triggered when specific conditions are met.
meta:enum.unknown: The trigger mechanism is not known.

ordering.title: Ordering
ordering.description: Execution semantics for the nodes of a graph when transitions do not fully specify the order.

meta:enum.sequential: Nodes execute in ordinal order. Each completes before the next begins.
meta:enum.unordered: All nodes execute, but the order is not guaranteed.

acknowledgment.title: Acknowledgment
acknowledgment.description: Indicates how the behavior or graph was identified. Multiple values indicate the behavior was both declared and observed.

meta:enum.declared: The behavior was explicitly declared, designed, or expected. Typically used for threat modelling, security requirements, and architectural documentation.
meta:enum.observed: The behavior was observed, detected, or measured during analysis or runtime. Typically used for anomaly detection, incident response, and behavioural analysis.

behaviorGraph.title: Behavior Graph
behaviorGraph.description: A graph of nodes joined by transitions that models behaviour over time. A node may be an activity, a state, an event, a gateway, or a start or end point, so the same construct expresses an activity or process flow (where the nodes are activities), a state machine (where the nodes are states), or a mixture of the two.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the graph.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the graph and the behaviour it models.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: An optional hint at the intent of the graph.

meta:enum.activity: The graph models a process or activity flow whose nodes are activities.
meta:enum.state-machine: The graph models a state machine whose nodes are states.
meta:enum.mixed: The graph mixes activity and state nodes.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the object whose behaviour this graph describes.

transitions.title: Transitions
transitions.description: The transitions between nodes. Transitions express the ordering of an activity flow and the triggered, guarded moves of a state machine.

behaviorNode.title: Behavior Node
behaviorNode.description: A single node in a behavior graph. The kind determines what the node represents and which additional properties apply.

meta:enum.activity: An action that is performed, identified by a behavior, a behavior-instance reference, or a nested graph. The activity-flow counterpart of a step.
meta:enum.state: A durable condition the subject rests in until a transition fires. The state-machine counterpart of a node. A composite state references its nested region through 'graph'.
meta:enum.event: An intermediate event that occurs during the graph.
meta:enum.gateway: A branch or merge point whose outgoing transitions carry the guards.
meta:enum.initial: The starting point of the graph or of a composite node.
meta:enum.final: A completion point of the graph or of a composite node.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the node.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the node.

ordinal.title: Ordinal
ordinal.description: Position within a sequential graph. Required when the graph ordering is 'sequential'. Ignored when transitions define the order.

behavior.title: Behavior
behavior.description: For an activity node, the behavior performed, drawn from the taxonomy. Mutually exclusive with 'ref' and 'graph'.

ref.title: Behavior Reference
ref.description: For an activity node, a reference to a behavior instance. Mutually exclusive with 'behavior' and 'graph'.

graph.title: Graph Reference
graph.description: A reference to a nested behavior graph. For an activity node it is the sub-process performed and is mutually exclusive with 'behavior' and 'ref'; for a composite node it is the nested region.

actors.title: Actors
actors.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to objects that perform this node.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to objects affected by this node.

eventType.title: Event Type
eventType.description: For an event node, the nature of the event, reusing the trigger vocabulary, such as signal, scheduled, or event-driven.

gatewayKind.title: Gateway Kind
gatewayKind.description: For a gateway node, the branching semantics applied across its outgoing transitions.

meta:enum.exclusive: Exactly one outgoing transition is taken, the first whose guard holds.
meta:enum.inclusive: Every outgoing transition whose guard holds is taken.
meta:enum.parallel: All outgoing transitions are taken concurrently, forking and later joining execution.
meta:enum.complex: Branch selection follows a complex condition described in the transition guards.

onEntry.title: On Entry
onEntry.description: For a state node, a reference to a behavior instance performed when the state is entered.

onExit.title: On Exit
onExit.description: For a state node, a reference to a behavior instance performed when the state is exited.

doActivity.title: Do Activity
doActivity.description: For a state node, a reference to a behavior instance performed while the subject remains in the state.

transition.title: Transition
transition.description: A directed edge from one node to another. In an activity flow it expresses ordering; in a state machine it expresses a triggered, guarded move between states.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the transition.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the transition.

guard.title: Guard
guard.description: A guard condition; the transition fires only when it evaluates to true.

default.title: Default
default.description: Whether this transition is taken when no other guard from the same source node holds.

effect.title: Effect
effect.description: Reference to a behavior instance performed when the transition fires.

blueprints.title: Blueprints
blueprints.description: A collection of blueprints, each supporting a wide range of analytical activities, from use case documentation and behavioural analysis to comprehensive system-level threat and risk modelling.

blueprint.title: Blueprint
blueprint.description: A versatile, machine-readable model designed to support a wide range of analytical activities, from use case documentation and behavioural analysis to comprehensive system-level threat and risk modelling.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the blueprint, used to identify this model within the BOM and distinguish it from other blueprints.

description.title: Description
description.description: A detailed explanation of the blueprint, including its scope, the system or subsystem it represents, and any relevant context about the modelling objectives or intended audience.

modelTypes.title: Model Types
modelTypes.description: The types of models being represented.

assets.title: Assets
assets.description: The assets (components, services, data, actors) included in the model.

dataStores.title: Data Stores
dataStores.description: Structured description of data stores used in the model.

dataSets.title: Data Sets
dataSets.description: Datasets handled by the system, including placements and sensitivity.

zones.title: Zones
zones.description: Logical, physical, or trust zones that group assets with similar characteristics.

flows.title: Flows
flows.description: Data, control, or process flows between assets.

relationships.title: Relationships
relationships.description: Static, structural relationships between elements of the model, complementing the runtime exchanges captured by flows.

actors.title: Actors
actors.description: Human or system actors involved in the model, distinct from assets.

assumptions.title: Assumptions
assumptions.description: Assumptions made during the modeling process.

useCases.title: Use Cases

ref.title: Use Case Reference
ref.description: A reference to a use case object defined in definitions.

assets.title: Assets
assets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the blueprint assets that are involved in or exercised by this use case.

flows.title: Flows
flows.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the blueprint flows that are triggered or traversed during the execution of this use case.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the requirements that are implemented, validated, or addressed by this use case.

useCases.description: Use cases analyzed as part of the model, linking actors, goals, and steps to the blueprint's assets, flows, and requirements.

requirements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the requirements, declared in definitions, that are derived from or related to this model.

modelType.title: Model Type
modelType.description: The type of model being represented, either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom name and description for types not covered by the enumeration. Threat, risk, and privacy analyses are represented in their own models, which reference the blueprint, rather than as blueprint model types.

oneOf.title: Predefined Model Type
oneOf.description: A predefined model type from the enumeration.

meta:enum.architecture: An architecture model of the system or software, capturing its major components, their responsibilities, and the interfaces and relationships between them. Broader than a single view, it frames the overall design.
meta:enum.behavioral: A behavioural model, capturing how the system acts over time through its processes, state changes, interactions, and control flow, as opposed to its static structure.
meta:enum.conceptual: A conceptual model at the highest level of abstraction, capturing the key entities, concepts, and relationships in technology-neutral terms without implementation detail. The first level of the conceptual, logical, and physical progression.
meta:enum.data-flow: A data flow model showing how data moves between processes, data stores, and external entities. This is the substrate most commonly used for STRIDE per-element threat analysis.
meta:enum.deployment: A deployment model showing how software is allocated to runtime and infrastructure, including nodes, containers, environments, and the artifacts placed on them.
meta:enum.logical: A logical model detailing the structure and behaviour of the system independent of any specific implementation technology. The middle level of the conceptual, logical, and physical progression.
meta:enum.network: A network model showing topology and connectivity, including segments, links, and the paths over which components communicate.
meta:enum.operational: An operational model describing how the system is run and supported in production, including procedures, responsibilities, and operational dependencies.
meta:enum.physical: A physical model at the implementation level, capturing the tangible realization of the system such as hardware, devices, infrastructure, topology, and physical locations. The most concrete level of the conceptual, logical, and physical progression.
meta:enum.process: A process model describing a business or system process as an ordered set of activities and decisions and the actors that carry them out.

oneOf.title: Custom Model Type
oneOf.description: A custom model type not covered by the predefined enumeration, allowing organisations to define domain-specific model categories.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom model type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom model type.

metadata.title: Blueprint Metadata
metadata.description: Metadata about the creation, review, and approval of the blueprint.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: When the blueprint was created or last updated.

authors.title: Authors
authors.description: The parties that created the blueprint. Each entry is either an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` should include `author`.

reviewer.description: The party that reviewed the blueprint. The party's `roles` should include `reviewer`.

approver.description: The party that approved the blueprint. The party's `roles` should include `signatory` or a custom role identifying the approving authority.

validityPeriod.title: Validity Period
validityPeriod.description: The period during which the blueprint is considered valid.

reviewFrequency.title: Review Frequency
reviewFrequency.description: ISO 8601 duration for review frequency (e.g., P3M for quarterly).

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: Specifies what is included and excluded from the blueprint model, establishing the boundaries within which the modelling and analysis is performed.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the scope.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of what is included and excluded from the model.

includedComponents.title: Included Components
includedComponents.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to components explicitly included.

excludedComponents.title: Excluded Components
excludedComponents.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to components explicitly excluded.

asset.title: Asset
asset.description: A component, service, data store, actor, or other element included in the blueprint model. An actor asset references a party, since a party such as a person, persona, system, or organization may itself be an asset, including a person in the intelligence sense of a human source.

bom-ref.description: Unique identifier for the asset.

componentRef.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the component this asset represents.

serviceRef.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the service this asset represents.

partyRef.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party this asset represents, such as a person, persona, system, or organization. Used when the asset is an actor.

type.title: Asset Type
type.description: The kind of asset. Use the custom option for kinds not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.actor: An actor that is an asset in its own right, referencing a party via partyRef. A party such as a system or organization is commonly an asset, and a person may be an asset in the intelligence sense of a human source.
meta:enum.broker: Message broker or event bus infrastructure.
meta:enum.cache: Caching layer or in-memory data store.
meta:enum.data-store: Database, file system, or data repository.
meta:enum.gateway: API gateway, load balancer, or proxy.
meta:enum.tool: A tool or capability invoked by an agent, such as a function, API, or service exposed to an AI agent.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom asset type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom asset type.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the asset.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the asset's role and purpose.

zone.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the zone containing this asset.

responsibilities.title: Responsibilities
responsibilities.description: Responsibilities or functions of this asset.

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to use case objects that describe how actors interact with this asset, including the specific goals, sequences of steps, alternative and exception flows, and any requirements that govern its usage.

oneOf.title: Component Reference Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined by reference to a component declared elsewhere in the BOM.

oneOf.title: Service Reference Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined by reference to a service declared elsewhere in the BOM.

oneOf.title: Party Reference Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined by reference to a party declared elsewhere in the BOM, such as a person, persona, system, or organization that is itself an asset.

oneOf.title: Inline Asset
oneOf.description: An asset defined inline within the blueprint with an explicit type and name.

dataStore.title: Data Store
dataStore.description: A persistent storage system used within the modelled system to hold, retrieve, or manage data.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the data store.

description.title: Description
description.description: Purpose and contents of the store.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of data store. Use the custom option for technologies not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.data-lake: Data lake or lakehouse for raw and semi-structured data.
meta:enum.data-warehouse: Analytical data warehouse optimised for OLAP workloads.
meta:enum.event-log: Event log or append-only streaming store.
meta:enum.hierarchical: Hierarchical or tree-structured database, such as a directory store.
meta:enum.in-memory: In-memory data store used as a primary store.
meta:enum.message-queue: Message queue or broker with persistent storage.
meta:enum.multi-model: A store supporting multiple data models.
meta:enum.search: Search index or full-text search engine.
meta:enum.vector: Vector database for embeddings and similarity search.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom data store type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom data store type.

vendor.title: Vendor
vendor.description: The vendor or publisher of the data store product.

product.title: Product
product.description: The name of the data store product.

version.title: Version
version.description: The version of the data store product.

zone.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the zone where the asset resides.

dataSets.title: Data Sets
dataSets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to data sets stored here.

dataSet.title: Data Set
dataSet.description: A logical collection of related data managed within the modelled system, including its classification, ownership, and storage placements.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the dataset.

description.title: Description
description.description: What information is contained and why it exists.

dataProfiles.title: Data Profiles
dataProfiles.description: Data profiles that govern the dataset. Each entry is either an inline profile object or a reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a previously declared profile, typically declared in the root profiles catalogue.

dataObjects.title: Data Objects
dataObjects.description: The specific data objects stored in the dataset. Each object names a payload and may reference one of the dataset's data profiles for its governing policy.

owners.title: Owners
owners.description: Parties that own the dataset. Each entry is either an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` should include `owner`.

items.title: Placement
items.description: Describes where a dataset physically or logically resides and the storage characteristics at that location.

dataStore.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the store holding this dataset.

encrypted.title: Encrypted
encrypted.description: Whether the dataset is encrypted at this location.

replicated.title: Replicated
replicated.description: Indicates if this placement is a replica.

name.description: Name of the interface.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of interface. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom interface type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom interface type.
Description of the interface.

specification.description: Link or reference to interface specification.

dataFormat.description: Data format (e.g., JSON, XML, Protocol Buffers).

zone.title: Zone
zone.description: A grouping of assets that share a common characteristic, security posture, or administrative control, so they can be reasoned about as a unit. A zone may be logical, physical, network, trust, or another kind, may nest within a parent zone, and is connected to or separated from other zones by boundaries.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the zone.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of the zone's characteristics.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of zone. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.availability: An availability or fault-isolation zone, such as a cloud availability zone, used to bound the blast radius of failures and reason about redundancy and resilience.
meta:enum.compliance: A compliance scope zone whose assets fall under a specific regulatory regime, such as PCI DSS, HIPAA, or FedRAMP, delineating what is in scope and the controls applied.
meta:enum.data: A data classification zone grouping assets by the sensitivity of the data they hold or process, such as public, internal, confidential, or restricted.
meta:enum.deployment: A deployment environment grouping assets by lifecycle stage, such as development, staging, or production.
meta:enum.functional: A functional grouping of assets that serve a common purpose or capability, independent of where or how they are deployed.
meta:enum.geographic: A geographic or data-residency zone bounding assets to a region, country, or jurisdiction, used to reason about sovereignty and cross-border data flows.
meta:enum.logical: A logical grouping of assets defined by architecture or design rather than physical placement.
meta:enum.network: A network segmentation zone, such as a subnet, VLAN, or security group, within which assets share network-level reachability and controls.
meta:enum.organizational: An organizational or administrative zone grouping assets under a common owner, team, or authority.
meta:enum.physical: A physical zone bounding assets to a tangible location, such as a data centre, building, or device enclosure.
meta:enum.process: A process or execution isolation zone, such as a sandbox, container runtime, or trusted execution environment, within which code runs with a defined level of isolation.
meta:enum.tenant: A tenancy isolation zone separating the assets and data of one tenant from another in a multi-tenant system.
meta:enum.trust: A security trust zone whose assets share a common level of trust, so that movement between trust zones is where authentication, authorization, and validation are enforced.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom zone type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom zone type.

parent.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the parent zone if this is a sub-zone.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.description: Constraints or limitations of this zone.

boundary.title: Boundary
boundary.description: An edge that separates or connects two or more zones, marking a point where security controls, policies, or trust levels change. The controls expected when data or actors cross it are captured in crossingRequirements, and the threat model's trust boundary extends a boundary with trust semantics.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the boundary.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of boundary. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.data: A data classification boundary, crossed when data moves between zones of differing sensitivity, where reclassification, masking, or validation may apply.
meta:enum.functional: A functional boundary between areas of differing responsibility or capability.
meta:enum.network: A network boundary, such as a firewall, gateway, or security group, controlling reachability between network zones.
meta:enum.organizational: An organizational or administrative boundary between assets under different ownership or authority.
meta:enum.physical: A physical security boundary, such as a facility perimeter or device enclosure, between physical zones.
meta:enum.process: A process or execution boundary between isolation contexts, such as between a sandbox and its host.
meta:enum.trust: A trust boundary between zones of differing trust, the primary point at which authentication, authorization, and input validation are enforced and a key trigger for threat analysis.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom boundary type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom boundary type.

zones.title: Zones
zones.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the zones this boundary connects or separates.

crossingRequirements.title: Crossing Requirements
crossingRequirements.description: The security controls, policies, and constraints applied when data or actors cross a boundary between zones, such as the authentication and authorization enforced, the protocols permitted, and the validation, logging, and monitoring performed at the crossing.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: Authentication required to cross this boundary.

authorization.title: Authorization
authorization.description: Authorization models enforced at this boundary.

dataValidation.title: Data Validation
dataValidation.description: Whether data is validated when crossing this boundary.

dataTransformation.title: Data Transformation
dataTransformation.description: Whether data is transformed when crossing this boundary.

sessionManagement.title: Session Management
sessionManagement.description: The lifecycle of the authenticated session established at this boundary, covering session timeouts, access and refresh token validity, and logout behaviour. These properties characterize the session that results from authenticating, independently of the authentication method used.

accessTokenExpires.title: Access Token Expires
accessTokenExpires.description: Whether access tokens issued for this boundary expire.

accessTokenTtl.title: Access Token Time To Live
accessTokenTtl.description: Validity period of an access token, in seconds.

refreshToken.title: Refresh Token
refreshToken.description: Whether refresh tokens are issued for this boundary.

refreshTokenTtl.title: Refresh Token Time To Live
refreshTokenTtl.description: Validity period of a refresh token, in seconds.

idleTimeout.title: Idle Timeout
idleTimeout.description: Period of inactivity after which the session ends, in seconds.

absoluteTimeout.title: Absolute Timeout
absoluteTimeout.description: Period after session creation after which the session ends regardless of activity, in seconds.

userLogout.title: User Logout
userLogout.description: Whether a user can explicitly end the session.

systemLogout.title: System Logout
systemLogout.description: Whether the system can end the session without user action.

relationship.title: Relationship
relationship.description: A typed structural relationship from one element to one or more others, complementing the runtime exchanges captured by flows. The subject is `ref`, and the kind of relationship is expressed as a single typed property whose value lists the targets, mirroring the dependency graph for readability.

ref.title: Reference
ref.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the subject element of the relationship.

aggregates.title: Aggregates
aggregates.description: The subject is the whole in a whole-part relationship in which each target part may exist independently of the whole.

associates.title: Associates
associates.description: The subject has a general association with each target.

composes.title: Composes
composes.description: The subject is the whole in a whole-part relationship in which each target part cannot exist without the whole.

contains.title: Contains
contains.description: The subject contains or nests each target.

dependsOn.title: Depends On
dependsOn.description: The subject depends on each target.

generalizes.title: Generalizes
generalizes.description: The subject is a generalisation of each target, as in inheritance.

realizes.title: Realizes
realizes.description: The subject realises or implements each target.

serves.title: Serves
serves.description: The subject provides a service to each target.

custom.title: Custom Relationships
custom.description: Relationships whose kind is not covered by the predefined properties. Each entry names the kind and lists its targets.

type.title: Type
type.description: The name of the custom relationship kind.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: The targets of the custom relationship.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom relationship kind.

relationshipTargets.title: Relationship Targets
relationshipTargets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the target elements of a relationship.

flow.title: Flow
flow.description: Represents a data, control, or process flow between two assets, capturing the direction, type, protocols, and security controls that govern the exchange.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name or description of the flow.

description.title: Description
description.description: Detailed description of what flows and why.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of flow. Use the custom option for types not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.physical: Physical movement of goods or materials.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom flow type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom flow type.

source.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the source of the flow. The source may be either an asset (such as a component, service, data store, or other element in the blueprint) or an actor that originates the exchange.

destination.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the destination of the flow. The destination may be either an asset (such as a component, service, data store, or other element in the blueprint) or an actor that receives the exchange.

bidirectional.title: Bidirectional
bidirectional.description: Whether flow occurs in both directions.

synchronous.title: Synchronous
synchronous.description: Whether the flow is synchronous or asynchronous.

encrypted.title: Encrypted
encrypted.description: Whether the data carried by this flow is encrypted in transit.

volume.title: Volume
volume.description: Expected volume or frequency of flow.

protocols.title: Protocols
protocols.description: Communication protocols used by this flow.

dataProfiles.title: Data Profiles
dataProfiles.description: Data profiles that govern the data carried by this flow. A flow may carry data governed by multiple profiles, for example a payment submission carrying both payment card data and customer personally identifiable information. Each entry is either an inline profile object or a reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a previously declared profile.

dataObjects.title: Data Objects
dataObjects.description: The specific data objects carried by this flow. Each object names a payload and may reference one of the flow's data profiles for its governing policy.

authorization.title: Authorization
authorization.description: Authorization models enforced on this flow.

sequence.title: Sequence
sequence.description: Ordering hint so flows can be assembled into sequence diagrams.

actor.title: Actor
actor.description: An actor is a contextual binding between a party and the modelled system. The party identifies who or what is acting (an organisation, person, system, or persona). The actor layer adds properties that are meaningful only within the blueprint, such as the permissions the actor holds in this system and the trust zone in which the actor operates. Identity, roles, archetype, and inter-party relations such as delegation are carried by the wrapped party and shall not be duplicated on the actor.

bom-ref.description: Unique identifier for this actor binding. References to actors from other parts of the blueprint, such as use cases or flows, use this identifier.

party.title: Party
party.description: The party that this actor binds to the modelled system. May be an inline party object declaring identity and roles in place, or a bom-link or bom-ref to a party declared elsewhere in the document.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative describing this actor's role within the modelled system, distinct from the party's identity-level description. Use to record context-specific framing such as why this actor is in scope or how it interacts with the assets in this blueprint.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: The permissions, capabilities, or duties this actor holds within the modelled system. Distinct from any innate party-level capabilities; these are scoped to the blueprint.

zone.title: Zone
zone.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the trust zone in which the actor operates.

assumption.title: Assumption
assumption.description: A stated belief or condition accepted as true for the purposes of the model, which may impact the validity of the analysis if proven false.

description.title: Description
description.description: A clear statement of the assumption being made, including the believed condition, the reasoning or evidence behind it, and any constraints or scope under which it applies.

topic.title: Topic
topic.description: The topic of the assumption. Use the custom option for topics not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.availability: Assumption about system uptime, redundancy, or failover capabilities.
meta:enum.business: Assumption about business requirements, constraints, or organizational context.
meta:enum.compliance: Assumption about regulatory, legal, or policy compliance obligations.
meta:enum.operational: Assumption about operational procedures, staffing, or maintenance practices.
meta:enum.performance: Assumption about throughput, latency, capacity, or scalability characteristics.
meta:enum.security: Assumption about threat landscape, trust boundaries, or security controls.
meta:enum.technical: Assumption about technology choices, capabilities, or technical constraints.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom assumption topic.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom assumption topic.

relatedAssets.title: Related Assets
relatedAssets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the assets that are directly affected by or dependent on this assumption, enabling impact analysis when the assumption's validity changes.

validity.title: Validity

meta:enum.invalid: The assumption has been disproven or is no longer applicable.
meta:enum.unknown: The validity of the assumption has not yet been assessed.
meta:enum.unverified: The assumption has been identified but not yet validated.
meta:enum.verified: The assumption has been confirmed as true through validation.

validity.description: The current validation state of the assumption, tracked over time to ensure that modelling decisions built on this assumption remain sound.

impact.title: Impact
impact.description: A narrative describing the consequences to the model, its assets, or the broader system if this assumption proves false, including the severity and scope of affected components.

owner.description: The party responsible for tracking, validating, and maintaining this assumption over the lifecycle of the model. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` should include `owner`.

validationMethod.title: Validation Method
validationMethod.description: The approach, test, or evidence that can be used to confirm or disprove this assumption, such as a review, audit, penetration test, or empirical measurement.

validationDate.title: Validation Date
validationDate.description: The date and time when the assumption was last validated or reviewed, used to track staleness and trigger re-evaluation.

visualization.title: Visualization
visualization.description: A visual representation of the model or a subset of its elements, capturing the diagram type, its content, and the elements depicted.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name or title of the visualization.

description.title: Description
description.description: Description of what the visualization shows.

url.title: URL
url.description: URL to the diagram if stored externally.

level.title: Level

meta:enum.high: High-level view showing major components and relationships.
meta:enum.medium: Intermediate view balancing breadth and depth.
meta:enum.overview: Top-level summary providing a broad perspective of the system.

level.description: Level of detail in the visualization.

elements.title: Elements
elements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to elements shown in this visualization.

layers.title: Layers
layers.description: Layers or views available in the visualization.

visualizationType.title: Visualization Type
visualizationType.description: The type of diagram or visual representation.

oneOf.title: Predefined Visualization Type
oneOf.description: A visualization type selected from a predefined set of well-known diagram types.

meta:enum.attack-tree: Attack tree diagram showing alternative paths to a goal.
meta:enum.block: Block diagram, including SysML block definition and internal block diagrams.
meta:enum.communication: Communication diagram, formerly known as the collaboration diagram.
meta:enum.flowchart: Flowchart depicting steps or decision logic.
meta:enum.matrix: Matrix diagram, such as a dependency structure or traceability matrix.
meta:enum.mind-map: Mind map diagram for decomposition or brainstorming.
meta:enum.timing: Timing diagram showing state changes over time.

oneOf.title: Custom Visualization Type
oneOf.description: A custom visualization type not covered by the predefined enumeration.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom visualization type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom visualization type.

ownership.title: Ownership
ownership.description: Parties responsible for the asset. Each entry is either an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` carry the relationship (`owner`, `steward`, `custodian`, `end-user`, or a custom role). Multiple parties may share the same role; preference among them is expressed via `role.order`.

authenticationType.title: Authentication Type
authenticationType.description: The authentication method or protocol used to verify identity, either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom object describing a method not covered by the enumeration.

oneOf.title: Predefined Authentication Type
oneOf.description: A predefined authentication method from the enumeration.

meta:enum.basic: HTTP Basic authentication (RFC 7617), where the browser presents a native credential dialog and the username and password are sent on each request.
meta:enum.biometric: Biometric authentication, such as fingerprint or face recognition.
meta:enum.eap: Extensible Authentication Protocol, commonly used with IEEE 802.1X.
meta:enum.form: Form-based authentication, where credentials such as a username and password are submitted through an application login form rather than the browser-native HTTP Basic dialog.
meta:enum.hmac: HMAC request signing using a shared secret key.
meta:enum.magic-link: Passwordless authentication using a single-use link.
meta:enum.pin: Personal identification number, a short secret used as a knowledge factor, such as a device or application unlock PIN.
meta:enum.push: Out-of-band push approval, where a prompt is sent to a registered device or application for the user to approve or deny, optionally confirmed by matching a displayed number.
meta:enum.session-cookie: Session authentication using a server-side session identified by a cookie.

oneOf.title: Custom Authentication Type
oneOf.description: A custom authentication method not covered by the predefined enumeration, allowing organisations to describe proprietary, emerging, or composite authentication approaches.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom authentication method.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom authentication method, including how identity is verified and any relevant constraints.

authorizationType.title: Authorization Type
authorizationType.description: The authorization model used to determine and enforce access permissions, either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom object describing a model not covered by the enumeration.

oneOf.title: Predefined Authorization Type
oneOf.description: A predefined authorization model from the enumeration.

oneOf.title: Custom Authorization Type
oneOf.description: A custom authorization model not covered by the predefined enumeration, allowing organisations to describe proprietary, domain-specific, or composite access control models.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom authorization model.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom authorization model, including how access decisions are made and any relevant constraints.

businessObjectives.title: Business Objectives
businessObjectives.description: A collection of reusable business objectives that other elements may reference.

businessObjective.title: Business Objective
businessObjective.description: A business goal that a subject supports and that threats, risks, use cases, and requirements can be traced back to. Business objectives anchor risk-centric analysis, such as the first stage of PASTA, where threats and risks are evaluated against what the organization is trying to achieve.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the business objective.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the business objective.

criticality.description: The business criticality of the objective.

owner.description: The party accountable for this objective. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party.

citations.title: Citations
citations.description: A collection of attributions indicating which entity supplied information for specific fields within the BOM.

citation.title: Citation
citation.description: Details a specific attribution of data within the BOM to a contributing entity or process.

items.title: Field Reference
items.description: A [JSON Pointer](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6901) identifying the BOM field to which the attribution applies.

pointers.title: Field References
pointers.description:
One or more [JSON Pointers](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6901) identifying the BOM fields to which the attribution applies.
Exactly one of the "pointers" or "expressions" elements must be present.

items.title: Path Expression
items.description: Specifies a [JSONPath](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9535) expression used to locate a value within a BOM.

expressions.title: Path Expressions
expressions.description:
One or more path expressions used to locate values within a BOM.
Exactly one of the "pointers" or "expressions" elements must be present.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: The date and time when the attribution was made or the information was supplied.

attributedTo.title: Attributed To
attributedTo.description:
The `bom-ref` of an object, such as a component, service, tool, organisational entity, or person that supplied the cited information.
At least one of the "attributedTo" or "process" elements must be present.

process.title: Process Reference
process.description:
The `bom-ref` to a process (such as a formula, workflow, task, or step) defined in the `formulation` section that executed or generated the attributed data.
At least one of the "attributedTo" or "process" elements must be present.

note.title: Note
note.description: A description or comment about the context or quality of the data attribution.

signatures.title: Signature
signatures.description: A digital signature verifying the authenticity or integrity of the attribution.

refType.description:
Identifier for referable and therefore interlinkable elements.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.
refType.$comment: TODO (breaking change): add a format constraint that prevents the value from staring with 'urn:cdx:'

refLinkType.description:
Descriptor for an element identified by the attribute 'bom-ref' in the same BOM document.
In contrast to `bomLinkElementType`.

bomLinkDocumentType.title: BOM-Link Document
bomLinkDocumentType.description: Descriptor for another BOM document. See https://cyclonedx.org/capabilities/bomlink/
bomLinkDocumentType.$comment: part of the pattern is based on `bom.serialNumber`'s pattern

bomLinkElementType.title: BOM-Link Element
bomLinkElementType.description: Descriptor for an element in a BOM document. See https://cyclonedx.org/capabilities/bomlink/
bomLinkElementType.$comment: part of the pattern is based on `bom.serialNumber`'s pattern

alg.title: Hash Algorithm
alg.description: The standard, well-known algorithm used to compute the hash.

content.title: Hash Value
content.description: The value of the hash computed using the standard, well-known algorithm.

hashAlgorithm.title: Hash Algorithm
hashAlgorithm.description: The algorithm that generated the hash value.

mediaType.title: Media Type
mediaType.description: The media type of the object. The media type can provide additional context about the kind of data being represented, such as an image, font, or executable.

attachment.title: Attachment
attachment.description: Specifies the metadata and content for an attachment.

encoding.title: Encoding
encoding.description: Specifies the optional encoding the text is represented in.

meta:enum.base64: Base64 is a binary-to-text encoding scheme that represents binary data in an ASCII string.

content.title: Attachment Text
content.description: The attachment data. Proactive controls such as input validation and sanitization should be employed to prevent misuse of attachment text.

externalReferences.title: External References
externalReferences.description: External references provide a way to document systems, sites, and information that may be relevant but are not included with the BOM. They may also establish specific relationships within or external to the BOM.

externalReference.title: External Reference
externalReference.description: External references provide a way to document systems, sites, and information that may be relevant but are not included with the BOM. They may also establish specific relationships within or external to the BOM.

url.title: URL
url.description: The URI (URL or URN) to the external reference. External references are URIs and therefore can accept any URL scheme including https ([RFC-7230](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc7230.txt)), mailto ([RFC-2368](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2368.txt)), tel ([RFC-3966](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3966.txt)), and dns ([RFC-4501](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4501.txt)). External references may also include formally registered URNs such as [CycloneDX BOM-Link](https://cyclonedx.org/capabilities/bomlink/) to reference CycloneDX BOMs or any object within a BOM. BOM-Link transforms applicable external references into relationships that can be expressed in a BOM or across BOMs.

comment.title: Comment
comment.description: A comment describing the external reference

type.title: Type
type.description: Specifies the type of external reference.

meta:enum.issue-tracker: Issue or defect tracking system, or an Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) system
meta:enum.bom: Bill of Materials (SBOM, OBOM, HBOM, SaaSBOM, etc)
meta:enum.source-distribution: The location where the source code distributable can be obtained. This is often an archive format such as zip or tgz. The source-distribution type complements use of the version control (vcs) type.
meta:enum.distribution-intake: The location where a component was published to. This is often the same as "distribution" but may also include specialized publishing processes that act as an intermediary.
meta:enum.license: The reference to the license file. If a license URL has been defined in the license node, it should also be defined as an external reference for completeness.
meta:enum.build-meta: Build-system specific meta file (i.e. pom.xml, package.json, .nuspec, etc)
meta:enum.build-system: Reference to an automated build system
meta:enum.security-contact: Specifies a way to contact the maintainer, supplier, or provider in the event of a security incident. Common URIs include links to a disclosure procedure, a mailto (RFC-2368) that specifies an email address, a tel (RFC-3966) that specifies a phone number, or dns (RFC-4501) that specifies the records containing DNS Security TXT.
meta:enum.model-card: A model card describes the intended uses of a machine learning model, potential limitations, biases, ethical considerations, training parameters, datasets used to train the model, performance metrics, and other relevant data useful for ML transparency.
meta:enum.log: A record of events that occurred in a computer system or application, such as problems, errors, or information on current operations.
meta:enum.configuration: Parameters or settings that may be used by other components or services.
meta:enum.evidence: Information used to substantiate a claim.
meta:enum.formulation: Describes the formulation of any referencable object within the BOM, including components, services, metadata, declarations, or the BOM itself.
meta:enum.attestation: Human or machine-readable statements containing facts, evidence, or testimony.
meta:enum.threat-model: An enumeration of identified weaknesses, threats, and countermeasures, dataflow diagram (DFD), attack tree, and other supporting documentation in human-readable or machine-readable format.
meta:enum.adversary-model: The defined assumptions, goals, and capabilities of an adversary.
meta:enum.risk-assessment: Identifies and analyzes the potential of future events that may negatively impact individuals, assets, and/or the environment. Risk assessments may also include judgments on the tolerability of each risk.
meta:enum.vulnerability-assertion: A Vulnerability Disclosure Report (VDR) which asserts the known and previously unknown vulnerabilities that affect a component, service, or product including the analysis and findings describing the impact (or lack of impact) that the reported vulnerability has on a component, service, or product.
meta:enum.exploitability-statement: A Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange (VEX) which asserts the known vulnerabilities that do not affect a product, product family, or organization, and optionally the ones that do. The VEX should include the analysis and findings describing the impact (or lack of impact) that the reported vulnerability has on the product, product family, or organization.
meta:enum.pentest-report: Results from an authorized simulated cyberattack on a component or service, otherwise known as a penetration test.
meta:enum.static-analysis-report: SARIF or proprietary machine or human-readable report for which static analysis has identified code quality, security, and other potential issues with the source code.
meta:enum.dynamic-analysis-report: Dynamic analysis report that has identified issues such as vulnerabilities and misconfigurations.
meta:enum.runtime-analysis-report: Report generated by analyzing the call stack of a running application.
meta:enum.component-analysis-report: Report generated by Software Composition Analysis (SCA), container analysis, or other forms of component analysis.
meta:enum.maturity-report: Report containing a formal assessment of an organization, business unit, or team against a maturity model.
meta:enum.certification-report: Industry, regulatory, or other certification from an accredited (if applicable) certification body.
meta:enum.codified-infrastructure: Code or configuration that defines and provisions virtualized infrastructure, commonly referred to as Infrastructure as Code (IaC).
meta:enum.quality-metrics: Report or system in which quality metrics can be obtained.
meta:enum.poam: Plans of Action and Milestones (POA&M) complement an "attestation" external reference. POA&M is defined by NIST as a "document that identifies tasks needing to be accomplished. It details resources required to accomplish the elements of the plan, any milestones in meeting the tasks and scheduled completion dates for the milestones".
meta:enum.perspective: A CycloneDX perspective defines a domain-specific view, enabling different audiences to interpret and navigate the data through their own conceptual lens.
meta:enum.electronic-signature: An e-signature is commonly a scanned representation of a written signature or a stylized script of the person's name.
meta:enum.digital-signature: A signature that leverages cryptography, typically public/private key pairs, which provides strong authenticity verification.
meta:enum.rfc-9116: Document that complies with [RFC 9116](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc9116.html) (A File Format to Aid in Security Vulnerability Disclosure)
meta:enum.patent: References information about patents which may be defined in human-readable documents or in machine-readable formats such as CycloneDX or ST.96. For detailed patent information or to reference the information provided directly by patent offices, it is recommended to leverage standards from the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) such as [ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/en/st96).
meta:enum.patent-family: References information about a patent family which may be defined in human-readable documents or in machine-readable formats such as CycloneDX or ST.96. A patent family is a group of related patent applications or granted patents that cover the same or similar invention. For detailed patent family information or to reference the information provided directly by patent offices, it is recommended to leverage standards from the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) such as [ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/en/st96).
meta:enum.patent-assertion: References assertions made regarding patents associated with a component or service. Assertions distinguish between ownership, licensing, and other relevant interactions with patents.
meta:enum.citation: A reference to external citations applicable to the object identified by this BOM entry or the BOM itself. When used with a BOM-Link, this allows offloading citations into a separate CycloneDX BOM.
meta:enum.swid-tag: A Software Identification (SWID) tag document conforming to ISO/IEC 19770-2. The reference resolves to the XML SoftwareIdentity document itself, including all of its metadata (entities, evidence, payload, links, and meta elements). This is distinct from the `swid` identifier scheme, which carries only the tagId of a SWID tag.
meta:enum.other: Use this if no other types accurately describe the purpose of the external reference.

hashes.title: Hashes
hashes.description: The hashes of the external reference (if applicable).

postalAddress.title: Postal address
postalAddress.description: An address used to identify a contactable location.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An optional identifier which can be used to reference the address elsewhere in the BOM. Every bom-ref must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

country.title: Country
country.description: The country name or the two-letter ISO 3166-1 country code.

region.title: Region
region.description: The region or state in the country.

locality.title: Locality
locality.description: The locality or city within the country.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An optional identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every bom-ref must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

name.title: Organization Name
name.description: The name of the organization

address.title: Organization Address
address.description: The physical address (location) of the organization

url.title: Organization URL(s)
url.description: The URL of the organization. Multiple URLs are allowed.

contact.title: Organizational Contact
contact.description: A contact at the organization. Multiple contacts are allowed.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An optional identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every bom-ref must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of a contact

email.title: Email Address
email.description: The email address of the contact.

phone.title: Phone
phone.description: The phone number of the contact.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Provides the ability to document properties in a name-value store. This provides flexibility to include data not officially supported in the standard without having to use additional namespaces or create extensions. Unlike key-value stores, properties support duplicate names, each potentially having different values. Property names of interest to the general public are encouraged to be registered in the [CycloneDX Property Taxonomy](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-property-taxonomy). Formal registration is optional.

property.title: Lightweight name-value pair
property.description: Provides the ability to document properties in a name-value store. This provides flexibility to include data not officially supported in the standard without having to use additional namespaces or create extensions. Unlike key-value stores, properties support duplicate names, each potentially having different values. Property names of interest to the general public are encouraged to be registered in the [CycloneDX Property Taxonomy](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-property-taxonomy). Formal registration is optional.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the property. Duplicate names are allowed, each potentially having a different value.

^ext:[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}:.+$.description:
CycloneDX supports a structured and namespace-aware mechanism for extensibility through the use of extensible properties. This mechanism enables organizations, ecosystems, and tool vendors to safely introduce custom properties without conflicting with the core schema or other extensions.

Extensible properties are defined as a JSON object whose keys must conform to a strict pattern that resembles a reverse domain name structure, prefixed with ext:. This pattern provides a namespacing convention that aligns with well-established practices in other structured formats (e.g., XML namespaces).

baseObject.description: Base object for all CycloneDX entities. Automatically includes support for extensible properties.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: An RFC 3339-compliant UTC timestamp using Zulu time (i.e., ending with 'Z'). The format must be 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' or include optional fractional seconds, e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.sssZ'. Offsets such as '+00:00' are not allowed.

ordinalVersion.title: Ordinal Version
ordinalVersion.description: An ordinal version is a dot-delimited, purely numeric identifier where each segment represents an ordered level in the hierarchy. The segments have no prescribed meaning beyond numeric ordering, allowing straightforward comparison and sorting.

lifecycle.title: Lifecycle
lifecycle.description: The product lifecycle(s) that this BOM represents.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the lifecycle phase

description.title: Description
description.description: The description of the lifecycle phase

lifecycles.title: Lifecycles
lifecycles.description: Lifecycles communicate the stage(s) in which data in the BOM was captured. Different types of data may be available at various phases of a lifecycle, such as the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC), IT Asset Management (ITAM), and Software Asset Management (SAM). Thus, a BOM may include data specific to or only obtainable in a given lifecycle.

phase.title: Phase
phase.description: A pre-defined phase in the product lifecycle.

meta:enum.design: BOM produced early in the development lifecycle containing an inventory of components and services that are proposed or planned to be used. The inventory may need to be procured, retrieved, or resourced prior to use.
meta:enum.pre-build: BOM consisting of information obtained prior to a build process and may contain source files and development artifacts and manifests. The inventory may need to be resolved and retrieved prior to use.
meta:enum.build: BOM consisting of information obtained during a build process where component inventory is available for use. The precise versions of resolved components are usually available at this time as well as the provenance of where the components were retrieved from.
meta:enum.post-build: BOM consisting of information obtained after a build process has completed and the resulting components(s) are available for further analysis. Built components may exist as the result of a CI/CD process, may have been installed or deployed to a system or device, and may need to be retrieved or extracted from the system or device.
meta:enum.operations: BOM produced that represents inventory that is running and operational. This may include staging or production environments and will generally encompass multiple SBOMs describing the applications and operating system, along with HBOMs describing the hardware that makes up the system. Operations Bill of Materials (OBOM) can provide full-stack inventory of runtime environments, configurations, and additional dependencies.
meta:enum.discovery: BOM consisting of information observed through network discovery providing point-in-time enumeration of embedded, on-premise, and cloud-native services such as server applications, connected devices, microservices, and serverless functions.
meta:enum.decommission: BOM containing inventory that will be, or has been retired from operations.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Textual strings that aid in discovery, search, and retrieval of the associated object. Tags often serve as a way to group or categorize similar or related objects by various attributes.

uid.title: UID
uid.description: A unique identifier of the commit. This may be version control specific. For example, Subversion uses revision numbers whereas git uses commit hashes.

url.title: URL
url.description: The URL to the commit. This URL will typically point to a commit in a version control system.

author.title: Author
author.description: The author who created the changes in the commit

committer.title: Committer
