committer.description: The person who committed or pushed the commit

message.title: Message
message.description: The text description of the contents of the commit

meta:enum.unofficial: A patch which is not developed by the creators or maintainers of the software being patched. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unofficial_patch](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unofficial_patch).
meta:enum.monkey: A patch which dynamically modifies runtime behavior. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monkey_patch](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monkey_patch).
meta:enum.backport: A patch which takes code from a newer version of the software and applies it to older versions of the same software. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Backporting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Backporting).
meta:enum.cherry-pick: A patch created by selectively applying commits from other versions or branches of the same software.

type.title: Patch Type
type.description: Specifies the purpose for the patch including the resolution of defects, security issues, or new behavior or functionality.

diff.title: Diff
diff.description: The patch file (or diff) that shows changes. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diff](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diff)

resolves.title: Resolves
resolves.description: A collection of issues the patch resolves

diff.title: Diff
diff.description: The patch file (or diff) that shows changes. Refer to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diff

text.title: Diff text
text.description: Specifies the optional text of the diff

url.title: URL
url.description: Specifies the URL to the diff

issue.title: Issue
issue.description: An individual issue that has been resolved.

meta:enum.defect: A fault, flaw, or bug in software.
meta:enum.enhancement: A new feature or behavior in software.
meta:enum.security: A special type of defect which impacts security.

type.title: Issue Type
type.description: Specifies the type of issue

id.title: Issue ID
id.description: The identifier of the issue assigned by the source of the issue

name.title: Issue Name
name.description: The name of the issue

description.title: Issue Description
description.description: A description of the issue

source.title: Source
source.description: The source of the issue where it is documented

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the source.

url.title: URL
url.description: The url of the issue documentation as provided by the source

references.title: References
references.description: A collection of URL's for reference. Multiple URLs are allowed.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: The timestamp in which the action occurred

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the individual who performed the action

email.title: E-mail
email.description: The email address of the individual who performed the action

locale.title: Locale
locale.description: Defines a syntax for representing two character language code (ISO-639) followed by an optional two character country code. The language code must be lower case. If the country code is specified, the country code must be upper case. The language code and country code must be separated by a minus sign. Examples: en, en-US, fr, fr-CA

meta:enum.application: A software application. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Application_software](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Application_software) for information about applications.
meta:enum.framework: A software framework. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_framework](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_framework) for information on how frameworks vary slightly from libraries.
meta:enum.library: A software library. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Library_(computing)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Library_(computing)) for information about libraries. All third-party and open source reusable components will likely be a library. If the library also has key features of a framework, then it should be classified as a framework. If not, or is unknown, then specifying library is recommended.
meta:enum.container: A packaging and/or runtime format, not specific to any particular technology, which isolates software inside the container from software outside of a container through virtualization technology. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OS-level_virtualization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OS-level_virtualization).
meta:enum.platform: A runtime environment that interprets or executes software. This may include runtimes such as those that execute bytecode, just-in-time compilers, interpreters, or low-code/no-code application platforms.
meta:enum.operating-system: A software operating system without regard to deployment model (i.e. installed on physical hardware, virtual machine, image, etc) Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operating_system](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operating_system).
meta:enum.device: A hardware device such as a processor or chip-set. A hardware device containing firmware SHOULD include a component for the physical hardware itself and another component of type 'firmware' or 'operating-system' (whichever is relevant), describing information about the software running on the device. See also the list of [known device properties](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-property-taxonomy/blob/main/cdx/device.md).
meta:enum.device-driver: A special type of software that operates or controls a particular type of device. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Device_driver](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Device_driver).
meta:enum.firmware: A special type of software that provides low-level control over a device's hardware. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firmware](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firmware).
meta:enum.file: A computer file. Refer to [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_file](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer_file) for information about files.
meta:enum.machine-learning-model: A model based on training data that can make predictions or decisions without being explicitly programmed to do so.
meta:enum.data: A collection of discrete values that convey information.
meta:enum.cryptographic-asset: A cryptographic asset including algorithms, protocols, certificates, keys, tokens, and secrets.

type.title: Component Type
type.description: Specifies the type of component. For software components, classify as application if no more specific appropriate classification is available or cannot be determined for the component.

mime-type.title: Mime-Type
mime-type.description: The mime-type of the component. When used on file components, the mime-type can provide additional context about the kind of file being represented, such as an image, font, or executable. Some library or framework components may also have an associated mime-type.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the component elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

group.title: Component Group
group.description: The grouping name or identifier. This will often be a shortened, single name of the company or project that produced the component, or the source package or domain name. Whitespace and special characters should be avoided. Examples include: apache, org.apache.commons, and apache.org.

name.title: Component Name
name.description: The name of the component. This will often be a shortened, single name of the component. Examples: commons-lang3 and jquery

version.title: Component Version
version.description:
The component version. The version should ideally comply with semantic versioning but is not enforced.
Must be used exclusively, either 'version' or 'versionRange', but not both.

versionRange.title: Component Version Range
versionRange.description:
For an external component, this specifies the accepted version range.
The value must adhere to the Package URL Version Range syntax (vers), as defined at <https://github.com/package-url/vers-spec
May only be used if `.isExternal` is set to `true`.
Must be used exclusively, either 'version' or 'versionRange', but not both.

isExternal.title: Component Is External
isExternal.description:
Determine whether this component is external.
An external component is one that is not part of an assembly, but is expected to be provided by the environment, regardless of the component's `.scope`. This setting can be useful for distinguishing which components are bundled with the product and which can be relied upon to be present in the deployment environment.
This may be set to `true` for runtime components only. For `$.metadata.component`, it must be set to `false`.

description.title: Component Description
description.description: Specifies a description for the component

meta:enum.required: The component is required for runtime
meta:enum.optional: The component is optional at runtime. Optional components are components that are not capable of being called due to them not being installed or otherwise accessible by any means. Components that are installed but due to configuration or other restrictions are prohibited from being called must be scoped as 'required'.
meta:enum.excluded: Components that are excluded provide the ability to document component usage for test and other non-runtime purposes. Excluded components are not reachable within a call graph at runtime.

scope.title: Component Scope
scope.description: Specifies the scope of the component. If scope is not specified, 'required' scope SHOULD be assumed by the consumer of the BOM.

pedigree.title: Component Pedigree
pedigree.description: Component pedigree is a way to document complex supply chain scenarios where components are created, distributed, modified, redistributed, combined with other components, etc. Pedigree supports viewing this complex chain from the beginning, the end, or anywhere in the middle. It also provides a way to document variants where the exact relation may not be known.

ancestors.title: Ancestors
ancestors.description: Describes zero or more components in which a component is derived from. This is commonly used to describe forks from existing projects where the forked version contains an ancestor node containing the original component it was forked from. For example, Component A is the original component. Component B is the component being used and documented in the BOM. However, Component B contains a pedigree node with a single ancestor documenting Component A - the original component from which Component B is derived from.

descendants.title: Descendants
descendants.description: Descendants are the exact opposite of ancestors. This provides a way to document all forks (and their forks) of an original or root component.

variants.title: Variants
variants.description: Variants describe relations where the relationship between the components is not known. For example, if Component A contains nearly identical code to Component B. They are both related, but it is unclear if one is derived from the other, or if they share a common ancestor.

commits.title: Commits
commits.description: A list of zero or more commits which provide a trail describing how the component deviates from an ancestor, descendant, or variant.

patches.title: Patches
patches.description: >A list of zero or more patches describing how the component deviates from an ancestor, descendant, or variant. Patches may be complementary to commits or may be used in place of commits.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Notes, observations, and other non-structured commentary describing the components pedigree.

components.title: Components
components.description: A list of software and hardware components included in the parent component. Entries may be concrete components or component-choice wrappers expressing conditional or alternate relationships. This is not a dependency tree. It provides a way to specify a hierarchical representation of component assemblies, similar to system → subsystem → parts assembly in physical supply chains.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.description: Provides the ability to document evidence collected through various forms of extraction or analysis.

data.title: Data
data.description: This object SHOULD be specified for any component of type `data` and must not be specified for other component types.

allOf.description: Requirement: ensure that `version` and `versionRange` are not present simultaneously.
Requirement: 'versionRange' must not be present when 'isExternal' is `false`.

componentOrChoice.title: Component or Component Choice
componentOrChoice.description: An entry in a components array. May be a concrete component, or a component-choice wrapper that expresses a conditional or alternate relationship between two or more components. The discriminator is the `type` property: when `type` is `component-choice`, the entry shall conform to the `componentChoice` schema; otherwise it shall conform to the `component` schema.

componentChoice.title: Component Choice
componentChoice.description: A wrapper that expresses a conditional or alternate relationship between two or more components. Use to model approved alternates, multi-sourced parts, build variants, and required component groups in physical device bills of materials.

type.title: Type
type.description: Discriminator for a component-choice entry. The value shall be the literal string `component-choice`.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the component-choice elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

name.title: Name
name.description: A display name for the choice. Typically corresponds to a reference designator, a design intent name, or a description of the role the chosen component fulfils.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the choice and the rationale behind the alternates.

meta:enum.OR: Any non-empty subset of the contained components may be installed. Use for approved alternates where more than one may legitimately appear in a given instance.
meta:enum.XOR: Exactly one of the contained components is installed in any single instance. Use for approved alternates that are mutually exclusive per instance, while the population of units may include multiple alternates across sourcing.
meta:enum.AND: All of the contained components are installed together as a group. Use for tightly coupled component sets such as a transceiver and its required matching balun.

operator.title: Operator
operator.description: The logical relationship between the contained components.

components.title: Components
components.description: Two or more alternate or grouped entries. Each entry may itself be a component-choice to express nested alternates, such as a choice between kits.

version.description: A single disjunctive version identifier, for a component or service.

versionRange.description: A version range specified in Package-URL Version Range syntax (vers) which is defined at https://github.com/package-url/vers-spec

copyrightText.title: Component Copyright
copyrightText.description: A copyright notice informing users of the underlying claims to copyright ownership in a published work.

copyright.title: Copyright
copyright.description: Captures intellectual property assertions, providing evidence of possible ownership and legal protection.

copyrightObject.title: Copyright
copyrightObject.description: A copyright notice informing users of the underlying claims to copyright ownership in a published work.

text.title: Copyright Text
text.description: The textual content of the copyright.

componentEvidence.title: Evidence
componentEvidence.description: Provides the ability to document evidence collected through various forms of extraction or analysis.

identity.title: Identity Evidence
identity.description: Evidence that substantiates the identity of a component. The identity may be an object or an array of identity objects. Support for specifying identity as a single object was introduced in CycloneDX v1.5. Arrays were introduced in v1.6. It is recommended that all implementations use arrays, even if only one identity object is specified.

occurrences.title: Occurrences
occurrences.description: Evidence of individual instances of a component spread across multiple locations.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the occurrence elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

location.title: Location
location.description: The location or path to where the component was found.

line.title: Line Number
line.description: The line number where the component was found.

offset.title: Offset
offset.description: The offset where the component was found.

symbol.title: Symbol
symbol.description: The symbol name that was found associated with the component.

additionalContext.title: Additional Context
additionalContext.description: Any additional context of the detected component (e.g. a code snippet).

accountInfo.title: Account Information
accountInfo.description: The account or user information associated with the occurrence.

systemOwner.title: System Owner
systemOwner.description: The owner of the system where the component was found.

startTime.title: Start Time
startTime.description: The date and time when the process detecting the occurrence started.

endTime.title: End Time
endTime.description: The date and time when the process detecting the occurrence ended.

usageCount.title: Usage Count
usageCount.description: The number of times the component occurred in the detecting process.

callstack.title: Call Stack
callstack.description: Evidence of the components use through the callstack.

frames.title: Frames
frames.description: Within a call stack, a frame is a discrete unit that encapsulates an execution context, including local variables, parameters, and the return address. As function calls are made, frames are pushed onto the stack, forming an array-like structure that orchestrates the flow of program execution and manages the sequence of function invocations.

package.title: Package
package.description: A package organizes modules into namespaces, providing a unique namespace for each type it contains.

module.title: Module
module.description: A module or class that encloses functions/methods and other code.

function.title: Function
function.description: A block of code designed to perform a particular task.

parameters.title: Parameters
parameters.description: Arguments that are passed to the module or function.

line.title: Line
line.description: The line number the code that is called resides on.

column.title: Column
column.description: The column the code that is called resides.

fullFilename.title: Full Filename
fullFilename.description: The full path and filename of the module.

componentIdentityEvidence.title: Identity Evidence
componentIdentityEvidence.description: Evidence that substantiates the identity of a component.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: The overall confidence of the evidence from 0 - 1, where 1 is 100% confidence.

concludedValue.title: Concluded Value
concludedValue.description: The value of the scheme that has been concluded based on the aggregate of all methods (if available).

methods.title: Methods
methods.description: The methods used to extract and/or analyze the evidence.

technique.title: Technique
technique.description: The technique used in this method of analysis.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: The confidence of the evidence from 0 - 1, where 1 is 100% confidence. Confidence is specific to the technique used. Each technique of analysis can have independent confidence.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value or contents of the evidence.

tools.title: BOM References
tools.description: The object in the BOM identified by its bom-ref. This is often a component or service but may be any object type supporting bom-refs. Tools used for analysis should already be defined in the BOM, either in the metadata/tools, components, or formulation.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the dataset elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

type.title: Type of Data
type.description: The general theme or subject matter of the data being specified.

meta:enum.source-code: Any type of code, code snippet, or data-as-code.
meta:enum.configuration: Parameters or settings that may be used by other components.
meta:enum.definition: Data that can be used to create new instances of what the definition defines.
meta:enum.other: Any other type of data that does not fit into existing definitions.

name.title: Dataset Name
name.description: The name of the dataset.

contents.title: Data Contents
contents.description: The contents or references to the contents of the data being described.

attachment.title: Data Attachment
attachment.description: A way to include textual or encoded data.

url.title: Data URL
url.description: The URL to where the data can be retrieved.

properties.title: Configuration Properties
properties.description: Provides the ability to document name-value parameters used for configuration.

sensitiveData.title: Sensitive Data
sensitiveData.description: A description of any sensitive data in a dataset.

description.title: Dataset Description
description.description: A description of the dataset. Can describe size of dataset, whether it's used for source code, training, testing, or validation, etc.

identifiers.title: Identifiers
identifiers.description: Identifiers asserted by one or more parties to identify this component. Each entry groups one or more identity claims by the party asserting them. Identifiers carry positive claims of identity. For unverified or inferred identity data, use evidence.

identifier.title: Identifier
identifier.description: A set of identifiers attributed to a single asserting party.

party.title: Asserting Party
party.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party making the identity assertion.

identities.title: Identities
identities.description: The discrete identity claims asserted by the party.

identity.title: Identity
identity.description: A single identity claim, pairing a typed identifier scheme with the value asserted under that scheme.

identityScheme.title: Identifier Scheme
identityScheme.description: The scheme under which an identifier is asserted. Either a predefined value or a custom scheme described by name and description.

meta:enum.purl: Package-URL identifier, conforming to the Package-URL specification.
meta:enum.cpe: Common Platform Enumeration name, conforming to NIST Interagency Report 7695.
meta:enum.swid: Software Identification tag identifier, conforming to ISO/IEC 19770-2.
meta:enum.omniborid: OmniBOR Artifact Identifier, also known as a gitoid.
meta:enum.epc-rfid: Electronic Product Code - RFID (EPC Tag Data Standard)
meta:enum.gtin-14: Global Trade Identification Number (GTIN / EAN/UCC-14 or ITF-14)
meta:enum.mpn: Manufacturer Part Number, assigned by the original manufacturer.
meta:enum.part-number: Part number assigned by a distributor, integrator, or operator.
meta:enum.model-number: Product model number assigned by the manufacturer.
meta:enum.sku: Stock Keeping Unit, assigned by a seller or distributor.
meta:enum.serial-number: Unique identifier for an individual instance of a product.
meta:enum.asset-tag: Asset tag assigned by the owning or operating organization.
meta:enum.udi-di: Unique Device Identifier, Device Identifier portion, conforming to ISO/IEC 15459 and applicable regulatory frameworks.
meta:enum.udi-pi: Unique Device Identifier, Production Identifier portion, conforming to ISO/IEC 15459 and applicable regulatory frameworks.
meta:enum.fcc-id: United States Federal Communications Commission equipment identifier.
meta:enum.imei: International Mobile Equipment Identity, conforming to 3GPP TS 23.003.
meta:enum.tei: Transparency Exchange Identifier conforming to the Transparency Exchange API specification.

oneOf.title: Custom Identifier Scheme
oneOf.description: A custom identifier scheme not represented in the predefined taxonomy.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom identifier scheme.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom identifier scheme.

compositions.title: Compositions
compositions.description: Compositions describe constituent parts (including components, services, and dependency relationships) and their completeness. The completeness of vulnerabilities expressed in a BOM may also be described.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the composition elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

aggregate.title: Aggregate
aggregate.description: Specifies an aggregate type that describes how complete a relationship is.

assemblies.title: BOM references
assemblies.description: The bom-ref identifiers of the components or services being described. Assemblies refer to nested relationships whereby a constituent part may include other constituent parts. References do not cascade to child parts. References are explicit for the specified constituent part only.

dependencies.title: BOM references
dependencies.description: The bom-ref identifiers of the components or services being described. Dependencies refer to a relationship whereby an independent constituent part requires another independent constituent part. References do not cascade to transitive dependencies. References are explicit for the specified dependency only.

vulnerabilities.title: BOM references
vulnerabilities.description: The bom-ref identifiers of the vulnerabilities being described.

meta:enum.complete: The relationship is complete. No further relationships including constituent components, services, or dependencies are known to exist.
meta:enum.incomplete: The relationship is incomplete. Additional relationships exist and may include constituent components, services, or dependencies.
meta:enum.incomplete_first_party_only: The relationship is incomplete. Only relationships for first-party components, services, or their dependencies are represented.
meta:enum.incomplete_first_party_proprietary_only: The relationship is incomplete. Only relationships for first-party components, services, or their dependencies are represented, limited specifically to those that are proprietary.
meta:enum.incomplete_first_party_opensource_only: The relationship is incomplete. Only relationships for first-party components, services, or their dependencies are represented, limited specifically to those that are opensource.
meta:enum.incomplete_third_party_only: The relationship is incomplete. Only relationships for third-party components, services, or their dependencies are represented.
meta:enum.incomplete_third_party_proprietary_only: The relationship is incomplete. Only relationships for third-party components, services, or their dependencies are represented, limited specifically to those that are proprietary.
meta:enum.incomplete_third_party_opensource_only: The relationship is incomplete. Only relationships for third-party components, services, or their dependencies are represented, limited specifically to those that are opensource.
meta:enum.unknown: The relationship may be complete or incomplete. This usually signifies a 'best-effort' to obtain constituent components, services, or dependencies but the completeness is inconclusive.
meta:enum.not_specified: The relationship completeness is not specified.

controls.title: Controls
controls.description: The safeguards and countermeasures that are recommended or in place. Controls may be declared on their own for governance, risk, and compliance use cases, or referenced from threats, trust boundaries, and risk responses.

control.title: Control
control.description: A safeguard or countermeasure that protects systems, data, or operations. A control binds the elements that implement it to the requirements it satisfies, and records its implementation status and assessed effectiveness.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the control elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the control.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the control.

category.title: Category
category.description: The category of the control. Use the custom option for a category specific to an organization's taxonomy.

meta:enum.preventive: Prevents an unwanted event from occurring.
meta:enum.detective: Identifies and records that an unwanted event has occurred or is occurring.
meta:enum.corrective: Remedies the condition that allowed an unwanted event.
meta:enum.compensating: Provides an alternative safeguard where a primary control is not feasible.
meta:enum.deterrent: Discourages an actor from attempting an unwanted action.
meta:enum.recovery: Restores operations after an unwanted event.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom category.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom category.

status.title: Status
status.description: The implementation status of the control.

appliesTo.title: Applies To
appliesTo.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the elements the control protects, such as components, services, zones, or boundaries. A control without this property applies to the organization or system as a whole.

implementedBy.title: Implemented By
implementedBy.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the components, services, formulation workflows, or parties that implement the control.

satisfies.title: Satisfies
satisfies.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the requirements the control satisfies, including requirements defined within standards.

effectiveness.title: Effectiveness
effectiveness.description: The assessed effectiveness of the control.

owner.description: The party accountable for the control. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party.

implementationStatus.title: Implementation Status
implementationStatus.description: The implementation status of a control or response. Use the custom option for a status specific to an organization's process.

meta:enum.recommended: Suggested by a producer, standard, or assessor. Not yet entered into the adopting organization's decision process.
meta:enum.proposed: Entered into the adopting organization's decision process but not yet approved.
meta:enum.rejected: Considered and declined, with no intent to implement.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom status.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom status.

effectiveness.title: Effectiveness
effectiveness.description: The measured or assessed effectiveness of a control or response.

percentage.title: Percentage
percentage.description: Effectiveness as a decimal from 0 to 1.

cryptoProperties.title: Cryptographic Properties
cryptoProperties.description: Cryptographic assets have properties that uniquely define them and that make them actionable for further reasoning. As an example, it makes a difference if one knows the algorithm family (e.g. AES) or the specific variant or instantiation (e.g. AES-128-GCM). This is because the security level and the algorithm primitive (authenticated encryption) are only defined by the definition of the algorithm variant. The presence of a weak cryptographic algorithm like SHA1 vs. HMAC-SHA1 also makes a difference.

assetType.title: Asset Type
assetType.description: Cryptographic assets occur in several forms. Algorithms and protocols are most commonly implemented in specialized cryptographic libraries. They may, however, also be 'hardcoded' in software components. Certificates and related cryptographic material like keys, tokens, secrets or passwords are other cryptographic assets to be modelled.

meta:enum.algorithm: Mathematical function commonly used for data encryption, authentication, and digital signatures.
meta:enum.certificate: An electronic document that is used to provide the identity or validate a public key.
meta:enum.protocol: A set of rules and guidelines that govern the behavior and communication with each other.
meta:enum.related-crypto-material: Other cryptographic assets related to algorithms, certificates, and protocols such as keys and tokens.

algorithmProperties.title: Algorithm Properties
algorithmProperties.description: Additional properties specific to a cryptographic algorithm.

primitive.title: primitive
primitive.description: Cryptographic building blocks used in higher-level cryptographic systems and protocols. Primitives represent different cryptographic routines: deterministic random bit generators (drbg, e.g. CTR_DRBG from NIST SP800-90A-r1), message authentication codes (mac, e.g. HMAC-SHA-256), blockciphers (e.g. AES), streamciphers (e.g. Salsa20), signatures (e.g. ECDSA), hash functions (e.g. SHA-256), public-key encryption schemes (pke, e.g. RSA), extended output functions (xof, e.g. SHAKE256), key derivation functions (e.g. pbkdf2), key agreement algorithms (e.g. ECDH), key encapsulation mechanisms (e.g. ML-KEM), authenticated encryption (ae, e.g. AES-GCM) and the combination of multiple algorithms (combiner, e.g. SP800-56Cr2).

meta:enum.drbg: Deterministic Random Bit Generator (DRBG) is a type of pseudorandom number generator designed to produce a sequence of bits from an initial seed value. DRBGs are commonly used in cryptographic applications where reproducibility of random values is important.
meta:enum.mac: In cryptography, a Message Authentication Code (MAC) is information used for authenticating and integrity-checking a message.
meta:enum.block-cipher: A block cipher is a symmetric key algorithm that operates on fixed-size blocks of data. It encrypts or decrypts the data in block units, providing confidentiality. Block ciphers are widely used in various cryptographic modes and protocols for secure data transmission.
meta:enum.stream-cipher: A stream cipher is a symmetric key cipher where plaintext digits are combined with a pseudorandom cipher digit stream (keystream).
meta:enum.signature: In cryptography, a signature is a digital representation of a message or data that proves its origin, identity, and integrity. Digital signatures are generated using cryptographic algorithms and are widely used for authentication and verification in secure communication.
meta:enum.hash: A hash function is a mathematical algorithm that takes an input (or 'message') and produces a fixed-size string of characters, which is typically a hash value. Hash functions are commonly used in various cryptographic applications, including data integrity verification and password hashing.
meta:enum.pke: Public Key Encryption (PKE) is a type of encryption that uses a pair of public and private keys for secure communication. The public key is used for encryption, while the private key is used for decryption. PKE is a fundamental component of public-key cryptography.
meta:enum.xof: An XOF is an extendable output function that can take arbitrary input and creates a stream of output, up to a limit determined by the size of the internal state of the hash function that underlies the XOF.
meta:enum.kdf: A Key Derivation Function (KDF) derives key material from another source of entropy while preserving the entropy of the input.
meta:enum.key-agree: In cryptography, a key-agreement is a protocol whereby two or more parties agree on a cryptographic key in such a way that both influence the outcome.
meta:enum.kem: A Key Encapsulation Mechanism (KEM) algorithm is a mechanism for transporting random keying material to a recipient using the recipient's public key.
meta:enum.ae: Authenticated Encryption (AE) is a cryptographic process that provides both confidentiality and data integrity. It ensures that the encrypted data has not been tampered with and comes from a legitimate source. AE is commonly used in secure communication protocols.
meta:enum.combiner: A combiner aggregates many candidates for a cryptographic primitive and generates a new candidate for the same primitive.
meta:enum.key-wrap: Key-wrap is a cryptographic technique used to securely encrypt and protect cryptographic keys using algorithms like AES.

algorithmFamily.title: Algorithm Family
algorithmFamily.description: A valid algorithm family identifier. If specified, this value shall be one of the enumeration of valid algorithm Family identifiers defined in the `cryptography-defs.schema.json` subschema.

parameterSetIdentifier.title: Parameter Set Identifier
parameterSetIdentifier.description: An identifier for the parameter set of the cryptographic algorithm. Examples: in AES128, '128' identifies the key length in bits, in SHA256, '256' identifies the digest length, '128' in SHAKE128 identifies its maximum security level in bits, and 'SHA2-128s' identifies a parameter set used in SLH-DSA (FIPS205).

ellipticCurve.title: Elliptic Curve
ellipticCurve.description: The specific underlying Elliptic Curve (EC) definition employed which is an indicator of the level of security strength, performance and complexity. If specified, this value shall be one of the enumeration of valid elliptic curves identifiers defined in the `cryptography-defs.schema.json` subschema.

executionEnvironment.title: Execution Environment
executionEnvironment.description: The target and execution environment in which the algorithm is implemented in.

meta:enum.software-plain-ram: A software implementation running in plain unencrypted RAM.
meta:enum.software-encrypted-ram: A software implementation running in encrypted RAM.
meta:enum.software-tee: A software implementation running in a trusted execution environment.
meta:enum.unknown: The execution environment is not known.

implementationPlatform.title: Implementation platforms
implementationPlatform.description: The target platforms for which the algorithm is implemented. The implementation can be 'generic', running on any platform or for a specific platform.

items.title: Platform
items.description: The target platform for the implementation.

certificationLevel.title: Certification Level
certificationLevel.description: The certification that the implementation of the cryptographic algorithm has received, if any. Certifications include revisions and levels of FIPS 140 or Common Criteria of different Extended Assurance Levels (CC-EAL).

meta:enum.unknown: The certification level is not known

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: The mode of operation in which the cryptographic algorithm (block cipher) is used.

padding.title: Padding
padding.description: The padding scheme that is used for the cryptographic algorithm.

meta:enum.oaep: Optimal Asymmetric Encryption Padding for RSA.
meta:enum.pss: Probabilistic Signature Scheme for RSA signatures.
meta:enum.unknown: The padding scheme is not known.

cryptoFunctions.title: Cryptographic functions
cryptoFunctions.description: The cryptographic functions implemented by the cryptographic algorithm.

classicalSecurityLevel.title: classical security level
classicalSecurityLevel.description: The classical security level that a cryptographic algorithm provides (in bits).

nistQuantumSecurityLevel.title: NIST security strength category
nistQuantumSecurityLevel.description: The NIST security strength category as defined in https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/post-quantum-cryptography/post-quantum-cryptography-standardization/evaluation-criteria/security-(evaluation-criteria). A value of 0 indicates that none of the categories are met.

secProperties.title: Security Properties
secProperties.description: Formal guarantees about an algorithm's resistance to specific adversarial capabilities under a defined threat model. Example: Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) may target IND-CPA or IND-CCA security; choosing IND-CCA impacts safe use in settings with active/chosen-ciphertext attacks.

certificateProperties.title: Certificate Properties
certificateProperties.description: Properties for cryptographic assets of asset type 'certificate'.

serialNumber.title: Serial Number
serialNumber.description: The serial number is a unique identifier for the certificate issued by a CA.

subjectName.title: Subject Name
subjectName.description: The subject name for the certificate.

issuerName.title: Issuer Name
issuerName.description: The issuer name for the certificate.

notValidBefore.title: Not Valid Before
notValidBefore.description: The date and time according to ISO-8601 standard from which the certificate is valid.

notValidAfter.title: Not Valid After
notValidAfter.description: The date and time according to ISO-8601 standard from which the certificate is not valid anymore.

certificateFileExtension.title: Certificate File Extension
certificateFileExtension.description: The file extension of the certificate.

fingerprint.title: Certificate Fingerprint
fingerprint.description: The fingerprint is a cryptographic hash of the certificate excluding it's signature.

certificateState.title: Certificate Lifecycle State
certificateState.description: The certificate lifecycle is a comprehensive process that manages digital certificates from their initial creation to eventual expiration or revocation. It typically involves several stages.

state.title: State
state.description: A pre-defined state in the certificate lifecycle.

meta:enum.pre-activation: The certificate has been issued by the issuing certificate authority (CA) but has not been authorized for use.
meta:enum.active: The certificate may be used to cryptographically protect information, cryptographically process previously protected information, or both.
meta:enum.deactivated: Certificates in the deactivated state shall not be used to apply cryptographic protection but, in some cases, may be used to process cryptographically protected information.
meta:enum.suspended: The use of a certificate may be suspended for several possible reasons.
meta:enum.revoked: A revoked certificate is a digital certificate that has been invalidated by the issuing certificate authority (CA) before its scheduled expiration date.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: A reason for the certificate being in this state.

name.title: State
name.description: The name of the certificate lifecycle state.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description of the certificate lifecycle state.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: A reason for the certificate being in this state.

creationDate.title: Creation Date
creationDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the certificate was created or pre-activated.

activationDate.title: Activation Date
activationDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the certificate was activated.

deactivationDate.title: Deactivation Date
deactivationDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the related certificate was deactivated.

revocationDate.title: Revocation Date
revocationDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the certificate was revoked.

destructionDate.title: Destruction Date
destructionDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the certificate was destroyed.

certificateExtensions.title: Certificate Extensions
certificateExtensions.description: A certificate extension is a field that provides additional information about the certificate or its use. Extensions are used to convey additional information beyond the standard fields.

meta:enum.basicConstraints: Specifies whether a certificate can be used as a CA certificate or not.
meta:enum.keyUsage: Specifies the allowed uses of the public key in the certificate.
meta:enum.extendedKeyUsage: Specifies additional purposes for which the public key can be used.
meta:enum.subjectAlternativeName: Allows inclusion of additional names to identify the entity associated with the certificate.
meta:enum.authorityKeyIdentifier: Identifies the public key of the CA that issued the certificate.
meta:enum.subjectKeyIdentifier: Identifies the public key associated with the entity the certificate was issued to.
meta:enum.authorityInformationAccess: Contains CA issuers and OCSP information.
meta:enum.certificatePolicies: Defines the policies under which the certificate was issued and can be used.
meta:enum.crlDistributionPoints: Contains one or more URLs where a Certificate Revocation List (CRL) can be obtained.
meta:enum.signedCertificateTimestamp: Shows that the certificate has been publicly logged, which helps prevent the issuance of rogue certificates by a CA. Log ID, timestamp and signature as proof.

commonExtensionValue.title: Value
commonExtensionValue.description: The value of the certificate extension.

oneOf.title: Custom Extensions
oneOf.description: Custom extensions may convey application-specific or vendor-specific data not covered by standard extensions. The structure and semantics of custom extensions are typically defined outside of public standards. CycloneDX leverages properties to support this capability.

customExtensionName.title: Name
customExtensionName.description: The name for the custom certificate extension.

customExtensionValue.title: Value
customExtensionValue.description: The description of the custom certificate extension.

relatedCryptoMaterialProperties.title: Related Cryptographic Material Properties
relatedCryptoMaterialProperties.description: Properties for cryptographic assets of asset type: `related-crypto-material`.

type.title: relatedCryptoMaterialType
type.description: The type for the related cryptographic material.

meta:enum.private-key: The confidential key of a key pair used in asymmetric cryptography.
meta:enum.public-key: The non-confidential key of a key pair used in asymmetric cryptography.
meta:enum.secret-key: A key used to encrypt and decrypt messages in symmetric cryptography.
meta:enum.key: A piece of information, usually an octet string, which, when processed through a cryptographic algorithm, processes cryptographic data.
meta:enum.ciphertext: The result of encryption performed on plaintext using an algorithm (or cipher).
meta:enum.signature: A cryptographic value that is calculated from the data and a key known only by the signer.
meta:enum.digest: The output of the hash function.
meta:enum.initialization-vector: A fixed-size random or pseudo-random value used as an input parameter for cryptographic algorithms.
meta:enum.nonce: A random or pseudo-random number that can only be used once in a cryptographic communication.
meta:enum.seed: The input to a pseudo-random number generator. Different seeds generate different pseudo-random sequences.
meta:enum.salt: A value used in a cryptographic process, usually to ensure that the results of computations for one instance cannot be reused by an attacker.
meta:enum.shared-secret: A piece of data known only to the parties involved, in a secure communication.
meta:enum.tag: A message authentication code (MAC), sometimes known as an authentication tag, is a short piece of information used for authenticating and integrity-checking a message.
meta:enum.additional-data: An unspecified collection of data with relevance to cryptographic activity.
meta:enum.password: A secret word, phrase, or sequence of characters used during authentication or authorization.
meta:enum.credential: Establishes the identity of a party to communication, usually in the form of cryptographic keys or passwords.
meta:enum.token: An object encapsulating a security identity.
meta:enum.unknown: The type of cryptographic asset is not known.

id.title: ID
id.description: The unique identifier for the related cryptographic material.

state.title: State
state.description: The key state as defined by NIST SP 800-57.

creationDate.title: Creation Date
creationDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the related cryptographic material was created.

activationDate.title: Activation Date
activationDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the related cryptographic material was activated.

updateDate.title: Update Date
updateDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the related cryptographic material was updated.

expirationDate.title: Expiration Date
expirationDate.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the related cryptographic material expires.

value.title: Value
value.description: The associated value of the cryptographic material.

size.title: Size
size.description: The size of the cryptographic asset (in bits).

format.title: Format
format.description: The format of the related cryptographic material (e.g. P8, PEM, DER).

securedBy.title: Secured By
securedBy.description: The mechanism by which the cryptographic asset is secured by.

keyUsage.title: Key Usage
keyUsage.description: Defines the permitted cryptographic usage for the asset.

protocolProperties.title: Protocol Properties
protocolProperties.description: Properties specific to cryptographic assets of type: `protocol`.

type.title: Type
type.description: The concrete protocol type.

meta:enum.unknown: The protocol type is not known

cipherSuites.title: Cipher Suites
cipherSuites.description: A list of cipher suites related to the protocol.

ikev2TransformTypes.title: IKEv2 Transform Types
ikev2TransformTypes.description: The IKEv2 transform types supported (types 1-4), defined in [RFC 7296 section 3.3.2](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc7296.html#section-3.3.2), and additional properties.

ke.title: Key Exchange Methods (KE)
ke.description: Transform Type 4: Key Exchange Method (KE) per [RFC 9370](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc9370.html), formerly called Diffie-Hellman Group (D-H).

esn.title: Extended Sequence Number (ESN)
esn.description: Specifies if an Extended Sequence Number (ESN) is used.

oid.title: OID
oid.description: The object identifier (OID) of the cryptographic asset.

name.title: Common Name
name.description: A common name for the cipher suite.

algorithms.title: Related Algorithms
algorithms.description: A list of algorithms related to the cipher suite.

items.title: Algorithm reference
items.description: The bom-ref to algorithm cryptographic asset.

identifiers.title: Cipher Suite Identifiers
identifiers.description: A list of common identifiers for the cipher suite.

tlsGroups.title: TLS Groups
tlsGroups.description: A list of TLS named groups (formerly known as curves) for this cipher suite. These groups define the parameters for key exchange algorithms like ECDHE.

items.title: Group Name
items.description: The name of the TLS group.

tlsSignatureSchemes.title: TLS Signature Schemes
tlsSignatureSchemes.description: A list of signature schemes supported for cipher suite. These schemes specify the algorithms used for digital signatures in TLS handshakes and certificate verification.

items.title: Signature Scheme
items.description: The name of the TLS signature scheme.

name.title: Name
name.description: A name for the encryption method.

keyLength.title: Encryption algorithm key length
keyLength.description: The key length of the encryption algorithm.

algorithm.title: Algorithm reference
algorithm.description: The bom-ref to algorithm cryptographic asset.

name.title: Name
name.description: A name for the pseudorandom function.

algorithm.title: Algorithm reference
algorithm.description: The bom-ref to algorithm cryptographic asset.

name.title: Name
name.description: A name for the integrity algorithm.

algorithm.title: Algorithm reference
algorithm.description: The bom-ref to algorithm cryptographic asset.

ikeV2Ke.title: Key Exchange Method (KE)
ikeV2Ke.description: Object representing a key exchange method (KE).

group.title: Group Identifier
group.description: A group identifier for the key exchange algorithm.

algorithm.title: Algorithm reference
algorithm.description: The bom-ref to algorithm cryptographic asset.

ikeV2Auth.title: IKEv2 Authentication method
ikeV2Auth.description: Object representing a IKEv2 Authentication method.

name.title: Name
name.description: A name for the authentication method.

algorithm.title: Algorithm reference
algorithm.description: The bom-ref to algorithm cryptographic asset.

meta:enum.generate: Generates random data, IVs, or nonces.
meta:enum.digest: Computes a hash value from input data.
meta:enum.tag: Generates an authentication tag for data integrity.
meta:enum.keyderive: Derives keys from another key or shared secret.
meta:enum.sign: Creates a digital signature using a private key.
meta:enum.verify: Verifies a digital signature using a public key.
meta:enum.encapsulate: Encapsulates a secret using a public key (KEM).
meta:enum.decapsulate: Decapsulates a secret using a private key (KEM).
meta:enum.keyagree: Derives a shared secret between parties.
meta:enum.wrap: Encrypts a key for secure storage or transport.
meta:enum.unwrap: Decrypts a wrapped key to recover the original key.
meta:enum.unknown: The cryptographic function is not known.

relatedCryptographicAssets.title: Related Cryptographic Assets
relatedCryptographicAssets.description: A list of cryptographic assets related to this component.

relatedCryptographicAsset.title: Related Cryptographic Asset
relatedCryptographicAsset.description: A cryptographic assets related to this component.

type.title: Type
type.description: Specifies the mechanism by which the cryptographic asset is secured by.

fingerprint.title: Fingerprint
fingerprint.description: The fingerprint is a cryptographic hash of the asset.

oneOf.title: Standard Hash
oneOf.description: A fingerprint computed using a standard, well-known hash algorithm.

oneOf.title: Custom Fingerprint
oneOf.description: A fingerprint computed with a custom or non-standard algorithm not covered by the standard hash algorithms.

customAlg.title: Custom Fingerprint Algorithm
customAlg.description: The name of the custom algorithm used to compute the fingerprint.

customContent.title: Custom Fingerprint Content
customContent.description: The value of the fingerprint computed using the custom algorithm.

securedBy.title: Secured By
securedBy.description: Specifies the mechanism by which the cryptographic asset is secured by.

mechanism.title: Mechanism
mechanism.description: Specifies the mechanism by which the cryptographic asset is secured by.

algorithmRef.title: References
algorithmRef.description: The bom-refs to the assets securing this asset (e.g., algorithms, hardware, keys).

dataProfiles.title: Data Profiles
dataProfiles.description: A catalogue of reusable data profile definitions. Each entry is a complete inline data profile carrying its own bom-ref so other objects may reference it.

dataProfile.title: Data Profile
dataProfile.description: Captures the classification, information types, and lifecycle requirements associated with data, including how it may be collected, accessed, processed, shared, handled, retained, and disposed of.

bom-ref.description: Unique identifier for this data profile.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name or label that identifies this data profile, such as the name of the dataset, information asset, or data domain it describes.

description.title: Description
description.description: A detailed explanation of the data this profile describes, including what information is contained, its purpose, origin, and any relevant context about how it is used within the system.

classification.description: The classification of the data, indicating the level of protection required. A data profile carries a single classification reflecting the highest applicable level for the data it describes.

informationTypes.title: Information Types
informationTypes.description: The types of information contained in the data. A data profile may contain multiple information types, for example a profile may carry both personally identifiable information and financial data. Each entry is either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom object describing an information type not covered by the enumeration.

examples.name: minor-health-data
examples.description: Health information for individuals under 18, subject to COPPA and HIPAA.

subjects.title: Subjects
subjects.description: The entities, groups, categories, or things that the data is about or pertains to. Identifying data subjects is essential for determining applicable privacy regulations, handling requirements, and protected class obligations.

purposes.title: Purposes
purposes.description: The stated purposes for which this data is collected, processed, or retained. Under the principle of purpose limitation, data must only be used for purposes compatible with those originally specified. Capturing purposes explicitly enables automated compliance checking and compatibility analysis across the data lifecycle.

jurisdictions.description: The jurisdictions whose laws and regulations apply to this data based on its nature, classification, or how it is processed and stored. See also subject.jurisdictions for jurisdictions that apply because of who the data is about.

regulations.description: The regulations that apply to this data based on its nature, classification, or processing context. See also subject.regulations for regulations that apply because of who the data is about.

dataFormats.title: Data Formats
dataFormats.description: The formats in which the data is stored or transmitted.

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: References to schema contracts or documents describing the data structure.

access.title: Access
access.description: References to requirement objects that define who may access the data and under what conditions, including need-to-know restrictions, clearance levels, audit logging, and break-glass procedures.

collection.title: Collection
collection.description: References to requirement objects that govern how data is collected or acquired, including consent mechanisms, lawful basis, purpose limitation, and notice obligations.

disposal.title: Disposal
disposal.description: References to requirement objects that specify how this data must be destroyed or rendered unrecoverable when no longer needed, including sanitisation methods, disposal timelines, and regulatory obligations governing data destruction.

handling.title: Handling
handling.description: References to requirement objects that govern how this data must be handled throughout its lifecycle, such as encryption at rest, access restrictions, anonymisation, or regulatory compliance obligations.

processing.title: Processing
processing.description: References to requirement objects that govern what may be done with the data, including purpose limitation, data minimisation, aggregation rules, and prohibited uses such as AI or ML training restrictions.

retention.title: Retention
retention.description: References to requirement objects that define how long this data must be retained, including minimum retention periods, maximum storage durations, and any regulatory or contractual obligations governing data preservation.

ttl.title: Time to Live
ttl.description: The time-to-live in seconds for transient data that is not persisted, such as protected health information carried inside a short-lived JSON Web Token or sensitive material held in memory for the duration of a single request. Distinct from retention, which applies to persisted data and is governed by requirement objects.

sharing.title: Sharing
sharing.description: References to requirement objects that govern how data may be shared with third parties, partners, or across jurisdictional boundaries, including data sharing agreements, cross-border transfer restrictions, and adequacy decisions.

dataProfileChoice.title: Data Profile Choice
dataProfileChoice.description: A data profile represented either as a complete inline object or as a reference to a previously declared profile. References use bom-link or bom-ref to point at a profile declared in the root profiles catalogue or elsewhere in the document.

oneOf.title: Reference
oneOf.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a previously declared data profile.

dataObject.title: Data Object
dataObject.description: A specific data payload such as a message, record, or document. Distinct from a data profile, which is a policy template; a data object is an instance whose name, schema, format, and field-level attributes describe a concrete payload. The governing policy is supplied by an optional reference to a data profile. A single profile may govern many data objects; each data object refers to at most one profile.

bom-ref.description: Unique identifier for this data object.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the data object, such as the name of the message, record, document, or table row it represents.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative explanation of the data object, including its purpose, origin, and any context relevant to interpreting its attributes.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: The data profile that governs this object's classification, information types, and lifecycle requirements. May be an inline profile object or a reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a previously declared profile.

informationTypes.title: Information Types
informationTypes.description: The types of information contained in this object. May refine or extend the information types declared on the referenced profile. Each entry is either a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom object describing an information type not covered by the enumeration.

schema.title: Schema
schema.description: The structural contract describing this object's shape, such as a JSON Schema, XSD, Avro schema, or Protobuf descriptor.

url.title: URL
url.description: URL or URN pointing to the schema document.

mediaType.title: Media Type
mediaType.description: Media type of the schema, for example application/schema+json, application/xml, or application/vnd.apache.avro+json.

comment.title: Comment
comment.description: Additional notes about the schema, such as its version or compatibility expectations.

format.title: Format
format.description: The serialisation or encoding format of this object, such as application/json, text/csv, or application/hl7-v2.

url.title: URL
url.description: URL or URN pointing to the format specification.

attributes.title: Attributes
attributes.description: The fields, columns, or properties contained in this object. Each attribute may carry a typed information type to support field-level classification and compliance analysis.

relationships.title: Relationships
relationships.description: Relationships from this data object to other data objects, such as the associations between entities in a logical or conceptual data model.

dataAttribute.title: Data Attribute
dataAttribute.description: A single field, column, or property within a data object. Carries an optional information type so that producers and consumers can classify and reason about data at field granularity rather than only at the object level.

bom-ref.description: Unique identifier for this attribute, enabling it to be referenced as a key target.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the attribute as it appears in the data object, such as a field name, column name, or property key.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative explanation of the attribute, such as its semantics, expected values, or constraints.

informationType.title: Information Type
informationType.description: The information type carried by this attribute. Field-level typing enables compliance analysis at finer granularity than the object-level information types.

required.title: Required
required.description: Indicates whether the attribute is required for the data object to be valid.

sensitive.title: Sensitive
sensitive.description: Indicates whether the attribute carries sensitive content that warrants extra protection beyond the object-level classification, such as masking in logs or stricter access controls.

key.title: Key

meta:enum.primary: The attribute is part of the primary key that uniquely identifies the data object.
meta:enum.foreign: The attribute is a foreign key referencing another data object.
meta:enum.unique: The attribute carries a uniqueness constraint.

key.description: The key role of the attribute within the data object.

references.title: References
references.description: For a foreign key, a reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the data object or attribute it refers to.

dataRelationship.title: Data Relationship
dataRelationship.description: A relationship from one data object to another, such as an association between entities in a logical or conceptual data model, carrying its cardinality and optionally the attributes that participate.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the relationship, such as the role or verb that describes it.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the relationship.

target.title: Target
target.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the related data object.

meta:enum.one-to-one: Each record of the source relates to at most one record of the target, and the reverse.
meta:enum.one-to-many: Each record of the source relates to many records of the target.
meta:enum.many-to-one: Many records of the source relate to one record of the target.
meta:enum.many-to-many: Many records of the source relate to many records of the target.

sourceAttributes.title: Source Attributes
sourceAttributes.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the attributes on this data object that participate in the relationship, such as the foreign key columns.

targetAttributes.title: Target Attributes
targetAttributes.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the attributes on the target data object that the relationship refers to, such as the primary key columns.

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: An entity, group, category, or thing that the data is about or pertains to. Subjects identify who or what the data describes, which is critical for determining applicable regulations, privacy obligations, and handling requirements. A subject is identified in one of three ways: inline by `name` and `type`, by an inline `party` object, or by `ref` pointing at any bom-ref'd element declared elsewhere in the document (a party, component, service, asset, dataset, or other element). At least one of these forms shall be supplied. Subject-context attributes such as protected-class status, applicable jurisdictions, and applicable regulations are carried here regardless of which identification form is used.

persona.description: Fleet vehicle owners enrolled in the connected-services platform.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name identifying the subject of the data, such as a specific entity name, group label, or category descriptor. Optional when `party` or `ref` is supplied.

type.title: Type
type.description: A free-form category describing the kind of subject. Organizations should establish consistent terminology within their data governance practices. Optional when `party` or `ref` is supplied.

party.title: Party
party.description: An inline party object identifying the subject. Use this when the subject is best modelled as a party (organization, person, system, or persona) and has not been declared elsewhere in the document. The party's `roles` should include `data-subject`.

ref.title: Reference
ref.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to any element declared elsewhere in the document that identifies the subject, such as a party, component, service, asset, dataset, or other bom-ref'd element. Use this when the subject is already represented elsewhere and you want to point at it rather than redeclare it.

protectedClass.title: Protected Class
protectedClass.description: Indicates whether this subject represents or includes a legally protected class under applicable regulations. Protected classes are groups defined by characteristics such as race, ethnicity, religion, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation, national origin, or genetic information. When true, additional regulatory obligations may apply to the collection, processing, and sharing of data about this subject, including GDPR special category data provisions, US civil rights protections, COPPA requirements for children, and HIPAA protections for health information.

jurisdictions.description: The jurisdictions whose laws and regulations apply specifically because of this subject. See also dataProfile.jurisdictions for jurisdictions that apply to the data itself.

regulations.description: The regulations that apply specifically because of this subject. See also dataProfile.regulations for regulations that apply to the data itself.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative explanation of the subject, its relationship to the data, and any special handling considerations. This may include why the subject is relevant, what aspects of the subject the data captures, and any known sensitivities or regulatory implications.

jurisdictions.title: Jurisdictions
jurisdictions.description: The geographic or political jurisdictions whose laws and regulations apply. Values should use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes or ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes for subnational jurisdictions.

regulations.title: Regulations
regulations.description: The specific laws, regulations, or compliance frameworks that apply.

purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.description: The stated reason for collecting, processing, or retaining data. Purpose is a foundational principle in data protection law — under GDPR Article 5(1)(b), CCPA, PIPEDA, and virtually every modern privacy framework, data must be collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes, and must not be further processed in a manner incompatible with those original purposes (purpose limitation). Capturing purposes explicitly enables automated compliance checking and purpose-compatibility analysis.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the purpose for which data is collected or processed.

description.title: Description
description.description: A detailed explanation of the purpose, how the data serves it, and any constraints on use. This narrative should be specific enough to evaluate whether subsequent processing activities are compatible with the original stated purpose.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis
legalBasis.description: The legal basis under applicable law that authorises data collection or processing for this purpose. Use terminology consistent with the applicable jurisdiction and regulation.

dataClassification.title: Data Classification
dataClassification.description: Classifies data by the level of protection it requires, either using a predefined classification level or a custom level not covered by the enumeration. Classification drives protection requirements such as access control, encryption, disclosure restrictions, and statutory handling obligations.

oneOf.title: Predefined Classification
oneOf.description: A predefined classification level from the enumeration.

meta:enum.internal: Internal use only within the organisation.
meta:enum.public: Public information that can be freely shared.
meta:enum.restricted: Highly restricted information with limited access and special protection measures.
meta:enum.classified: Government or defence classified information subject to national security controls, clearance requirements, and statutory handling obligations.

oneOf.title: Custom Classification
oneOf.description: A custom classification level not covered by the predefined enumeration.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom classification level.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of what this custom classification level means and the protections it implies.

informationType.title: Information Type
informationType.description: Identifies a type of information contained in the data, either using a predefined value from the enumeration or a custom name and description for types not covered by the enumeration. A single dataset can contain multiple information types and is captured as an array on the data profile.

oneOf.title: Predefined Information Type
oneOf.description: A predefined information type from the enumeration.

meta:enum.authentication: Authentication data such as passwords, PINs, or security questions.
meta:enum.behavioral: Behavioural data such as browsing history, usage patterns, or profiling data.
meta:enum.biometric: Biometric data such as fingerprints, facial recognition, or voice patterns.
meta:enum.communication: Communication content such as messages, emails, or call records.
meta:enum.location: Precise or inferred geographic location data.

oneOf.title: Custom Information Type
oneOf.description: A custom information type not covered by the predefined enumeration.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom information type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom information type and any handling implications it carries.

dataGovernance.title: Data Governance
dataGovernance.description: Parties responsible for the data throughout its lifecycle. Each entry is either an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` carry the governance relationship (`owner` for ownership and risk accountability, `steward` for content and business rules, `custodian` for safe custody, transport, and storage, `data-controller`, `data-processor`, `data-recipient`, or a custom role). Multiple parties may share the same role; preference among them is expressed via `role.order`.

dataFlowDirection.title: Data Flow Direction
dataFlowDirection.description: Specifies the flow direction of the data. Direction is relative to the service.

meta:enum.bi-directional: Data flows in and out of the service.
meta:enum.unknown: The directional flow of data is not known.

declarations.title: Declarations
declarations.description: The list of declarations which describe the conformance to standards. Each declaration may include attestations, claims, and evidence.

assessors.title: Assessors
assessors.description: The list of assessors evaluating claims and determining conformance to requirements and confidence in that assessment.

items.title: Assessor
items.description: The assessor who evaluates claims and determines conformance to requirements and confidence in that assessment.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

thirdParty.title: Third Party
thirdParty.description: The boolean indicating if the assessor is outside the organization generating claims. A value of false indicates a self assessor.

attestations.title: Attestations
attestations.description: The list of attestations asserted by an assessor that maps requirements to claims.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: The short description explaining the main points of the attestation.

assessor.title: Assessor
assessor.description: The `bom-ref` to the assessor asserting the attestation.

map.title: Map
map.description: The grouping of requirements to claims and the attestors declared conformance and confidence thereof.

requirement.title: Requirement
requirement.description: The `bom-ref` to the requirement being attested to.

claims.title: Claims
claims.description: The list of `bom-ref` to the claims being attested to.

counterClaims.title: Counter Claims
counterClaims.description: The list of  `bom-ref` to the counter claims being attested to.

conformance.title: Conformance
conformance.description: The conformance of the claim meeting a requirement.

score.title: Score
score.description: The conformance of the claim between and inclusive of 0 and 1, where 1 is 100% conformance.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: The rationale for the conformance score.

mitigationStrategies.title: Mitigation Strategies
mitigationStrategies.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate identified gaps in conformance with the requirement. Each mitigation strategy should be substantiated by evidence.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: The confidence of the claim meeting the requirement.

score.title: Score
score.description: The confidence of the claim between and inclusive of 0 and 1, where 1 is 100% confidence.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: The rationale for the confidence score.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

target.title: Target
target.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the target of the claim, such as a control, system, application, module, team, person, process, or business unit.

predicate.title: Predicate
predicate.description: The specific statement or assertion about the target.

mitigationStrategies.title: Mitigation Strategies
mitigationStrategies.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate identified weaknesses in the evidence supporting the claim. Each mitigation strategy should be substantiated by evidence.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: The written explanation of why the evidence provided substantiates the claim.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.description: The list of `bom-ref` to evidence that supports this claim.

counterEvidence.title: Counter Evidence
counterEvidence.description: The list of `bom-ref` to counterEvidence that supports this claim.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

propertyName.title: Property Name
propertyName.description: The reference to the property name as defined in the [CycloneDX Property Taxonomy](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-property-taxonomy/).

description.title: Description
description.description: The written description of what this evidence is and how it was created.

data.title: Data
data.description: The output or analysis that supports claims.

name.title: Data Name
name.description: The name of the data.

contents.title: Data Contents
contents.description: The contents or references to the contents of the data being described.

attachment.title: Data Attachment
attachment.description: A way to include textual or encoded data.

url.title: Data URL
url.description: The URL to where the data can be retrieved.

sensitiveData.title: Sensitive Data
sensitiveData.description: A description of any sensitive data included.

created.title: Created
created.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the evidence was created.

expires.title: Expires
expires.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the evidence is no longer valid.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: The list of targets which claims are made against.
