organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.description: The list of organizations which claims are made against.

components.title: Components
components.description: The list of components which claims are made against.

services.title: Services
services.description: The list of services which claims are made against.

affirmation.title: Affirmation
affirmation.description: A concise statement affirmed by an individual regarding all declarations, often used for third-party auditor acceptance or recipient acknowledgment. It includes a list of authorized signatories who assert the validity of the document on behalf of the organization.

statement.title: Statement
statement.description:
The brief statement affirmed by an individual regarding all declarations.
*- Notes This could be an affirmation of acceptance by a third-party auditor or receiving individual of a file.
examples: I certify, to the best of my knowledge, that all information is correct.

signatories.title: Signatories
signatories.description: The list of signatories authorized on behalf of an organization to assert validity of this document.

name.title: Name
name.description: The signatory's name.

role.title: Role
role.description: The signatory's role within an organization.

definitions.title: Definitions
definitions.description: A collection of reusable objects that are defined and may be used elsewhere in the BOM.

dependencies.title: Dependencies
dependencies.description: Provides the ability to document dependency relationships including provided & implemented components.

dependency.title: Dependency
dependency.description: Defines the direct dependencies of a component, service, or the components provided/implemented by a given component. Components or services that do not have their own dependencies must be declared as empty elements within the graph. Components or services that are not represented in the dependency graph may have unknown dependencies. It is recommended that implementations assume this to be opaque and not an indicator of an object being dependency-free. It is recommended to leverage compositions to indicate unknown dependency graphs.

ref.title: Reference
ref.description: References a component or service by its bom-ref attribute

dependsOn.title: Depends On
dependsOn.description: The bom-ref identifiers of the components or services that are dependencies of this dependency object.

provides.title: Provides
provides.description:
The bom-ref identifiers of the components or services that define a given specification or standard, which are provided or implemented by this dependency object.
For example, a cryptographic library which implements a cryptographic algorithm. A component which implements another component does not imply that the implementation is in use.

formulation.title: Formulation
formulation.description: Describes the formulation of any referencable object within the BOM, including components, services, metadata, declarations, or the BOM itself. This may encompass how the object was created, assembled, deployed, tested, certified, or otherwise brought into its present form. Common examples include software build pipelines, deployment processes, AI/ML model training, cryptographic key generation or certification, and third-party audits. Processes are modeled using declared and observed formulas, composed of workflows, tasks, and individual steps.

formula.title: Formula
formula.description: Describes workflows and resources that captures rules and other aspects of how the associated BOM component or service was formed.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the formula elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

components.title: Components
components.description: Transient components that are used in tasks that constitute one or more of this formula's workflows

services.title: Services
services.description: Transient services that are used in tasks that constitute one or more of this formula's workflows

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.description: List of workflows that can be declared to accomplish specific orchestrated goals and independently triggered.
workflows.$comment: Different workflows can be designed to work together to perform end-to-end CI/CD builds and deployments.

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.$comment: Workflow are as task themselves and can trigger other workflow tasks.  These relationships can be modeled in the taskDependencies graph.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the workflow elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

uid.title: Unique Identifier (UID)
uid.description: The unique identifier for the resource instance within its deployment context.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the resource instance.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the resource instance.

resourceReferences.title: Resource references
resourceReferences.description: References to component or service resources that are used to realize the resource instance.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.description: The tasks that comprise the workflow.
tasks.$comment: Note that tasks can appear more than once as different instances (by name or UID).

taskDependencies.title: Task dependency graph
taskDependencies.description: The graph of dependencies between tasks within the workflow.

taskTypes.title: Task types
taskTypes.description: Indicates the types of activities performed by the set of workflow tasks.
taskTypes.$comment: Currently, these types reflect common CI/CD actions.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: The trigger that initiated the task.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: The sequence of steps for the task.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Represents resources and data brought into a task at runtime by executor or task commands
examples: a `configuration` file which was declared as a local `component` or `externalReference`

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Represents resources and data output from a task at runtime by executor or task commands
examples: a log file or metrics data produced by the task

timeStart.title: Time start
timeStart.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the task started.

timeEnd.title: Time end
timeEnd.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the task ended.

workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.description: A set of named filesystem or data resource shareable by workflow tasks.

runtimeTopology.title: Runtime topology
runtimeTopology.description: A graph of the component runtime topology for workflow's instance.
runtimeTopology.$comment: A description of the runtime component and service topology.  This can describe a partial or complete topology used to host and execute the task (e.g., hardware, operating systems, configurations, etc.),

task.title: Task
task.description: Describes the inputs, sequence of steps and resources used to accomplish a task and its output.
task.$comment: Tasks are building blocks for constructing assemble CI/CD workflows or pipelines.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the task elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

uid.title: Unique Identifier (UID)
uid.description: The unique identifier for the resource instance within its deployment context.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the resource instance.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the resource instance.

resourceReferences.title: Resource references
resourceReferences.description: References to component or service resources that are used to realize the resource instance.

taskTypes.title: Task types
taskTypes.description: Indicates the types of activities performed by the set of workflow tasks.
taskTypes.$comment: Currently, these types reflect common CI/CD actions.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: The trigger that initiated the task.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: The sequence of steps for the task.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Represents resources and data brought into a task at runtime by executor or task commands
examples: a `configuration` file which was declared as a local `component` or `externalReference`

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Represents resources and data output from a task at runtime by executor or task commands
examples: a log file or metrics data produced by the task

timeStart.title: Time start
timeStart.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the task started.

timeEnd.title: Time end
timeEnd.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the task ended.

workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.description: A set of named filesystem or data resource shareable by workflow tasks.

runtimeTopology.title: Runtime topology
runtimeTopology.description: A graph of the component runtime topology for task's instance.
runtimeTopology.$comment: A description of the runtime component and service topology.  This can describe a partial or complete topology used to host and execute the task (e.g., hardware, operating systems, configurations, etc.),

step.description: Executes specific commands or tools in order to accomplish its owning task as part of a sequence.

name.title: Name
name.description: A name for the step.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the step.

commands.title: Commands
commands.description: Ordered list of commands or directives for the step

executed.title: Executed
executed.description: A text representation of the executed command.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.description: A named filesystem or data resource shareable by workflow tasks.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the workspace elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

uid.title: Unique Identifier (UID)
uid.description: The unique identifier for the resource instance within its deployment context.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the resource instance.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: The names for the workspace as referenced by other workflow tasks. Effectively, a name mapping so other tasks can use their own local name in their steps.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the resource instance.

resourceReferences.title: Resource references
resourceReferences.description: References to component or service resources that are used to realize the resource instance.

accessMode.title: Access mode
accessMode.description: Describes the read-write access control for the workspace relative to the owning resource instance.

mountPath.title: Mount path
mountPath.description: A path to a location on disk where the workspace will be available to the associated task's steps.

managedDataType.title: Managed data type
managedDataType.description: The name of a domain-specific data type the workspace represents.
managedDataType.$comment: This property is for CI/CD frameworks that are able to provide access to structured, managed data at a more granular level than a filesystem.

volumeRequest.title: Volume request
volumeRequest.description: Identifies the reference to the request for a specific volume type and parameters.
examples: a kubernetes Persistent Volume Claim (PVC) name

volume.title: Volume
volume.description: Information about the actual volume instance allocated to the workspace.
volume.$comment: The actual volume allocated may be different than the request.

volume.title: Volume
volume.description: An identifiable, logical unit of data storage tied to a physical device.

uid.title: Unique Identifier (UID)
uid.description: The unique identifier for the volume instance within its deployment context.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the volume instance

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: The mode for the volume instance.

path.title: Path
path.description: The underlying path created from the actual volume.

sizeAllocated.title: Size allocated
sizeAllocated.description: The allocated size of the volume accessible to the associated workspace. This should include the scalar size as well as IEC standard unit in either decimal or binary form.

persistent.title: Persistent
persistent.description: Indicates if the volume persists beyond the life of the resource it is associated with.

remote.title: Remote
remote.description: Indicates if the volume is remotely (i.e., network) attached.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: Represents a resource that can conditionally activate (or fire) tasks based upon associated events and their data.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the trigger elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

uid.title: Unique Identifier (UID)
uid.description: The unique identifier for the resource instance within its deployment context.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the resource instance.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the resource instance.

resourceReferences.title: Resource references
resourceReferences.description: References to component or service resources that are used to realize the resource instance.

type.title: Type
type.description: The source type of event which caused the trigger to fire.

event.title: Event
event.description: The event data that caused the associated trigger to activate.

conditions.title: Conditions
conditions.description: A list of conditions used to determine if a trigger should be activated.

timeActivated.title: Time activated
timeActivated.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the trigger was activated.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Represents resources and data brought into a task at runtime by executor or task commands
examples: a `configuration` file which was declared as a local `component` or `externalReference`

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Represents resources and data output from a task at runtime by executor or task commands
examples: a log file or metrics data produced by the task

event.title: Event
event.description: Represents something that happened that may trigger a response.

uid.title: Unique Identifier (UID)
uid.description: The unique identifier of the event.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the event.

timeReceived.title: Time Received
timeReceived.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the event was received.

data.title: Data
data.description: Encoding of the raw event data.

source.title: Source
source.description: References the component or service that was the source of the event

target.title: Target

resource.title: Resource

examples: a reference to a configuration file in a repository (i.e., a bom-ref)
a reference to a scanning service used in a task (i.e., a bom-ref)

These string-based environment variables typically act as flags or signals to software, indicating that a feature should be enabled, a mode should be activated, or a specific condition is present. Their presence alone conveys meaning.

These string-based environment variables typically act as flags or signals to software, indicating that a feature should be enabled, a mode should be activated, or a specific condition is present. Their presence alone conveys meaning.

resourceReferenceChoice.title: Resource reference choice

meta:enum.copy: A task that copies software or data used to accomplish other tasks in the workflow.
meta:enum.clone: A task that clones a software repository into the workflow in order to retrieve its source code or data for use in a build step.
meta:enum.lint: A task that checks source code for programmatic and stylistic errors.
meta:enum.scan: A task that performs a scan against source code, or built or deployed components and services. Scans are typically run to gather or test for security vulnerabilities or policy compliance.
meta:enum.merge: A task that merges changes or fixes into source code prior to a build step in the workflow.
meta:enum.build: A task that builds the source code, dependencies and/or data into an artifact that can be deployed to and executed on target systems.
meta:enum.test: A task that verifies the functionality of a component or service.
meta:enum.deliver: A task that delivers a built artifact to one or more target repositories or storage systems.
meta:enum.deploy: A task that deploys a built artifact for execution on one or more target systems.
meta:enum.release: A task that releases a built, versioned artifact to a target repository or distribution system.
meta:enum.clean: A task that cleans unnecessary tools, build artifacts and/or data from workflow storage.
meta:enum.other: A workflow task that does not match current task type definitions.

parameter.title: Parameter
parameter.description: A representation of a functional parameter.

name.title: Name

dataType.title: Data type
dataType.description: The data type of the parameter.

cyclonedx-jss_X590_2023_10-2.0.title: CycloneDX Model for JSON Signature Scheme (JSS)
cyclonedx-jss_X590_2023_10-2.0.description: JSON Schema implementing ITU-T X.590 (10/2023) – JSON Signature Scheme (JSS). Defines the structure for digitally signing JSON objects while keeping the payload in JSON format. Supports single signatures, multiple independent signatures, and chained counter-signatures. Intended for use as a $defs import in CycloneDX 2.0.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: An RFC 3339 timestamp in the UTC+0 time zone. Must use the 'Z' suffix. Sub-second precision, when present, MUST be exactly three digits (millisecond precision). If no sub-second digits are included, the decimal point MUST NOT be present. Matches the pattern: yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss[.sss]Z

identifier.title: Identifier
identifier.description: A Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) conformant with RFC 4122. Used to uniquely identify a signature object. Signatures sharing the same 'id' are treated as versions of the same logical signature, differentiated by their 'modified' timestamp.

hashAlgorithm.title: Hash Algorithm
hashAlgorithm.description: This property identifies the hashing algorithm, as defined by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA)…, that was used to hash the JCS version of the full JSON object (JSON Object + JSS Signature) and is a case-sensitive ASCII string. Implementations MAY use any current and widely accepted hashing algorithm (e.g., sha-256, sha-512) that is defined in the IANA registry. The actual signing process, defined in the algorithm property, sometimes uses an internal hashing algorithm inside the signing process itself, this property MAY identify the same hashing algorithm as the signing process or MAY identify a different hashing algorithm.

algorithmVocabulary.title: Signature Algorithm Type Vocabulary (signature-algorithm-type-ov)
algorithmVocabulary.description: NOTE – At the time of this writing quantum safe algorithms could come from those defined in XMSS [IETF RFC 8391] section 5.3 or LMS [IETF RFC 8554] section 5.1 and other algorithms could come from those defined in JWA [IETF RFC 7518] section 3.1 or [IETF RFC 8037] section 3.1 (see the table below for a list of values from those RFCs). While JWA [IETF RFC 7518] section 3.1 defines the following symmetric algorithms: HS256, HS384, and HS512, these algorithms SHOULD NOT be used. If one of these three symmetric algorithms is used, the sharing and transmission of those keys is out of scope for this Recommendation.
algorithmVocabulary.$comment: Normative source: ITU-T X.590 clause 6.2.2 and Table 1

algorithm.title: Signing Algorithm
algorithm.description: This property identifies the algorithm that was used to sign the JSON data and is a case-sensitive ASCII string. The value for this property SHOULD come from the signature-algorithm-type-ov vocabulary (see clause 6.2.2) and SHOULD be a current and widely accepted quantum safe algorithm, but MAY use any currently accepted safe algorithm.

anyOf.title: Other algorithm identifier
anyOf.description: Any other current, widely accepted, safe algorithm identifier not present in the signature-algorithm-type-ov vocabulary (X.590 clause 6.2.2 makes that vocabulary a SHOULD, not a MUST).

publicKey.title: Public Key (PEM, header-stripped)
publicKey.description: This property contains a privacy enhanced mail (PEM) encoded public key without the header and footer for the algorithm selected in the algorithm property.

publicCertChain.title: Public Certificate Chain
publicCertChain.description: This property contains a public key certificate for the algorithm selected in the algorithm property and MUST follow the requirements defined in section 4.7 of [IETF RFC 7517] as quoted here. This property "contains a chain (X.509 certificate chain) of one or more PKIX certificates [IETF RFC 5280]. The certificate chain is represented as a JSON array of certificate value strings. Each string in the array is a base64-encoded (section 4 of [IETF RFC 4648] – not base64URL.encoded) DER [b-ITU-T X.690] PKIX certificate value. The PKIX certificate containing the key value MUST be the first certificate. This MAY be followed by additional certificates, with each subsequent certificate being the one used to certify the previous one. The key in the first certificate MUST match the public key." This property is called "x5c" in section 4.7 of [IETF RFC 7517].

items.description: Base64-encoded (standard, not base64URL) DER-encoded PKIX certificate value (RFC 5280).

certUrl.title: Certificate URL
certUrl.description: This property contains a uniform resource identifier (URI) [IETF RFC 3986] that refers to a resource for an X.509 public key certificate or certificate chain [IETF RFC 5280] for the algorithm selected in the algorithm property and MUST follow the requirements defined in section 4.6 of [IETF RFC 7517] as quoted here. "The identified resource MUST provide a representation of the certificate or certificate chain that conforms to RFC 5280 [IETF RFC 5280] in PEM-encoded form, with each certificate delimited as specified in section 6.1 of RFC 4945 [IETF RFC 4945]. The key in the first certificate MUST match the public key. The protocol used to acquire the resource MUST provide integrity protection; an HTTP GET request to retrieve the certificate MUST use TLS [IETF RFC 2818] [IETF RFC 5246]; the identity of the server MUST be validated, as per section 6 of RFC 6125 [IETF RFC 6125]." This property is called "x5u" in section 4.6 of [IETF RFC 7517].

thumbprint.title: Certificate Thumbprint
thumbprint.description: This property contains a fingerprint of a public key or public key certificate for the algorithm selected in the algorithm property and MUST follow the requirements defined in section 4.9 of [IETF RFC 7517] as quoted here. This property "is a base64URL.encoded SHA-256 thumbprint (a.k.a. digest, X.509 certificate SHA-256 thumbprint) of the DER encoding of an X.509 certificate [IETF RFC 5280]. Note that certificate thumbprints are also sometimes known as certificate fingerprints. The key in the certificate MUST match the public key." This property is called "x5t#S256" in section 4.9 of [IETF RFC 7517].

signatureObject.title: Signature Object
signatureObject.description: A JSS signature object as defined in ITU-T X.590 clause 6.2. Captures the hashing algorithm, signing algorithm, public key material (in one of four forms), the base64URL-encoded digital signature value, and optional metadata. May nest a child 'signature' property to form a counter-signature chain.
signatureObject.$comment: unevaluatedProperties: true is intentional. ITU-T X.590 clause 6.2 explicitly states that implementers MAY add additional properties to satisfy meta-data requirements for their application. The clause 6.3 properties (type, id, related_to, etc.) are themselves defined as non-exhaustive examples of such extensions.

value.title: Signature Value
value.description: A base64URL.encoded signature that was created using the signature algorithm defined in the algorithm property and a key. In pseudo code it is defined as: base64URL.encode(sign(algorithm, key, hash(jcs(<JSONObject with Signature Object>)))).

signature.title: Counter-Signature
signature.description: This property enables a signature to be countersigned, meaning a signature can be signed by another signature.

type.title: Type Indicator
type.description: The value of this property MUST be jss.

id.title: Signature Identifier
id.description: A value that uniquely identifies the signature. All signatures with the same ID are considered different versions of the same signature and the version of the signature is identified by its modified property.

related_to.title: Related Object Reference
related_to.description: A value that can identify the original JSON object that was signed with this signature. If the signature is detached from the original JSON object this property SHOULD be populated.

related_version.title: Related Object Version
related_version.description: A value that can identify the version of the original JSON object that was signed with this signature. If the signature is detached from the original JSON object this property SHOULD be populated.

created.title: Created Timestamp
created.description: The time at which this signature was originally created. The creator can use any time it deems most appropriate as the time the signature was created, but it MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds). The created property MUST NOT be changed when creating a new version of the signature.

modified.title: Modified Timestamp
modified.description: The time that this particular version of the signature was last modified. The creator can use any time it deems most appropriate as the time that this version of the signature was modified, but it MUST be precise to the nearest millisecond (exactly three digits after the decimal place in seconds). The modified property MUST be later than or equal to the value of the created property. If the created and modified properties are the same, then this is the first version of the signature.
modified.$comment: Normative source: ITU-T X.590 clause 6.3.1. Cross-field invariant: 'modified' MUST be greater than or equal to 'created'. JSON Schema 2020-12 cannot compare two sibling property values, so this constraint is NOT enforced by this schema and MUST be validated by the consuming tool or conformance suite.

revoked.title: Revoked Flag
revoked.description: A boolean that identifies if the signature creator deems that this signature is no longer valid. The default value is false.

signee.title: Signee
signee.description: An unstructured string value for the name of the entity or organization that produced this signature.

valid_from.title: Valid From
valid_from.description: The time from which this signature is considered valid. If omitted, the signature is valid at all times or until the timestamp defined by valid_until. If the revoked property is true then this property MUST be ignored.

valid_until.title: Valid Until
valid_until.description: The time at which this signature is no longer considered valid. If the valid_until property is omitted, then there is no constraint on the latest time for which the signature is valid. This property MUST be greater than the timestamp in the valid_from property if the valid_from property is defined. If the revoked property is true then this property MUST be ignored.
valid_until.$comment: Normative source: ITU-T X.590 clause 6.3.1. Cross-field invariant: when 'valid_from' is present, 'valid_until' MUST be greater than 'valid_from'. JSON Schema 2020-12 cannot compare two sibling property values, so this constraint is NOT enforced by this schema and MUST be validated by the consuming tool or conformance suite.

anyOf.description: Public key material provided as PEM-encoded public key (header/footer stripped).
Public key material provided as a base64-encoded DER X.509 certificate chain.
Public key material provided by reference to a TLS-accessible certificate URI.
Public key material referenced by a base64URL-encoded SHA-256 certificate thumbprint.

signatures.title: Signatures Array
signatures.description: A JSON array of one or more signature objects. MUST be located at the top-level of the signed JSON object. The property name used to hold this array SHOULD be 'signatures', but implementations MAY use a different name provided it does not conflict with any other top-level property name in the host JSON object. All signature objects in this array are independent parallel signatures over the same JSON object; for chained counter-signatures, use the nested 'signature' property within an individual signature object.
signatures.$comment: Normative source: ITU-T X.590 clause 6 – 'The property that holds the signature MUST be a JSON list property and MUST be located at the top-level of the original JSON object.'

licenseChoice.title: License Choice
licenseChoice.description: A list of SPDX licenses and/or named licenses and/or SPDX License Expression.

oneOf.title: License Expression
oneOf.description:
Specifies the details and attributes related to a software license.
It must be a valid SPDX license expression, along with additional properties such as license acknowledgment.

expression.title: SPDX License Expression
expression.description:
A valid SPDX license expression.
Refer to https://spdx.org/specifications for syntax requirements.

items.description:
This document specifies the details and attributes related to a software license identifier. An SPDX expression may be a compound of license identifiers.
The `license_identifier` property serves as the key that identifies each record. Note that this key is not required to be unique, as the same license identifier could apply to multiple, different but similar license details, texts, etc.

licenseIdentifier.title: License Identifier
licenseIdentifier.description:
The valid SPDX license identifier. Refer to https://spdx.org/specifications for syntax requirements.
This property serves as the primary key, which uniquely identifies each record.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the license elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

text.title: License texts
text.description: A way to include the textual content of the license.

url.title: License URL
url.description: The URL to the license file. If specified, a 'license' externalReference should also be specified for completeness

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the license elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

license.title: License
license.description: Specifies the details and attributes related to a software license. It can either include a valid SPDX license identifier or a named license, along with additional properties such as license acknowledgment, comprehensive commercial licensing information, and the full text of the license.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the license elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

id.title: License ID (SPDX)
id.description: A valid SPDX license identifier. If specified, this value must be one of the enumeration of valid SPDX license identifiers defined in the spdx.schema.json (or spdx.xml) subschema which is synchronized with the official SPDX license list.

name.title: License Name
name.description: The name of the license. This may include the name of a commercial or proprietary license or an open source license that may not be defined by SPDX.

text.title: License text
text.description: A way to include the textual content of a license.

url.title: License URL
url.description: The URL to the license file. If specified, a 'license' externalReference should also be specified for completeness

licenseAcknowledgementEnumeration.title: License Acknowledgement
licenseAcknowledgementEnumeration.description: Declared licenses and concluded licenses represent two different stages in the licensing process within software development. Declared licenses refer to the initial intention of the software authors regarding the licensing terms under which their code is released. On the other hand, concluded licenses are the result of a comprehensive analysis of the project's codebase to identify and confirm the actual licenses of the components used, which may differ from the initially declared licenses. While declared licenses provide an upfront indication of the licensing intentions, concluded licenses offer a more thorough understanding of the actual licensing within a project, facilitating proper compliance and risk management. Observed licenses are defined in `@.evidence.licenses`. Observed licenses form the evidence necessary to substantiate a concluded license.

meta:enum.declared: Declared licenses represent the initial intentions of authors regarding the licensing terms of their code.
meta:enum.concluded: Concluded licenses are verified and confirmed.

licensing.title: Licensing information
licensing.description: Licensing details describing the licensor/licensee, license type, renewal and expiration dates, and other important metadata

altIds.title: Alternate License Identifiers
altIds.description: License identifiers that may be used to manage licenses and their lifecycle

licensor.title: Licensor
licensor.description: The individual or organization that grants a license to another individual or organization

organization.title: Licensor (Organization)
organization.description: The organization that granted the license

individual.title: Licensor (Individual)
individual.description: The individual, not associated with an organization, that granted the license

licensee.title: Licensee
licensee.description: The individual or organization for which a license was granted to

organization.title: Licensee (Organization)
organization.description: The organization that was granted the license

individual.title: Licensee (Individual)
individual.description: The individual, not associated with an organization, that was granted the license

purchaser.title: Purchaser
purchaser.description: The individual or organization that purchased the license

organization.title: Purchaser (Organization)
organization.description: The organization that purchased the license

individual.title: Purchaser (Individual)
individual.description: The individual, not associated with an organization, that purchased the license

purchaseOrder.title: Purchase Order
purchaseOrder.description: The purchase order identifier the purchaser sent to a supplier or vendor to authorize a purchase

licenseTypes.title: License Type
licenseTypes.description: The type of license(s) that was granted to the licensee.

meta:enum.academic: A license that grants use of software solely for the purpose of education or research.
meta:enum.appliance: A license covering use of software embedded in a specific piece of hardware.
meta:enum.client-access: A Client Access License (CAL) allows client computers to access services provided by server software.
meta:enum.concurrent-user: A Concurrent User license (aka floating license) limits the number of licenses for a software application and licenses are shared among a larger number of users.
meta:enum.core-points: A license where the core of a computer's processor is assigned a specific number of points.
meta:enum.custom-metric: A license for which consumption is measured by non-standard metrics.
meta:enum.device: A license that covers a defined number of installations on computers and other types of devices.
meta:enum.evaluation: A license that grants permission to install and use software for trial purposes.
meta:enum.named-user: A license that grants access to the software to one or more pre-defined users.
meta:enum.node-locked: A license that grants access to the software on one or more pre-defined computers or devices.
meta:enum.oem: An Original Equipment Manufacturer license that is delivered with hardware, cannot be transferred to other hardware, and is valid for the life of the hardware.
meta:enum.perpetual: A license where the software is sold on a one-time basis and the licensee can use a copy of the software indefinitely.
meta:enum.processor-points: A license where each installation consumes points per processor.
meta:enum.subscription: A license where the licensee pays a fee to use the software or service.
meta:enum.user: A license that grants access to the software or service by a specified number of users.

lastRenewal.title: Last Renewal
lastRenewal.description: The timestamp indicating when the license was last renewed. For new purchases, this is often the purchase or acquisition date. For non-perpetual licenses or subscriptions, this is the timestamp of when the license was last renewed.

expiration.title: Expiration
expiration.description: The timestamp indicating when the current license expires (if applicable).

metadata.title: BOM Metadata
metadata.description: Provides additional information about a BOM.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the BOM was created.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: The tool(s) used in the creation, enrichment, and validation of the BOM.

components.description: A list of software and hardware components used as tools.

services.description: A list of services used as tools. This may include microservices, function-as-a-service, and other types of network or intra-process services.

manufacturer.title: BOM Manufacturer
manufacturer.description:
The organization that created the BOM.
Manufacturer is common in BOMs created through automated processes. BOMs created through manual means may have `@.authors` instead.

authors.title: BOM Authors
authors.description:
The person(s) who created the BOM.
Authors are common in BOMs created through manual processes. BOMs created through automated means may have `@.manufacturer` instead.

component.description: The component that the BOM describes.

supplier.title: Supplier
supplier.description: The organization that supplied the component that the BOM describes. The supplier may often be the manufacturer, but may also be a distributor or repackager.

licenses.title: BOM License(s)
licenses.description:
The license information for the BOM document.
This may be different from the license(s) of the component(s) that the BOM describes.

distributionConstraints.title: Distribution Constraints
distributionConstraints.description: Conditions and constraints governing the sharing and distribution of the data or components described by this BOM.

tlp.description: The Traffic Light Protocol (TLP) classification that controls the sharing and distribution of the data that the BOM describes.

tlpClassification.title: Traffic Light Protocol (TLP) Classification
tlpClassification.description:
Traffic Light Protocol (TLP) is a classification system for identifying the potential risk associated with artefact, including whether it is subject to certain types of legal, financial, or technical threats. Refer to [https://www.first.org/tlp/](https://www.first.org/tlp/) for further information.
The default classification is "CLEAR"

meta:enum.CLEAR: The information is not subject to any restrictions as regards the sharing.
meta:enum.GREEN: The information is subject to limited disclosure, and recipients can share it within their community but not via publicly accessible channels.
meta:enum.AMBER: The information is subject to limited disclosure, and recipients can only share it on a need-to-know basis within their organization and with clients.
meta:enum.AMBER_AND_STRICT: The information is subject to limited disclosure, and recipients can only share it on a need-to-know basis within their organization.
meta:enum.RED: The information is subject to restricted distribution to individual recipients only and must not be shared.

party.title: Party
party.description:
Identifies an organization, individual, system, or abstract archetype that participates in supplying, producing, attesting, operating, owning, regulating, or otherwise relating to the subject. Each party plays one or more named roles. Roles may carry preference order (`role.order`) to express primary, alternate, and secondary rankings among parties sharing the same role.

Exactly one identity sub-shape (`organization`, `person`, `system`, or `persona`) shall be present. Optional sub-objects layer inter-party relationships and standard extension data.

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: One or more roles the party fulfils. Each role may carry an `order` indicating preference among parties sharing the same role (for example, primary versus alternate supplier).

organization.description: Identity attributes valid when the party is an organization, company, government body, or other collective.

person.description: Identity attributes valid when the party is an individual person.

system.title: System
system.description: Identity attributes valid when the party is a software system, hardware system, service account, automation, or autonomous agent.

persona.title: Persona
persona.description: Identity attributes valid when the party is an abstract archetype rather than a specific named instance.

relations.title: Relations
relations.description: Links from this party to other parties.

parties.title: Parties
parties.description: Parties associated with the subject. Each item identifies an organization, individual, system, or abstract archetype playing one or more named roles such as manufacturer, supplier, author, integrator, quality-control, or any custom role. The same party can hold multiple roles, each independently ranked via `role.order` to express preference (for example, primary supplier with order 1 and alternate supplier with order 2). Items may be inline party objects or references to parties declared elsewhere.

partyChoice.title: Party Choice
partyChoice.description: A party represented either as a complete object or as a reference to a previously declared party.

oneOf.title: Reference
oneOf.description: A refLinkType pointing to a previously declared party.

role.title: Role
role.description: A role that a party fulfils. May be a predefined role from the CycloneDX role taxonomy or a custom role definition. The optional `order` property ranks parties that share the same role, supporting use cases such as primary versus alternate suppliers in hardware manufacturing supply chains.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom role.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom role, including its responsibilities and scope.

order.title: Order
order.description: Preference order among parties sharing this role. Lower values indicate higher preference. Ties are permitted. Absence means unranked.

role.title: Role
role.description: A predefined role from the CycloneDX role taxonomy.

meta:enum.agent: Software or AI agent acting autonomously or semi-autonomously, typically on behalf of a principal.
meta:enum.assembler: The party that assembles or integrates constituent parts into the subject. Common in hardware manufacturing and software build pipelines.
meta:enum.asserter: The party making assertions about the subject, such as patent ownership or compliance claims.
meta:enum.attacker: A hostile party targeting the subject.
meta:enum.auditor: The party that conducted an audit or assessment of the subject.
meta:enum.author: The party that created the subject. Common when the subject is created through manual processes.
meta:enum.broker: The party that acts as an intermediary in commercial transactions.
meta:enum.carrier: The party that physically transports goods, such as shipping lines, airlines, or trucking companies.
meta:enum.certificate-authority: The party that issues, signs, and manages digital certificates within a public key infrastructure.
meta:enum.committer: The party who committed or pushed changes to a version control system.
meta:enum.competitor: A competing party in the same market or domain as the subject's organization.
meta:enum.consignee: The party designated to receive a shipment of goods.
meta:enum.consignor: The party that sends or ships goods to a consignee.
meta:enum.contributor: A party that contributed to the development of the subject without being the primary author.
meta:enum.customer: Customer of the organization that owns the subject.
meta:enum.custodian: The party responsible for the safe custody, transport, and storage of the subject.
meta:enum.customs-broker: The party that facilitates the clearance of goods through customs barriers.
meta:enum.data-controller: The party that determines the purposes and means of processing personal data.
meta:enum.data-processor: The party that processes personal data on behalf of a data controller.
meta:enum.data-recipient: The party to which personal data is disclosed.
meta:enum.data-subject: The natural person whose personal data is processed.
meta:enum.delegate: A party exercising authority on behalf of another party identified via relations.delegatedBy.
meta:enum.distributor: The party that distributes the subject to downstream consumers or customers.
meta:enum.end-user: The end user of a system, service, or product.
meta:enum.engineer: Engineer responsible for designing, implementing, or operating a system.
meta:enum.exporter: The party that sends goods to another country for trade or sale.
meta:enum.freight-forwarder: The party that arranges the shipment and logistics of goods on behalf of shippers.
meta:enum.holder: The party that holds a verifiable credential, claim, or asset.
meta:enum.importer: The party that brings goods into a country from abroad for trade or sale.
meta:enum.insider-threat: A hostile or negligent party with legitimate access.
meta:enum.inspector: The party that inspects goods for quality, safety, or regulatory compliance.
meta:enum.insurer: The party that provides insurance coverage.
meta:enum.integrator: The party that integrates the subject into a larger system or product.
meta:enum.issuer: The party that issues a credential, claim, identifier, or asset.
meta:enum.key-escrow-agent: The party that holds copies of cryptographic keys in escrow.
meta:enum.legal-contact: The designated party to contact for legal matters.
meta:enum.licensee: The party to which a license for the subject has been granted.
meta:enum.licensor: The party that grants a license for the subject.
meta:enum.maintainer: The party responsible for ongoing maintenance, including updates, patches, and security fixes.
meta:enum.manufacturer: The party that manufactured or produced the subject. Common when the subject is produced through automated processes.
meta:enum.operator: The party responsible for operating or running the subject in a production environment.
meta:enum.owner: The party that holds ownership rights over the subject.
meta:enum.packager: The party that packages goods for storage, shipment, or retail sale.
meta:enum.partner: Business partner with a defined relationship.
meta:enum.principal: The party on whose behalf another party acts, paired with delegate.
meta:enum.publisher: The party that published the subject, making it available for consumption.
meta:enum.purchaser: The party that purchased the subject or a license for its use.
meta:enum.quality-control: The party responsible for quality control activities, including inspection, testing, and verification.
meta:enum.regulator: Government or industry regulator with administrative authority over the subject.
meta:enum.relying-party: The party that relies on credentials, claims, or attestations issued by another party.
meta:enum.repackager: The party that repackages the subject, potentially combining it with other components.
meta:enum.researcher: Security researcher, bug bounty hunter, or academic conducting authorized study.
meta:enum.reviewer: The party that reviewed the subject or its associated evidence.
meta:enum.security-contact: The designated party to contact in the event of a security incident.
meta:enum.signatory: The party authorized to sign on behalf of an organization, affirming the validity or accuracy of statements or attestations.
meta:enum.steward: The party responsible for the content, context, and associated business rules of the subject.
meta:enum.subject: The party to whom an issued credential, claim, or identifier is bound.
meta:enum.supplier: The party that supplied the subject. The supplier may often be the manufacturer, but may also be a distributor or repackager.
meta:enum.support-contact: The designated party to contact for technical support.
meta:enum.third-party-logistics: The party that provides outsourced logistics services.
meta:enum.timestamp-authority: The party that issues trusted timestamps.
meta:enum.validation-authority: The party that provides certificate validation services.
meta:enum.verifier: The party that verifies credentials, claims, or attestations.
meta:enum.warehouse-operator: The party responsible for storing, handling, and managing inventory within a warehouse or distribution centre.

order.title: Order
order.description: Preference order among parties sharing this role. Lower values indicate higher preference. Ties are permitted. Absence means unranked. For example, a primary supplier may have `order: 1` while an alternate supplier has `order: 2`.

organization.title: Organization Identity
organization.description: Identity attributes for a party that is an organization, company, government body, or other collective.

name.title: Name
name.description: The common display or trading name of the organization. Use when the registered legal name is unknown or when the everyday name differs from the legal one.

legalName.title: Legal Name
legalName.description: The registered legal name of the organization, including any suffix such as Inc., S.r.l., GmbH, or LLC.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the organization itself, distinct from any role-specific or contextual description applied at the party wrapper level.

logo.title: Logo
logo.description: URL to an image representing the organization. Useful for catalog, datasheet, and user interface views.

foundingDate.title: Founding Date
foundingDate.description: The date the organization was founded. Supports supplier due diligence and age-of-organization signals.

dissolutionDate.title: Dissolution Date
dissolutionDate.description: The date the organization was dissolved or wound down. When present, the organization is no longer active.

jurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction
jurisdiction.description: Country of registration as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 code, optionally followed by an ISO 3166-2 subdivision separated by a hyphen.

identifiers.title: Identifiers
identifiers.description: Identifiers issued to or associated with the organization. May include legal and registration identifiers (LEI, DUNS, CAGE, NCAGE, EORI, VAT, tax identifiers) and non-legal identifiers such as workload or machine identities.

formerNames.title: Former Names
formerNames.description: Prior names of the organization. Use when the entity has been renamed, merged, or acquired. Distinct from `aliases`, which captures concurrent alternate designations.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: Concurrent alternate designations for the organization. Distinct from `formerNames`, which captures historical names. Common when modeling tracked threat-actor groups that are known by different designations across threat-intelligence vendors.

url.title: URLs
url.description: URLs associated with the organization. Each entry carries a `name` label and a `url` value so producers can describe homepage, support portal, press, status, code repository, and similar without ambiguity.

name.title: Name
name.description: Label describing the URL, such as `homepage`, `support`, `press`, `status`, or `repository`.

addresses.title: Addresses
addresses.description: Physical addresses associated with the organization.

person.title: Person Identity
person.description: Identity attributes for a party that is an individual person. The name model is intentionally unstructured: a single freeform `name` captures the full name as the person wishes to be known, accommodating the wide variation of naming conventions across cultures. See https://www.w3.org/International/questions/qa-personal-names for background.

name.title: Name
name.description: The person's full name as they wish to be known. A single freeform string. No assumption is made about given-versus-family ordering, mononym versus multi-component names, patronymics, or honorific embedding. Producers should use the form the person uses themselves.

sortName.title: Sort Name
sortName.description: Optional sortable form of the name for catalog and index views. Use when the natural form of `name` does not sort intuitively, for example a comma-separated last-name-first form.

honorificPrefix.title: Honorific Prefix
honorificPrefix.description: Honorific or title preceding the name, used in forms of address. Distinct from the name itself.

honorificSuffix.title: Honorific Suffix
honorificSuffix.description: Honorific or post-nominal letters following the name, used in forms of address. Distinct from the name itself.

jobTitle.title: Job Title
jobTitle.description: The person's job title. Useful for credit lines and attribution.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the person, distinct from any role-specific or contextual description applied at the party wrapper level.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email addresses associated with the person. Each entry carries a `name` label and an `address` value so producers can describe work, personal, support, and similar contexts without ambiguity.

name.title: Name
name.description: Label describing the email, such as `work`, `personal`, `support`, or `security`.

phone.title: Phone
phone.description: Phone numbers associated with the person. Each entry carries a `name` label and a `number` value. Numbers should be expressed in E.164 form where possible.

name.title: Name
name.description: Label describing the phone, such as `office`, `mobile`, `fax`, or `pager`.

url.title: URLs
url.description: URLs associated with the person. Each entry carries a `name` label and a `url` value so producers can describe homepage, social profiles, code repositories, and similar without ambiguity.

name.title: Name
name.description: Label describing the URL, such as `homepage`, `github`, `linkedin`, `mastodon`, or `orcid`.

affiliation.title: Affiliation
affiliation.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to an organization party with which the person is affiliated.

system.title: System Identity
system.description: Identity attributes for a party that is a software system, hardware system, service account, automation, or autonomous agent.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: The kind of system the party represents. May be a predefined kind from the CycloneDX system-kind taxonomy expressed as a plain string, or a custom kind expressed as an object with `name` and optional `description`.

meta:enum.software-system: Application, service, or platform that performs actions as itself.
meta:enum.hardware-system: Physical device or appliance that performs actions. Includes vehicles, drones, satellites, medical devices, network equipment, and industrial controllers unless a more specific kind applies.
meta:enum.service-account: Non-human identity used by automation to authenticate.
meta:enum.machine-identity: Cryptographic identity such as a certificate principal or workload identity.
meta:enum.automation: Pipeline, job, or scheduled task that performs actions deterministically.
meta:enum.agent: Autonomous or semi-autonomous agent that can plan and execute. Includes AI agents.
meta:enum.bot: Scripted automation that interacts with an interface. Includes chatbots, robotic process automation bots, and scraping bots.
meta:enum.oracle: External data feed or oracle, including blockchain oracles that bridge off-chain data into on-chain systems.
meta:enum.device: Physical end user device such as a phone or IoT device acting as a party in its own right.
meta:enum.robot: Physical robot or autonomous mechanical system. For software-only counterparts, see `bot`, `agent`, or `automation`.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom kind.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom kind.

ref.title: Reference
ref.description: Reference to any bom-ref'd object that represents this system. The referenced object provides the underlying identity (component, service, blueprint asset, or other object type). Use to associate the party with its concrete representation declared elsewhere.

identifiers.title: Identifiers
identifiers.description: Machine identifiers for the system, such as service principal, workload identity, certificate subject, or device serial.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Capabilities or duties the system possesses.

persona.title: Persona Identity
persona.description: Identity attributes for a party that is an abstract archetype rather than a specific named instance. Use for generic users, generic suppliers, or hypothesized attackers when a specific party is intentionally not named. When the specific instance is known (for example a tracked APT group), use `organization` instead.

description.title: Description
description.description: Free-text description of the persona instance. Use to refine the archetype with details relevant to the threat model, such as the persona's assumed context, history, or behavioral pattern.
examples: Disgruntled administrator with elevated access to the payment gateway and motive driven by recent performance review.

archetype.title: Archetype
archetype.description: The persona archetype. May be a predefined archetype from the CycloneDX persona-archetype taxonomy expressed as a plain string, or a custom archetype expressed as an object with `name` and optional `description`.

meta:enum.end-user: Typical end user of the system.
meta:enum.power-user: Advanced user with higher than average privileges.
meta:enum.external: Generic external party without an account or operational relationship.
meta:enum.anonymous: Unauthenticated visitor interacting with the system.
meta:enum.customer: Customer of the organization that owns the subject.
meta:enum.partner: Business partner with a defined relationship.
meta:enum.supplier: Generic supplier providing goods or services on a transactional basis.
meta:enum.vendor: Vendor delivering and operating goods or services with operational access to the subject.
meta:enum.contractor: Third party with operational access under contract.
meta:enum.third-party: Generic third party that does not fit partner, supplier, vendor, or contractor.
meta:enum.researcher: Security researcher, bug bounty hunter, or academic. Authorized probing party.
meta:enum.regulator: Government or industry regulator with administrative authority over the subject.
meta:enum.law-enforcement: Law enforcement entity with investigative authority.
meta:enum.insider-threat: Hostile or negligent party with legitimate access.
meta:enum.hacktivist: Hostile party motivated by ideology or activism.
meta:enum.nation-state: Hostile party sponsored or directed by a national government.
meta:enum.organized-crime: Hostile party operating as part of an organized criminal enterprise.
meta:enum.public: General public not in direct interaction with the subject.

meta:enum.internal: Persona is internal to the organization that owns the subject.
meta:enum.external: Persona is external to the organization that owns the subject.
meta:enum.mixed: Persona may appear in either internal or external contexts.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Capabilities the persona is assumed to hold.

assumedPosture.title: Assumed Security Posture
assumedPosture.description: Assumed security posture of the persona in this context.

partyRelations.title: Party Relations
partyRelations.description: Links from this party to other parties. Captures hierarchical, organizational, and delegation relationships. Order ranking is on the role itself (`role.order`), not here.

parent.title: Parent
parent.description: Reference to a parent party. Models organizational hierarchy, group membership, and corporate parent or subsidiary relationships.

delegatedBy.title: Delegated By
delegatedBy.description: Reference to another party that delegated authority to this one. Models situations such as an autonomous agent acting on behalf of a human user, a contractor acting on behalf of a principal, or a service account assuming a role.

identifier.title: Identifier
identifier.description: An identifier issued by a recognized authority. Includes legal and registration identifiers as well as machine and workload identities.

scheme.title: Scheme
scheme.description: The identifier scheme. May be a predefined scheme from the CycloneDX identifier-scheme taxonomy expressed as a plain string, or a custom scheme expressed as an object with `name` and optional `description` and `url`.

meta:enum.ncage: NATO Commercial and Government Entity code.
meta:enum.cage: United States Commercial and Government Entity code.
meta:enum.eori: Economic Operator Registration and Identification number used in European Union customs.
meta:enum.gst: Goods and Services Tax registration number.
meta:enum.ruc: Registro Unico de Contribuyentes used in several Latin American countries.
meta:enum.sec-cik: United States Securities and Exchange Commission Central Index Key.
meta:enum.isin: International Securities Identification Number per ISO 6166.
meta:enum.gtin: GS1 Global Trade Item Number when the party is also a registered trade entity.
meta:enum.iso6523: ISO 6523 organization identifier. The value should encode the four-digit International Code Designator (ICD) followed by the organization identifier per the registry referenced by the ICD.
meta:enum.ofac-sdn: United States Office of Foreign Assets Control Specially Designated Nationals list identifier.
meta:enum.un-lm: United Nations Locode for a specific facility or jurisdiction.
meta:enum.oidc-sub: OpenID Connect subject identifier for a machine or service identity.
meta:enum.spiffe: SPIFFE ID for a workload identity.
meta:enum.did: W3C Decentralized Identifier (DID) per https://www.w3.org/TR/did-core/. Common for autonomous agents, federated services, and verifiable-credential subjects.
meta:enum.vc-id: W3C Verifiable Credential identifier per https://www.w3.org/TR/vc-data-model/.

url.title: URL
url.description: URL of the scheme registry or specification.

schemeVersion.title: Scheme Version
schemeVersion.description: The version of the scheme that issued this identifier, if applicable.

issuer.title: Issuer
issuer.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party that issued the identifier.

postalAddress.title: Postal Address
postalAddress.description: An address used to identify a contactable or operational location.

bom-ref.title: Reference Identifier
bom-ref.description: An optional identifier that can be used to reference the address from elsewhere. Every bom-ref shall be unique within the containing instance. The value should not start with the prefix 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Link identifiers.

country.title: Country
country.description: The country name or the two-letter ISO 3166-1 country code. When the isoCode property is present, the value of isoCode is authoritative.

region.title: Region
region.description: The region or state in the country. When the isoCode property carries an ISO 3166-2 subdivision, the value of isoCode is authoritative.

isoCode.title: ISO Code
isoCode.description: An ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, optionally followed by an ISO 3166-2 subdivision code separated by a hyphen. When present, this property is authoritative over the free-text country and region properties.

locality.title: Locality
locality.description: The locality or city within the country.

streetAddress.title: Street Address
streetAddress.description: The street address. Multi-line addresses are expressed as a single string with line breaks (`\n`) between lines. Implementations and serialization formats are not required to preserve the order of elements in an array, so a multi-line address shall not be modeled as an array.

latitude.title: Latitude
latitude.description: Latitude in decimal degrees. Four or more decimal places are recommended for facility-level precision.

longitude.title: Longitude
longitude.description: Longitude in decimal degrees. Four or more decimal places are recommended for facility-level precision.

altitude.title: Altitude
altitude.description: Altitude in meters above the reference ellipsoid identified by the datum property.

datum.title: Datum
datum.description: The geodetic datum used for the coordinates. WGS84 is assumed when omitted.

patents.title: Patents
patents.description: The list of either individual patents or patent families.

patent.title: Patent
patent.description: A patent is a legal instrument, granted by an authority, that confers certain rights over an invention for a specified period, contingent on public disclosure and adherence to relevant legal requirements. The summary information in this object is aligned with [WIPO ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/en/st96/) principles where applicable.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

patentNumber.title: Patent Number
patentNumber.description: The unique number assigned to the granted patent by the issuing authority. Aligned with `PatentNumber` in WIPO ST.96. Refer to [PatentNumber in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/PatentNumber.xsd).

publicationNumber.title: Patent Publication Number
publicationNumber.description:
This is the number assigned to a patent application once it is published. Patent applications are generally published 18 months after filing (unless an applicant requests non-publication). This number is distinct from the application number. 

Purpose: Identifies the publicly available version of the application. 

Format: Varies by jurisdiction, often similar to application numbers but includes an additional suffix indicating publication. 

Example:
 - US: US20240000123A1 (indicates the first publication of application US20240000123) 
 - Europe: EP23123456A1 (first publication of European application EP23123456). 

WIPO ST.96 v8.0: 
 - Publication Number field: https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/PublicationNumber.xsd

title.title: Patent Title
title.description: The title of the patent, summarising the invention it protects. Aligned with `InventionTitle` in WIPO ST.96. Refer to [InventionTitle in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/InventionTitle.xsd).

abstract.title: Patent Abstract
abstract.description: A brief summary of the invention described in the patent. Aligned with `Abstract` and `P` in WIPO ST.96. Refer to [Abstract in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/Abstract.xsd).

filingDate.title: Filing Date
filingDate.description: The date the patent application was filed with the jurisdiction. Aligned with `FilingDate` in WIPO ST.96. Refer to [FilingDate in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/FilingDate.xsd).

grantDate.title: Grant Date
grantDate.description: The date the patent was granted by the jurisdiction. Aligned with `GrantDate` in WIPO ST.96. Refer to [GrantDate in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/GrantDate.xsd).

patentExpirationDate.title: Expiration Date
patentExpirationDate.description: The date the patent expires. Derived from grant or filing date according to jurisdiction-specific rules.

patentLegalStatus.title: Legal Status
patentLegalStatus.description: Indicates the current legal status of the patent or patent application, based on the WIPO ST.27 standard. This status reflects administrative, procedural, or legal events. Values include both active and inactive states and are useful for determining enforceability, procedural history, and maintenance status.

meta:enum.pending: The patent application has been filed but not yet examined or granted.
meta:enum.granted: The patent application has been examined and a patent has been issued.
meta:enum.revoked: The patent has been declared invalid through a legal or administrative process.
meta:enum.expired: The patent has reached the end of its enforceable term.
meta:enum.lapsed: The patent is no longer in force due to non-payment of maintenance fees or other requirements.
meta:enum.withdrawn: The patent application was voluntarily withdrawn by the applicant.
meta:enum.abandoned: The patent application was abandoned, often due to lack of action or response.
meta:enum.suspended: Processing of the patent application has been temporarily halted.
meta:enum.reinstated: A previously abandoned or lapsed patent has been reinstated.
meta:enum.opposed: The patent application or granted patent is under formal opposition proceedings.
meta:enum.terminated: The patent or application has been officially terminated.
meta:enum.invalidated: The patent has been invalidated, either in part or in full.
meta:enum.in-force: The granted patent is active and enforceable.

patentAssignee.title: Patent Assignees
patentAssignee.description: A collection of organisations or individuals to whom the patent rights are assigned. This supports joint ownership and allows for flexible representation of both corporate entities and individual inventors.

patentFamily.title: Patent Family
patentFamily.description: A patent family is a group of related patent applications or granted patents that cover the same or similar invention. These patents are filed in multiple jurisdictions to protect the invention across different regions or countries. A patent family typically includes patents that share a common priority date, originating from the same initial application, and may vary slightly in scope or claims to comply with regional legal frameworks. Fields align with WIPO ST.96 standards where applicable.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM. 

For a patent, it might be a good idea to use a patent number as the BOM reference ID.

familyId.title: Patent Family ID
familyId.description: The unique identifier for the patent family, aligned with the `id` attribute in WIPO ST.96 v8.0's `PatentFamilyType`. Refer to [PatentFamilyType in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/PatentFamilyType.xsd).

members.title: Family Members
members.description: A collection of patents or applications that belong to this family, each identified by a `bom-ref` pointing to a patent object defined elsewhere in the BOM.

items.title: BOM Reference
items.description: A `bom-ref` linking to a patent or application object within the BOM.

patentAssertions.title: Patent Assertions
patentAssertions.description: A list of assertions made regarding patents associated with this component or service. Assertions distinguish between ownership, licensing, and other relevant interactions with patents.

items.title: Patent Assertion
items.description: An assertion linking a patent or patent family to this component or service.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: A reference to the patent or patent family object within the BOM. This must match the `bom-ref` of a `patent` or `patentFamily` object.

assertionType.title: Assertion Type
assertionType.description: The type of assertion being made about the patent or patent family. Examples include ownership, licensing, and standards inclusion.

meta:enum.ownership: The manufacturer asserts ownership of the patent or patent family.
meta:enum.license: The manufacturer asserts they have a license to use the patent or patent family.
meta:enum.third-party-claim: A third party has asserted a claim or potential infringement against the manufacturer’s component or service.
meta:enum.standards-inclusion: The patent is part of a standard essential patent (SEP) portfolio relevant to the component or service.
meta:enum.prior-art: The manufacturer asserts the patent or patent family as prior art that invalidates another patent or claim.
meta:enum.exclusive-rights: The manufacturer asserts exclusive rights granted through a licensing agreement.
meta:enum.non-assertion: The manufacturer asserts they will not enforce the patent or patent family against certain uses or users.
meta:enum.research-or-evaluation: The patent or patent family is being used under a research or evaluation license.

patentRefs.title: Patent References
patentRefs.description: A list of BOM references (`bom-ref`) linking to patents or patent families associated with this assertion.

oneOf.title: Reference
oneOf.description: A reference to a previously defined `organizationalContact` or `organizationalEntity` object in the BOM. The value must be a valid `bom-ref` pointing to one of these objects.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Additional notes or clarifications regarding the assertion, if necessary. For example, geographical restrictions, duration, or limitations of a license.

patentApplicationNumber.title: Patent Application Number
patentApplicationNumber.description: The unique number assigned to a patent application when it is filed with a patent office. It is used to identify the specific application and track its progress through the examination process. Aligned with `ApplicationNumber` in ST.96. Refer to [ApplicationIdentificationType in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/ApplicationIdentificationType.xsd).

patentJurisdiction.title: Jurisdiction
patentJurisdiction.description: The jurisdiction or patent office where the priority application was filed, specified using WIPO ST.3 codes. Aligned with `IPOfficeCode` in ST.96. Refer to [IPOfficeCode in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Common/IPOfficeCode.xsd).

patentFilingDate.title: Filing Date
patentFilingDate.description: The date the priority application was filed, aligned with `FilingDate` in ST.96. Refer to [FilingDate in ST.96](https://www.wipo.int/standards/XMLSchema/ST96/V8_0/Patent/FilingDate.xsd).

priorityApplication.title: Priority Application
priorityApplication.description: The priorityApplication contains the essential data necessary to identify and reference an earlier patent filing for priority rights. In line with WIPO ST.96 guidelines, it includes the jurisdiction (office code), application number, and filing date-the three key elements that uniquely specify the priority application in a global patent context.

perspectives.title: Perspectives
perspectives.description: Defines domain-specific views into the document, enabling different audiences to interpret and navigate the data through their own conceptual lens. Each perspective identifies relevant data types and may provide domain-specific terminology mappings.

perspective.title: Perspective
perspective.description: A domain-specific view that identifies the types of data relevant to a particular audience and provides optional terminology mappings to facilitate interpretation. Perspectives enable tooling to generate filtered views, translate terminology, and validate document completeness against audience-specific requirements.

name.title: Perspective Name
name.description: The name of the perspective, typically indicating the target audience or domain.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the perspective, its intended audience, and the use cases it supports.

domains.title: Domains
domains.description: The domains or disciplines to which this perspective applies. Allows selection of multiple pre-defined domains, custom domains, or a combination of both.

mappings.title: Data Type Mappings
mappings.description: An array of mappings that identify the types of data relevant to this perspective using JSON path expressions. Each mapping may include domain-specific terminology.

perspectiveMapping.title: Perspective Mapping
perspectiveMapping.description: Maps a JSON path expression to domain-specific terminology, enabling audiences to interpret CycloneDX data using familiar nomenclature. Each mapping identifies a data type within the document structure and may provide alternative names and descriptions used by the target audience.

expression.title: Path Expression
expression.description: A [JSONPath](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9535) expression that identifies the types of data relevant to this perspective.

nativeName.title: Native Name
nativeName.description: The domain-specific term used by the target audience to describe the data identified by the expression.

nativeDescription.title: Native Description
nativeDescription.description: A domain-specific description of the data type, explaining its significance within the context of the perspective.

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: Describes the importance of a field in relation to other fields. Values shall be between 0.0 and 1.0. This allows for relative ranking of mappings, where higher values indicate greater importance.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: Explains why this data type is relevant to the perspective and how it should be interpreted or used.

perspectiveRelevance.title: Perspective Relevance
perspectiveRelevance.description: Indicates the importance of a data type to a perspective.

meta:enum.required: This data type is essential for the perspective and should always be present.
meta:enum.recommended: This data type is highly relevant and should be included when available.
meta:enum.optional: This data type provides supplementary information that may be useful.
meta:enum.informative: This data type provides background context but is not directly actionable.

perspectiveDomainChoice.title: Perspective Domain Choice
perspectiveDomainChoice.description: Allows selection of a pre-defined domain or specification of a custom domain.

name.title: Domain Name
name.description: The name of the custom domain.

description.title: Domain Description
