description.description: A description of the custom domain, its scope, and the concerns it addresses.

preDefinedPerspectiveDomain.title: Pre-Defined Perspective Domain
preDefinedPerspectiveDomain.description: A pre-defined domain or discipline to which a perspective applies. Domains span security disciplines, safety engineering, industry verticals, technology areas, compliance frameworks, and cross-cutting concerns.

meta:enum.application-security: Security concerns related to software applications, including vulnerabilities, secure coding practices, input validation, and dependency management.
meta:enum.cloud-security: Security concerns specific to cloud computing environments, including configuration, identity management, data protection, and shared responsibility models.
meta:enum.container-security: Security concerns related to containerised applications, including image provenance, runtime protection, orchestration security, and supply chain integrity.
meta:enum.cryptographic-security: Concerns related to cryptographic implementations, algorithm selection, key management, certificate handling, and post-quantum cryptography readiness.
meta:enum.cyber-security: Broad security concerns encompassing threat detection, incident response, security operations, and defence-in-depth strategies.
meta:enum.data-security: Concerns related to protecting data at rest, in transit, and in use, including encryption, access controls, and data loss prevention.
meta:enum.endpoint-security: Security concerns for end-user devices including workstations, mobile devices, and peripherals, encompassing malware protection and device management.
meta:enum.firmware-security: Security concerns specific to firmware, including secure boot, firmware updates, integrity verification, and protection against persistent threats.
meta:enum.hardware-security: Security concerns related to physical hardware, including trusted platform modules, hardware security modules, side-channel attacks, and tamper resistance.
meta:enum.identity-access-management: Concerns related to authentication, authorisation, identity lifecycle management, privileged access, and zero-trust architectures.
meta:enum.network-security: Security concerns related to network infrastructure, including firewalls, intrusion detection, segmentation, and secure communications.
meta:enum.operational-security: Security concerns related to the deployment, configuration, and day-to-day operation of systems in production environments.
meta:enum.physical-security: Security concerns related to physical access controls, environmental protections, surveillance, and tamper evidence.
meta:enum.supply-chain-security: Concerns related to the security, integrity, and trustworthiness of components, vendors, and processes throughout the supply chain.
meta:enum.web-security: Security concerns specific to web applications and services, including OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, API security, and client-side protections.
meta:enum.automotive-safety: Safety concerns for automotive systems aligned with ISO 26262, including hazard analysis, functional safety requirements, and ASIL classifications.
meta:enum.aviation-safety: Safety concerns for aviation systems aligned with DO-178C, DO-254, and DO-326A, including design assurance levels and airworthiness requirements.
meta:enum.environmental-safety: Concerns related to environmental protection, hazardous materials handling, emissions, and ecological impact assessment.
meta:enum.functional-safety: Safety concerns aligned with IEC 61508 and derivative standards, focusing on systematic failures, safety integrity levels, and risk reduction.
meta:enum.machinery-safety: Safety concerns for industrial machinery aligned with ISO 12100 and IEC 62443, including safeguarding, emergency stops, and risk assessment.
meta:enum.maritime-safety: Safety concerns for maritime and offshore systems, including vessel automation, navigation systems, and compliance with maritime regulations.
meta:enum.nuclear-safety: Safety concerns for nuclear facilities and systems, including defence-in-depth, safety classification, and regulatory compliance with nuclear standards.
meta:enum.patient-safety: Safety concerns for medical devices and healthcare systems focusing on preventing harm to patients, including adverse event prevention and clinical risk management.
meta:enum.process-safety: Safety concerns for industrial processes involving hazardous materials, including process hazard analysis, safety instrumented systems, and layers of protection.
meta:enum.railway-safety: Safety concerns for railway systems aligned with EN 50126, EN 50128, and EN 50129, including signalling, train control, and safety cases.
meta:enum.aerospace-defense: Concerns specific to aerospace and defence industries, including mission-critical systems, military standards, and classified information handling.
meta:enum.automotive: Industry-specific concerns for automotive manufacturers and suppliers, including ISO/SAE 21434 cyber security and vehicle type approval.
meta:enum.building-automation: Concerns related to building management systems, HVAC controls, access systems, and smart building infrastructure.
meta:enum.consumer-electronics: Concerns specific to consumer electronic devices, including product safety, electromagnetic compatibility, and consumer protection regulations.
meta:enum.critical-infrastructure: Concerns related to systems designated as critical infrastructure, including sector-specific requirements and national security considerations.
meta:enum.education: Concerns specific to educational institutions and EdTech, including student data protection, FERPA compliance, and academic integrity.
meta:enum.energy-utilities: Concerns specific to energy and utility sectors, including NERC CIP compliance, grid security, and operational continuity.
meta:enum.financial-services: Concerns specific to financial institutions, including PCI DSS, SOX compliance, fraud prevention, and transaction integrity.
meta:enum.government: Concerns specific to government agencies and contractors, including FedRAMP, FISMA, and public sector procurement requirements.
meta:enum.healthcare: Concerns specific to healthcare organisations, including HIPAA compliance, electronic health records, and healthcare interoperability.
meta:enum.insurance: Concerns specific to insurance industry, including actuarial data integrity, claims processing security, and regulatory compliance.
meta:enum.legal: Concerns specific to legal industry, including attorney-client privilege, e-discovery, and legal hold requirements.
meta:enum.manufacturing: Concerns specific to manufacturing environments, including production systems, quality management, and shop floor security.
meta:enum.media-entertainment: Concerns specific to media and entertainment, including digital rights management, content protection, and broadcast systems.
meta:enum.pharmaceuticals: Concerns specific to pharmaceutical industry, including FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GxP compliance, and drug safety systems.
meta:enum.retail: Concerns specific to retail industry, including point-of-sale security, e-commerce platforms, and customer data protection.
meta:enum.telecommunications: Concerns specific to telecommunications providers, including network infrastructure, subscriber data, and regulatory compliance.
meta:enum.transportation: Concerns related to transportation systems beyond automotive and rail, including logistics, fleet management, and traffic control systems.
meta:enum.artificial-intelligence: Concerns related to AI systems, including model transparency, algorithmic bias, explainability, and ethical considerations.
meta:enum.edge-computing: Concerns specific to edge computing deployments, including distributed processing, local data handling, and remote device management.
meta:enum.embedded-systems: Concerns specific to embedded systems development, including real-time constraints, resource limitations, and hardware-software integration.
meta:enum.industrial-control-systems: Concerns specific to ICS environments, including PLCs, DCS, and industrial automation security aligned with IEC 62443.
meta:enum.industrial-iot: Concerns specific to Industrial Internet of Things deployments, combining IT security with OT requirements and industrial protocols.
meta:enum.internet-of-things: Concerns related to IoT devices and ecosystems, including device security, communication protocols, and fleet management.
meta:enum.machine-learning: Concerns specific to ML models, including training data provenance, model lineage, performance metrics, and reproducibility.
meta:enum.medical-devices: Concerns specific to medical device development aligned with IEC 62304, FDA guidance, and MDR/IVDR requirements.
meta:enum.mobile: Concerns specific to mobile applications and devices, including app store requirements, mobile-specific vulnerabilities, and device management.
meta:enum.operational-technology: Concerns specific to OT environments, bridging IT and industrial systems with focus on availability, safety, and legacy system integration.
meta:enum.robotics: Concerns specific to robotic systems, including autonomous operation, human-robot interaction safety, and motion control security.
meta:enum.scada: Concerns specific to Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition systems, including remote monitoring, control system security, and protocol vulnerabilities.
meta:enum.smart-grid: Concerns specific to smart grid infrastructure, including advanced metering, grid automation, and distributed energy resource management.
meta:enum.data-protection: Concerns related to data protection regulations such as GDPR, CCPA, and cross-border data transfer requirements.
meta:enum.export-control: Concerns related to export control regulations, including EAR, ITAR, and dual-use technology restrictions.
meta:enum.intellectual-property: Concerns related to patents, trade secrets, copyright, and other intellectual property rights and protections.
meta:enum.legal-compliance: Regulatory and legal requirements, including contractual obligations, liability considerations, and legal holds.
meta:enum.licensing: Concerns related to software licensing, licence compatibility, open source obligations, and commercial licensing terms.
meta:enum.privacy: Concerns related to personal data protection, privacy by design, consent management, and individual rights.
meta:enum.regulatory-compliance: Concerns related to compliance with industry-specific regulations, standards, and certification requirements.
meta:enum.maintainability: Concerns related to the ease of maintaining, updating, and supporting software and systems over their lifecycle.
meta:enum.performance: Concerns related to system performance, scalability, response times, and resource utilisation.
meta:enum.quality-assurance: Concerns related to testing, validation, verification, and overall quality metrics and processes.
meta:enum.reliability: Concerns related to system reliability, fault tolerance, mean time between failures, and degradation modes.
meta:enum.testability: Concerns related to the ability to test components and systems, including test coverage, test automation, and test data management.
meta:enum.incident-response: Concerns related to detecting, responding to, and recovering from security incidents and breaches.
meta:enum.penetration-testing: Concerns related to authorised security testing, vulnerability assessment, and security validation activities.
meta:enum.red-team: Concerns related to adversarial simulation, attack path analysis, and security control effectiveness testing.
meta:enum.risk-management: Concerns related to risk identification, assessment, treatment, and ongoing risk monitoring.
meta:enum.threat-intelligence: Concerns related to threat actor analysis, indicators of compromise, and proactive threat hunting.
meta:enum.threat-modeling: Concerns related to identifying weaknesses, threats, countermeasures, attack surfaces, and trust boundaries.
meta:enum.vulnerability-management: Concerns related to vulnerability identification, prioritisation, remediation tracking, and disclosure processes.
meta:enum.accessibility: Concerns related to ensuring systems are usable by people with disabilities, including WCAG compliance.
meta:enum.ethics: Concerns related to ethical considerations in technology development, including algorithmic fairness and responsible innovation.
meta:enum.human-factors: Concerns related to human-system interaction, usability, operator error prevention, and ergonomic design.
meta:enum.interoperability: Concerns related to the ability of systems to exchange and use information effectively across boundaries.
meta:enum.resilience: Concerns related to system resilience, business continuity, disaster recovery, and graceful degradation.
meta:enum.sustainability: Concerns related to environmental sustainability, energy efficiency, and responsible resource consumption.
meta:enum.transparency: Concerns related to openness, auditability, and the ability for stakeholders to understand system behaviour and composition.

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.description: A registry of reusable, named characterizations of how a subject behaves or is governed within a domain. Profiles separate characterization from identity: identity describes who or what the subject is; the profile describes the durable attributes that characterize it. A profile is referenced by its bom-ref from the elements it characterizes, so a single profile can be reused by many subjects.

type.title: Type
type.description: The software versioning type the release note describes.

featuredImage.title: Featured image
featuredImage.description: The URL to an image that may be prominently displayed with the release note.

socialImage.title: Social image
socialImage.description: The URL to an image that may be used in messaging on social media platforms.

description.title: Description
description.description: A short description of the release.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the release note was created.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: One or more alternate names the release may be referred to. This may include unofficial terms used by development and marketing teams (e.g. code names).

resolves.title: Resolves
resolves.description: A collection of issues that have been resolved.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Zero or more release notes containing the locale and content. Multiple note objects may be specified to support release notes in a wide variety of languages.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Provides the ability to document properties in a name-value store. This provides flexibility to include data not officially supported in the standard without having to use additional namespaces or create extensions. Unlike key-value stores, properties support duplicate names, each potentially having different values. Property names of interest to the general public are encouraged to be registered in the [CycloneDX Property Taxonomy](https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-property-taxonomy). Formal registration is optional.

releaseType.description:
The software versioning type. It is recommended that the release type use one of 'major', 'minor', 'patch', 'pre-release', or 'internal'. Representing all possible software release types is not practical, so standardizing on the recommended values, whenever possible, is strongly encouraged.

* __major__ = A major release may contain significant changes or may introduce breaking changes.
* __minor__ = A minor release, also known as an update, may contain a smaller number of changes than major releases.
* __patch__ = Patch releases are typically unplanned and may resolve defects or important security issues.
* __pre-release__ = A pre-release may include alpha, beta, or release candidates and typically have limited support. They provide the ability to preview a release prior to its general availability.
* __internal__ = Internal releases are not for public consumption and are intended to be used exclusively by the project or manufacturer that produced it.

note.title: Note
note.description: A note containing the locale and content.

locale.title: Locale
locale.description: The ISO-639 (or higher) language code and optional ISO-3166 (or higher) country code. Examples include: "en", "en-US", "fr" and "fr-CA"

text.title: Release note content
text.description: Specifies the full content of the release note.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.description: A collection of requirements representing specific needs, constraints, or capabilities that must be met.

requirement.title: Requirement
requirement.description: A requirement represents a specific need, constraint, or capability that must be met.

id.title: Identifier
id.description: A unique identifier for the requirement, often used in requirement management systems (e.g., REQ-001).

name.title: Name
name.description: A short name or title for the requirement.

description.title: Description
description.description: A detailed description of the requirement.

type.title: Requirement Types
type.description: The categories or classifications applicable to the requirement.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description: The priority level of the requirement.

stakeholders.title: Stakeholders
stakeholders.description: The individuals or groups who have an interest in the requirement.

acceptanceCriteria.title: Acceptance Criteria
acceptanceCriteria.description: Criteria that must be met for the requirement to be considered satisfied.

dependencies.title: Dependencies
dependencies.description: Other requirements that this requirement depends on.

parent.title: Parent BOM Reference
parent.description: Optional reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a parent requirement. Establishes a hierarchy of requirements.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: The reasoning or justification for the requirement.

fitCriterion.title: Fit Criterion
fitCriterion.description: A measurable test that the solution must pass to be acceptable to the stakeholders.

effort.title: Effort Estimation
effort.description: An estimation of the effort required to implement the requirement.

risk.title: Risk
risk.description: The risk level associated with implementing or not implementing the requirement.

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: Files or documents attached to the requirement.

requirementType.title: Requirement Type
requirementType.description: The category or classification applicable to the requirement.

meta:enum.accessibility: Requirements ensuring inclusive access for people with disabilities or impairments.
meta:enum.auditability: Requirements defining the capability of a system to record activities for review and compliance purposes.
meta:enum.availability: Requirements describing the uptime and operational availability of systems or services.
meta:enum.business: Requirements derived from organizational goals, strategies, or business processes.
meta:enum.compatibility: Requirements ensuring the software or system functions correctly with other systems or legacy components.
meta:enum.compliance: Requirements ensuring adherence to internal policies, industry standards, or external agreements beyond regulatory mandates.
meta:enum.deployment: Requirements related to deploying, configuring, and operating the system in production environments.
meta:enum.functional: Requirements specifying the fundamental behaviors and capabilities of the system or application.
meta:enum.interoperability: Requirements addressing the ability of the system to interact seamlessly with external systems or products.
meta:enum.localization: Requirements specifying adaptations for specific languages, cultures, or regional needs.
meta:enum.maintainability: Requirements aimed at ensuring the ease of performing maintenance, enhancements, or repairs.
meta:enum.non-functional: Requirements that define constraints or qualities such as performance, usability, reliability, etc., rather than specific functionality.
meta:enum.other: Requirements that do not clearly fit into other specified categories.
meta:enum.performance: Requirements specifying response times, throughput, or other efficiency-related aspects.
meta:enum.portability: Requirements enabling the system or components to be transferred easily across various environments or platforms.
meta:enum.privacy: Requirements specifically addressing the handling and protection of personal and sensitive data.
meta:enum.regulatory: Requirements necessary for legal compliance, dictated by laws, regulations, or standards.
meta:enum.reliability: Requirements ensuring the system consistently performs under specified conditions and duration.
meta:enum.scalability: Requirements focused on the ability of the system to grow and manage increased demand effectively.
meta:enum.security: Requirements intended to protect systems, data, and users from malicious actions or threats.
meta:enum.system: Technical requirements defining infrastructure, architecture, or underlying technologies.
meta:enum.testability: Requirements ensuring the system can be effectively validated and tested.
meta:enum.usability: Requirements addressing the ease of use, intuitiveness, and overall user experience.
meta:enum.user: Requirements captured directly from user needs, tasks, and expectations.

requirementStatus.title: Status
requirementStatus.description: The current status of the requirement in the development lifecycle.

acceptanceCriterion.title: Acceptance Criterion
acceptanceCriterion.description: A specific condition that must be met for the requirement to be considered satisfied.

id.title: Identifier
id.description: A unique identifier for the acceptance criterion.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the acceptance criterion.

status.title: Status
status.description: The current status of the acceptance criterion.

type.title: Dependency Type
type.description: The type of dependency relationship.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the dependency relationship.

requirementAssertions.title: Requirement Assertions
requirementAssertions.description: A list of assertions describing how a component relates to specific requirements.

items.title: Requirement Assertion
items.description: An assertion linking one or more requirements to a component, specifying the nature of the relationship.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the assertion elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

assertionType.title: Assertion Type
assertionType.description: The type of assertion being made about the relationship between the component and the requirement.

meta:enum.conflicts: The component is in tension with the requirement but a definitive determination of violation has not been made. This may indicate partial incompatibility, competing constraints, or an unresolved evaluation.
meta:enum.not-applicable: The requirement does not pertain to this component. The evaluator has reviewed the requirement and determined it is irrelevant.
meta:enum.not-assessed: The relationship between the component and the requirement has not yet been evaluated.
meta:enum.other: A relationship that does not fit into the other predefined assertion types.
meta:enum.partially-satisfies: The component partially meets the requirement but does not fully satisfy all of its conditions or acceptance criteria.
meta:enum.satisfies: The component fully satisfies the requirement, meeting all of its conditions and acceptance criteria.
meta:enum.violates: The component has been evaluated and conclusively determined to not meet the requirement, breaching one or more of its conditions or acceptance criteria.

requirementRefs.title: Requirement References
requirementRefs.description: A list of BOM references linking to requirement objects defined in the BOM.

description.title: Description
description.description: Additional context or clarification regarding the assertion.

risks.title: Risks
risks.description: A collection of risks identified through risk analysis, together with the assessments that evaluate them. The model is framework-neutral and can document risk regardless of the risk management framework in use, including [ISO 31000](https://www.iso.org/iso-31000-risk-management.html), [NIST SP 800-30](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/30/r1/final), the [NIST AI Risk Management Framework](https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework), [COSO ERM](https://www.coso.org/), [ISO/IEC 27005](https://www.iso.org/standard/80585.html), and OCTAVE. It accommodates qualitative, semi-quantitative, and quantitative assessment, and each rating records the scoring methodology used, such as [FAIR](https://www.fairinstitute.org/), [OWASP Risk Rating](https://owasp.org/www-community/OWASP_Risk_Rating_Methodology), DREAD, [FMEA](https://asq.org/quality-resources/fmea), NIST SP 800-30, or OCTAVE Allegro. Each risk separates inherent, residual, and target ratings so risk reduction is explicit.

risks.title: Risks
risks.description: The documented risks identified through risk analysis.

assessments.title: Assessments
assessments.description: Point-in-time risk assessment events, each evaluating one or more of the documented risks.

riskAppetites.title: Risk Appetites
riskAppetites.description: The risk appetites that apply to this collection. Multiple entries support a document covering more than one party.

type.title: Type
type.description: The domain or category of risk. Use the custom option for domains specific to an organization's risk taxonomy.

meta:enum.financial: Financial loss and economic impact risks.
meta:enum.safety: Human safety and physical security risks.
meta:enum.ethical: Ethical risks, including the responsible use of automated decision-making and artificial intelligence.
meta:enum.societal: Risks to society, communities, or democratic and civic processes.
meta:enum.human-rights: Risks to fundamental human rights, including discrimination and loss of autonomy.
meta:enum.health: Risks to physical or mental health and wellbeing.
meta:enum.legal: Legal exposure, including liability and contractual risks.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom risk domain.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom risk domain.
Additional context for this risk domain.

riskAttribute.title: Risk Attribute
riskAttribute.description: A security or privacy attribute representing a potential impact area when a risk is realized. Use the custom option for attributes specific to an organization's risk taxonomy.

meta:enum.accountability: Assigning responsibility for actions and decisions to individuals or entities.
meta:enum.authentication: Verifying the identity of users, devices, or systems before granting access.
meta:enum.authorization: Granting permissions based on identity and roles to perform specific actions or access resources.
meta:enum.authenticity: Ensuring that data, communications, or entities are genuine and can be verified.
meta:enum.availability: Ensuring systems, data, and services are accessible and operational when needed.
meta:enum.compliance: Adherence to applicable laws, regulations, policies, and standards.
meta:enum.confidentiality: Preventing unauthorized access to or disclosure of information.
meta:enum.connectivity: Maintaining secure and reliable communication between systems or components.
meta:enum.control: Enabling individuals to manage how their personal data is used and shared.
meta:enum.data-subject-rights: Guaranteeing individuals' rights to access, correct, delete, or restrict their personal data.
meta:enum.integrity: Ensuring information is accurate, complete, and unaltered by unauthorized actors.
meta:enum.minimization: Limiting data collection and retention to what is strictly necessary for the intended purpose.
meta:enum.non-repudiation: Providing proof of origin and delivery to prevent denial of actions or communications.
meta:enum.possession: Ensuring control or custody over data or assets, regardless of ownership.
meta:enum.privacy: Protecting individuals' personal information in accordance with legal and ethical standards.
meta:enum.purpose-limitation: Ensuring that personal data is only used for the purpose explicitly specified at collection.
meta:enum.transparency: Ensuring individuals are informed about data practices, including collection, use, and sharing.
meta:enum.utility: Ensuring data is usable and in a format suitable for the intended purpose.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom risk attribute.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom risk attribute.

name.description: A human-readable name for the risk.

statement.description: Structured risk statement describing source, event, and impact.

description.description: Additional narrative about the risk.

domains.description: The risk domains this risk affects.

affects.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the subjects this risk concerns. A subject may be a component, service, dataset, or model, or a party such as a person, persona, or organization, including a group at risk of being systematically disadvantaged.

relatedThreats.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats or threat scenarios from a threat model that inform this risk. A reference may point to a discrete threat or to a threat scenario.

relatedVulnerabilities.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the vulnerabilities that inform this risk.

relatedWeaknesses.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the weaknesses that inform this risk.

relatedRequirements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the requirements whose absence or non-conformance gives rise to this risk, such as a requirement that is unmet or only partially met.

relatedStandards.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the standards whose non-conformance gives rise to this risk.

relatedClaims.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the conformance claims that give rise to this risk, such as a [CycloneDX Attestations](https://cyclonedx.org/capabilities/attestations/) claim recording that a requirement is unmet or only partially met. The degree of conformance is carried by the claim, not by the risk.

relatedBusinessObjectives.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the business objectives this risk threatens.

inherentRisk.description: The assessed risk before any responses are applied.

residualRisk.description: The assessed risk remaining after responses are applied.

targetRisk.description: The risk level the organization aims to reach for this risk. The overall risk appetite is recorded separately on the risks collection or assessment.

responses.description: The responses applied to this risk.

status.title: Status
status.description: The lifecycle status of the risk. Use the custom option for a status specific to an organization's risk process.

meta:enum.identified: Risk has been identified but not yet evaluated.
meta:enum.assessed: Risk has been assessed and prioritized.
meta:enum.mitigated: Responses are in place reducing this risk.
meta:enum.transferred: Risk has been transferred to another party.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom status.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom status.

owner.description: The party accountable for this risk. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party. The party's `roles` should include `owner`.

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: A methodology-neutral assessment of risk along likelihood and impact, with an overall score. The same shape expresses inherent, residual, and target risk so reductions can be compared. A rating can be qualitative (a `level`), semi-quantitative (a numeric `score` on a bounded scale), or quantitative (for example a probable monetary loss under [FAIR](https://www.fairinstitute.org/)). For a catalogue of assessment techniques, see [IEC 31010](https://www.iso.org/standard/72140.html).

detectability.title: Detectability
detectability.description: The ease or likelihood of detecting the event before or as it is realized, used as a third axis by methodologies such as [FMEA](https://asq.org/quality-resources/fmea), whose risk priority number multiplies severity, occurrence, and detection.

score.description: The detection value on the scale defined by the methodology. For FMEA, a higher value indicates that the event is harder to detect and therefore riskier.

description.description: A narrative explanation of the detectability assessment.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: Confidence in this rating, expressed as a decimal from 0 to 1, where 1 is full confidence. Supports quantitative methods that reason over uncertainty.

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: The justification for this rating, including the method and the evidence considered.

level.title: Level
level.description: The qualitative likelihood level representing the overall assessed probability of the risk or scenario being realized.

meta:enum.very-low: Very unlikely to occur (less than 10% probability).
meta:enum.very-high: Very likely to occur (85 to 99% probability).
meta:enum.certain: Almost certain to occur (greater than 99% probability).

score.description: Numeric likelihood score on the scale defined by the methodology.

probability.description: Probability as a decimal from 0 to 1.

frequency.title: Frequency
frequency.description: The expected number of events within the timeframe, for frequency-based methods such as the [FAIR](https://www.fairinstitute.org/) loss event frequency. Unlike a probability, a frequency may exceed 1.

timeframe.title: Timeframe
timeframe.description: The period over which the probability or frequency is assessed, expressed as an [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) duration, for example P1Y for one year.

range.description: A range for the probability or frequency, supporting quantitative methods that reason over distributions.

factors.description: The factors considered when determining the likelihood level.

rationale.description: The justification for the likelihood rating.

likelihoodFactor.title: Likelihood Factor
likelihoodFactor.description: An individual contributing factor used to determine the overall likelihood of a risk or scenario being realized. Likelihood is rarely a single judgment. It is typically derived from multiple independent factors such as threat actor capability, exploit maturity, attack surface exposure, and control effectiveness. Each factor captures a discrete dimension of likelihood with its own rating, enabling transparent, auditable, and methodology-aligned risk assessments.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the likelihood factor being evaluated. Should clearly identify the dimension of likelihood being assessed, such as 'Threat Actor Capability', 'Exploit Availability', or 'Control Effectiveness'.

type.title: Type
type.description: The category of likelihood factor being assessed. Predefined types align with common risk assessment methodologies including FAIR, OWASP Risk Rating, and NIST SP 800-30.

oneOf.title: Predefined Likelihood Factor Type
oneOf.description: A likelihood factor type defined by the CycloneDX standard, aligned with established risk assessment methodologies including FAIR, OWASP Risk Rating, and NIST SP 800-30.

meta:enum.attack-vector: Accessibility of the attack path to the threat actor, considering network proximity, authentication requirements, and interaction complexity.
meta:enum.contact-frequency: How often the threat actor comes into contact with the target asset, independent of any intent to act. Aligns with the FAIR contact frequency factor.
meta:enum.control-effectiveness: Strength, reliability, and coverage of existing preventive, detective, or corrective controls that reduce the probability of successful exploitation. Corresponds to resistance strength in FAIR.
meta:enum.detectability: Likelihood that the activity is detected before the risk is realized. Lower detectability increases the chance of a successful action. Aligns with the OWASP Risk Rating intrusion detection factor and the FMEA detection axis.
meta:enum.discoverability: Ease with which the weakness or opportunity can be found by a potential actor. Aligns with the OWASP Risk Rating ease of discovery factor.
meta:enum.exploit-maturity: Availability, reliability, and sophistication of known exploits, attack toolkits, or proof-of-concept code targeting the identified weakness.
meta:enum.exposure: Degree to which the target asset, system, or service is visible, discoverable, or reachable by potential threat actors.
meta:enum.motivation: Level of incentive, intent, or determination driving the threat actor to pursue exploitation, including financial gain, ideology, or competitive advantage.
meta:enum.opportunity: Availability of the conditions, timing, or circumstances required for the threat actor to act, including access windows, environmental factors, and resource availability.
meta:enum.targeting: Degree to which the threat actor specifically selects this asset or organization rather than acting opportunistically. Aligns with the NIST SP 800-30 targeting factor.
meta:enum.threat-capability: Technical skill, resources, tooling, and sophistication of the threat actor relative to the complexity of the attack required.
meta:enum.vulnerability: Presence, severity, and exploitability of weaknesses in the target system, application, or process that could be leveraged by a threat actor.

oneOf.title: Custom Likelihood Factor Type
oneOf.description: A custom likelihood factor type not covered by predefined values, allowing organizations to extend the model with methodology-specific factors.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom likelihood factor type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom likelihood factor type and how it contributes to likelihood assessment.

level.title: Level
level.description: The qualitative rating assigned to this factor, representing the assessed degree to which this factor contributes to the overall likelihood of the risk being realized.

meta:enum.very-low: This factor has minimal contribution to risk realization (less than 10% influence).
meta:enum.low: This factor has limited contribution to risk realization (10 to 30% influence).
meta:enum.medium: This factor moderately contributes to risk realization (30 to 60% influence).
meta:enum.high: This factor strongly contributes to risk realization (60 to 85% influence).
meta:enum.very-high: This factor very strongly contributes to risk realization (85 to 99% influence).
meta:enum.certain: This factor almost certainly contributes to risk realization (greater than 99% influence).

score.title: Score
score.description: A numeric score representing the assessed value of this factor on the scale defined by the methodology, where a higher value indicates a greater contribution to likelihood.

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: The relative importance or weight of this factor in the overall likelihood calculation, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1. When multiple factors are present, weights can be used to produce a weighted average likelihood score. A weight of 1 indicates full importance; a weight of 0 indicates no importance.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative explanation of how this factor was evaluated, the evidence or reasoning supporting the assigned level or score, and how it contributes to the overall likelihood assessment.

meta:enum.negligible: Minimal impact with no significant consequences.

polarity.title: Polarity
polarity.description: Whether the impact represents a harm or a benefit. Benefits support modeling opportunities and positive risk.

score.description: Numeric impact score on the scale defined by the methodology.

categories.description: The categories of impact, including harms relevant to AI and societal risk.

factors.title: Factors
factors.description: The factors considered when determining the impact, allowing methodologies that derive impact from multiple weighted dimensions to record the breakdown.

range.description: A range for the impact score, supporting quantitative methods that reason over distributions.

description.description: A description of the potential impact.

impactCategory.title: Impact Category
impactCategory.description: A category of impact, including harms relevant to AI and societal risk. Use the custom option for categories specific to an organization's risk taxonomy.

meta:enum.integrity: Impact on data or system integrity.
meta:enum.bias: Systematic bias in automated outputs or decisions.
meta:enum.discrimination: Unfair treatment of individuals or groups, including on the basis of protected attributes.
meta:enum.fairness: Impact on the fair and equitable treatment of affected groups.
meta:enum.human-rights: Impact on fundamental human rights, including autonomy and dignity.
meta:enum.environmental: Environmental impact, including energy use and carbon emissions.
meta:enum.societal: Impact on society, communities, or civic processes.
meta:enum.physical: Physical harm to people or property.
meta:enum.health: Impact on physical or mental health.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom impact category.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom impact category.

impactFactor.title: Impact Factor
impactFactor.description: An individual contributing factor used to determine the overall impact of a risk or scenario. Methodologies such as OWASP Risk Rating, DREAD, and OCTAVE Allegro derive impact from several weighted dimensions, and each factor captures one dimension with its own rating.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the impact factor being evaluated, such as 'Financial damage' or 'Reputation damage'.

category.description: The impact dimension this factor measures.

score.title: Score
score.description: A numeric score representing the assessed value of this factor on the scale defined by the methodology, where a higher value indicates a greater contribution to impact.

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: The relative importance of this factor in the overall impact calculation, expressed as a decimal from 0 to 1. OCTAVE Allegro, for example, ranks impact areas and multiplies the ranking by the factor value.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative explanation of how this factor was evaluated and how it contributes to the overall impact assessment.

estimateRange.title: Estimate Range
estimateRange.description: A three-point estimate, supporting quantitative methods such as FAIR that reason over distributions rather than single values.

minimum.description: The minimum, or lower bound, estimate.

maximum.description: The maximum, or upper bound, estimate.

financialLoss.description: The estimated financial loss, in the units given by currency.

currency.description: The currency of the financial values, as an [ISO 4217](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html) code.

downtime.description: The expected downtime, as an [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) duration.

dataRecords.description: The number of data records affected.

recovery.description: The recovery time, as an [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) duration.

financialLossRange.description: A distribution of estimated financial loss, supporting quantitative methods such as FAIR that reason over ranges.

affectedGroups.description: The number of distinct groups or populations affected.

riskScore.title: Risk Score
riskScore.description: The overall risk score, expressed as a qualitative `level`, a numeric `score`, or both, with `vector` and `methodology` recording how it was derived. The scale of `score` is defined by the methodology, since rating systems differ. For example, [OWASP Risk Rating](https://owasp.org/www-community/OWASP_Risk_Rating_Methodology) averages factors from 0 to 9, [DREAD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DREAD_%28risk_assessment_model%29) averages five 1 to 10 ratings, [FMEA](https://asq.org/quality-resources/fmea) multiplies three 1 to 10 axes into a 1 to 1000 risk priority number, and [FAIR](https://www.fairinstitute.org/) expresses probable loss in monetary terms.

score.description: Numeric risk score on the scale defined by the methodology.

vector.description: A textual representation of the metric values used to derive the score, in the format defined by the methodology.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.description: The risk rating methodology used to derive this score. Only methodologies that define a scoring calculation are listed; governance frameworks such as [ISO 31000](https://www.iso.org/iso-31000-risk-management.html) are recorded as standards rather than as a score methodology. Use the custom option for methodologies not listed.

meta:enum.dread: [DREAD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DREAD_%28risk_assessment_model%29) risk rating model, the average of five ratings (damage, reproducibility, exploitability, affected users, discoverability) each scored 1 to 10.
meta:enum.fair: [Factor Analysis of Information Risk (FAIR)](https://www.fairinstitute.org/), a quantitative methodology expressing risk as loss event frequency multiplied by loss magnitude.
meta:enum.fmea: [Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)](https://asq.org/quality-resources/fmea), using a risk priority number of severity multiplied by occurrence multiplied by detection.
meta:enum.nist-sp-800-30: [NIST SP 800-30](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/30/r1/final) guide for conducting risk assessments, using its exemplary likelihood and impact scales.
meta:enum.owasp-risk-rating: [OWASP Risk Rating](https://owasp.org/www-community/OWASP_Risk_Rating_Methodology), averaging likelihood and impact factors each scored 0 to 9.
meta:enum.qualitative-matrix: A qualitative risk matrix that derives a risk level by plotting a likelihood band against an impact band, such as a five by five matrix producing a score from 1 to 25.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom risk methodology.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom risk methodology.

riskResponse.title: Risk Response
riskResponse.description: An action taken to modify a risk, pairing the chosen strategy with the controls that implement it. Covers the full set of dispositions, from avoiding or reducing a risk to accepting it or, for a positive risk, pursuing it.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference this response elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.description: The disposition chosen for the risk.

meta:enum.avoid: Eliminate the risk by removing its source or not proceeding with the activity.
meta:enum.reduce: Lower the likelihood or impact of the risk through controls.
meta:enum.transfer: Shift the risk to another party, for example through insurance or contract.
meta:enum.accept: Take no further action and retain the risk.
meta:enum.exploit: Pursue an opportunity so that a positive risk occurs.
meta:enum.enhance: Increase the likelihood or benefit of a positive risk.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the response.

controls.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that implement this response.

status.title: Status
status.description: The implementation status of the response.

owner.description: The party accountable for this response. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party.

targetDate.title: Target Date
targetDate.description: The date by which this response is targeted to be implemented.

addresses.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the items this response addresses, such as a threat, vulnerability, weakness, or another risk.

name.title: Name
name.description: A human-readable name for the assessment.

type.title: Type
type.description: The kinds of assessment performed, given as one or more named exercises or instruments. The type classifies the assessment exercise; the domains of risk evaluated are carried by the referenced risks rather than restated here. Use the custom option for kinds specific to an organization's process.

meta:enum.security: Security risk assessment, evaluating threats to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of a subject.
meta:enum.privacy: Privacy risk assessment, evaluating risks to individuals arising from the processing of personal data.
meta:enum.operational: Operational risk assessment, evaluating risks to ongoing operations and continuity.
meta:enum.safety: Safety risk assessment, evaluating risks of harm to people or property.
meta:enum.financial: Financial risk assessment, evaluating exposure to financial loss, such as credit, market, or liquidity risk.
meta:enum.environmental: Environmental risk assessment, evaluating the effect of an activity on the natural environment, including energy use and emissions.
meta:enum.supply-chain: Supply chain risk assessment, evaluating risks across the chain that delivers a product or service, including software and hardware provenance, for example following [NIST SP 800-161](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/161/r1/final).
meta:enum.compliance: Compliance assessment, evaluating conformance with applicable laws, regulations, policies, or standards.
meta:enum.data-protection-impact: Data protection impact assessment (DPIA), assessing high-risk processing of personal data, for example as described under Article 35 of the [GDPR](https://gdpr-info.eu/art-35-gdpr/).
meta:enum.fundamental-rights-impact: Fundamental rights impact assessment (FRIA), evaluating the effect of a system on fundamental rights, for example as introduced for certain high-risk AI systems by the [EU AI Act](https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/).
meta:enum.ai-impact: Artificial intelligence impact assessment, evaluating the risks and impacts of an AI system, for example following the [NIST AI Risk Management Framework](https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework).
meta:enum.business-impact: Business impact analysis (BIA), evaluating the operational and financial consequences of disruption to a business function.
meta:enum.third-party: Third-party risk assessment, evaluating the risk arising from a specific external party relationship, such as a vendor, supplier, or service provider, as distinct from the broader supply chain.
meta:enum.threat: Threat assessment, evaluating the threats and threat actors relevant to a subject.
meta:enum.vulnerability: Vulnerability assessment, evaluating weaknesses that could be exploited.
meta:enum.model-risk: Model risk assessment, evaluating the risk that a decision or quantitative model is incorrect or misused, including model validation.
meta:enum.fraud: Fraud risk assessment, evaluating exposure to fraudulent activity and the controls that detect or prevent it.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom assessment type.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom assessment type.

cadence.title: Cadence
cadence.description: The temporal cadence or occasion on which the assessment is performed. Use the custom option for patterns specific to an organization's process.

meta:enum.initial: Initial, baseline assessment performed once at the outset.
meta:enum.continuous: Continuous, automated monitoring, as in a GRC engineering or continuous controls monitoring practice.
meta:enum.triggered: Assessment triggered by a specific event, such as a change or an incident.
meta:enum.ad-hoc: Unplanned, one-off assessment, neither scheduled nor triggered by a defined event.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom assessment cadence.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom assessment cadence.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: The scope of the assessment, including the systems, boundaries, and what is in or out of scope.

status.title: Status
status.description: The lifecycle status of the assessment. Use the custom option for a status specific to an organization's process.

meta:enum.approved: The assessment has been reviewed and approved.
meta:enum.superseded: The assessment has been replaced by a later one.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom status.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom status.

assessors.title: Assessors
assessors.description: The parties that performed the assessment. Each may be an inline party or a reference to a previously declared party, and may be a person or an automated system, agent, or tool. Automated assessors support continuous and automated assessment, as in a GRC engineering practice. A party's `roles` should include `auditor` or `assessor`.

summary.description: An executive summary of the assessment.

risks.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to risk entries evaluated in this assessment.

method.title: Method
method.description: The aggregation method used to derive the overall risk from the assessed risks. Use the custom option for methods not listed, such as a quantitative roll-up of loss distributions under FAIR.

meta:enum.maximum: The overall risk equals the highest individual risk.
meta:enum.sum: The overall risk is the sum of the individual risks, representing total exposure.
meta:enum.average: The overall risk is the mean of the individual risks.
meta:enum.weighted-average: The overall risk is a weighted mean of the individual risks.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom aggregation method.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom aggregation method.
An optional explanation for custom aggregation approaches.

overallRisk.description: The aggregated risk result for this assessment.

assumptions.title: Assumptions
assumptions.description: The assumptions, constraints, and limitations under which the assessment was made, to be re-evaluated when they change.

nextReview.description: When the next review should occur.

riskAppetites.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the risk appetites, declared under the risks collection, that applied for this assessment.

meta:enum.critical: Critical impact with severe business consequences.

appetiteLevel.description: A qualitative risk appetite level, ranging from risk-averse to risk-seeking.

meta:enum.averse: Avoidance of risk is the priority, and only the lowest-risk options are acceptable.
meta:enum.minimal: A preference for very low risk, accepting risk only where it is unavoidable.
meta:enum.cautious: A preference for safe options, accepting limited and well-understood risk for reward.
meta:enum.open: A willingness to accept higher risk where the potential reward justifies it.
meta:enum.hungry: An active willingness to seek risk in pursuit of greater reward, within defined control limits.

riskAppetite.title: Risk Appetite
riskAppetite.description: The amount and type of risk a party is willing to pursue or retain, stated explicitly so that it is not left implicit in individual target ratings. An appetite may be attributed to a specific party through `party`, so a single document can record the appetites of more than one party, and it may be stated overall and refined per risk domain. Quantitative tolerances for a specific risk are expressed through that risk's target rating, and measurement of actual risk against the appetite is recorded through assessments.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference this risk appetite elsewhere, such as from an assessment.

party.description: The party whose risk appetite this is. Set this when a single document captures the appetites of more than one party. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party.

statement.title: Statement
statement.description: A narrative statement of the overall risk appetite, including any qualifications or context.

rationale.description: The justification for this appetite, such as the strategic objective or regulatory obligation that informs it.

owner.description: The party accountable for setting and approving this appetite, such as a board or risk committee. May be an inline party object or a reference to a previously declared party.

horizon.description: The time horizon over which this appetite applies, expressed as an [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) duration, for example P1Y for one year.

nextReview.description: When this appetite is next due for review, since appetite changes with strategy, market conditions, and the threat landscape.

domain.description: The risk domain this appetite entry applies to.

level.description: The appetite level for this risk domain.

statement.description: A narrative statement of the appetite for this risk domain.

rationale.description: The justification for the appetite for this risk domain.

categories.description: Per-domain appetite, refining the overall appetite for specific risk domains such as security, privacy, or financial.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the service elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: The organization that provides the service.

group.title: Service Group
group.description: The grouping name, namespace, or identifier. This will often be a shortened, single name of the company or project that produced the service or domain name. Whitespace and special characters should be avoided.

name.title: Service Name
name.description: The name of the service. This will often be a shortened, single name of the service.

description.title: Service Description
description.description: Specifies a description for the service

endpoints.title: Endpoints
endpoints.description: The endpoint URIs of the service. Multiple endpoints are allowed.

authenticated.title: Authentication Required
authenticated.description: A boolean value indicating if the service requires authentication. A value of true indicates the service requires authentication prior to use. A value of false indicates the service does not require authentication.

x-trust-boundary.title: Crosses Trust Boundary
x-trust-boundary.description: A boolean value indicating if use of the service crosses a trust zone or boundary. A value of true indicates that by using the service, a trust boundary is crossed. A value of false indicates that by using the service, a trust boundary is not crossed.

trustZone.title: Trust Zone
trustZone.description: The name of the trust zone the service resides in.

data.title: Data
data.description: Specifies information about the data including the directional flow of data and the data classification.

services.title: Services
services.description: A list of services included or deployed behind the parent service. This is not a dependency tree. It provides a way to specify a hierarchical representation of service assemblies.

flow.title: Directional Flow
flow.description: Specifies the flow direction of the data. Direction is relative to the service. Inbound flow states that data enters the service. Outbound flow states that data leaves the service. Bi-directional states that data flows both ways and unknown states that the direction is not known.

name.title: Name
name.description: Name for the defined data

description.title: Description
description.description: Short description of the data content and usage
examples: Credit card information being exchanged in between the web app and the database

source.title: Source
source.description: The URI, URL, or BOM-Link of the components or services the data came in from

destination.title: Destination
destination.description: The URI, URL, or BOM-Link of the components or services the data is sent to

standards.title: Standards
standards.description: The list of standards which may consist of regulations, industry or organizational-specific standards, maturity models, best practices, or any other requirements which can be evaluated against or attested to.

standard.title: Standard
standard.description: A standard may consist of regulations, industry or organizational-specific standards, maturity models, best practices, or any other requirements which can be evaluated against or attested to.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the standard. This will often be a shortened, single name of the standard.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description of the standard.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: The owner of the standard, often the entity responsible for its release.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.description: The list of requirements comprising the standard.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

identifier.title: Identifier
identifier.description: The unique identifier used in the standard to identify a specific requirement. This should match what is in the standard and should not be the requirements bom-ref.

text.title: Text
text.description: The textual content of the requirement.

descriptions.title: Descriptions
descriptions.description: The supplemental text that provides additional guidance or context to the requirement, but is not directly part of the requirement.

openCre.title: OWASP OpenCRE Identifier(s)
openCre.description: The Common Requirements Enumeration (CRE) identifier(s). CRE is a structured and standardized framework for uniting security standards and guidelines. CRE links each section of a resource to a shared topic identifier (a Common Requirement). Through this shared topic link, all resources map to each other. Use of CRE promotes clear and unambiguous communication among stakeholders.

parent.title: Parent BOM Reference
parent.description: The `bom-ref` to a parent requirement. This establishes a hierarchy of requirements. Top-level requirements must not define a parent. Only child requirements should define parents.

levels.title: Levels
levels.description: The list of levels associated with the standard. Some standards have different levels of compliance.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the object elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

identifier.title: Identifier
identifier.description: The identifier used in the standard to identify a specific level.

description.title: Description
description.description: The description of the level.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.description: The list of requirement `bom-ref`s that comprise the level.

threats.title: Threats
threats.description: Threat-modelling content, including the documented threats, the scenarios that realize them, the attack patterns and attack trees that describe how they are carried out, and the trust boundaries they cross.

threats.title: Threats
threats.description: The documented threats. Each describes what can go wrong, independent of any specific actor or occurrence.

scenarios.title: Scenarios
scenarios.description: The threat scenarios, each realizing one or more documented threats in a specific way.

attackPatterns.title: Attack Patterns
attackPatterns.description: A library of attack patterns, typically aligned with CAPEC, that threats and scenarios can reference.

attackTrees.title: Attack Trees
attackTrees.description: Attack trees that decompose attacker goals into sub-goals and techniques.

attackPaths.title: Attack Paths
attackPaths.description: Attack paths that describe the ordered progression of an attack across elements and trust boundaries, including lateral movement.

abuseCases.title: Abuse Cases
abuseCases.description: Abuse or misuse cases that describe how the system can be exercised by an adversary.

trustBoundaries.title: Trust Boundaries
trustBoundaries.description: The trust boundaries of the system, extending the architectural boundaries defined in a blueprint with trust semantics.

methodologies.title: Methodologies
methodologies.description: The threat modeling methodologies applied to produce this threat model, such as STRIDE, LINDDUN, PASTA, or attack trees.

methodology.title: Threat Modeling Methodology
methodology.description: A threat modeling methodology applied to produce this threat model. Use the custom option for methodologies not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.STRIDE: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege.
meta:enum.LINDDUN: Linkability, Identifiability, Non-repudiation, Detectability, Disclosure of information, Unawareness, Non-compliance.
meta:enum.PASTA: Process for Attack Simulation and Threat Analysis.
meta:enum.MAESTRO: Multi-Agent Environment, Security, Threat, Risk, and Outcome.
meta:enum.VAST: Visual, Agile, and Simple Threat modelling.
meta:enum.ATFAA: Advanced Threat Framework for Autonomous AI Agents.
meta:enum.attack-tree: Attack tree methodology, decomposing attacker goals into sub-goals and techniques.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom threat modeling methodology.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom threat modeling methodology.

threat.title: Threat
threat.description: A documented threat, describing what can go wrong independent of any specific actor or occurrence. A threat can be catalogued on its own; a threat scenario references it to describe a specific realization.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the threat elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the threat.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the threat.

source.title: Source
source.description: The source of the threat, such as a threat intelligence feed, a threat catalogue, or an analysis activity.

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: The nature of the threat source that gives rise to this threat, following the threat source types of [NIST SP 800-30](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/30/r1/final). Use the custom option for an origin not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.adversarial: An adversary acting with intent, such as an individual, group, organization, or nation state.
meta:enum.accidental: An erroneous action taken without malicious intent, such as a mistake by a privileged user or administrator.
meta:enum.structural: A failure of equipment, software, or environmental controls, such as resource exhaustion or expiry of a component.
meta:enum.environmental: A natural or human-made event outside the control of the organization, such as a fire, flood, or wide-area infrastructure outage.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom origin.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom origin.

categories.title: Categories
categories.description: The methodology-specific categories that this threat falls under. A threat may be categorized under more than one methodology, for example STRIDE and MAESTRO. The methodology is one of the supported threat-modelling methodologies, and it determines which category values are permitted.

items.title: Threat Category
items.description: A threat category. The taxonomy determines the permitted category values.

taxonomy.title: Taxonomy
taxonomy.description: The threat classification taxonomy that the category is drawn from.

meta:enum.STRIDE: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege.
meta:enum.LINDDUN: Linkability, Identifiability, Non-repudiation, Detectability, Disclosure of information, Unawareness, and Non-compliance.
meta:enum.MAESTRO: Multi-Agent Environment, Security, Threat, Risk, and Outcome, whose categories are its seven architectural layers.
meta:enum.MITRE-ATTACK: The MITRE ATT&CK framework, whose categories are its Enterprise tactics.

category.title: Category
category.description: The category within the taxonomy. The permitted values are determined by the taxonomy.

meta:enum.spoofing: Impersonating something or someone, violating authenticity.
meta:enum.tampering: Modifying data or code, violating integrity.
meta:enum.repudiation: Denying having performed an action, violating non-repudiation.
meta:enum.information-disclosure: Exposing information to unauthorized parties, violating confidentiality.
meta:enum.denial-of-service: Denying or degrading service to legitimate users, violating availability.
meta:enum.elevation-of-privilege: Gaining capabilities without proper authorization, violating authorization.

meta:enum.linkability: Linking data items to learn more about an individual.
meta:enum.non-repudiation: Being unable to deny a claim, harming plausible deniability.
meta:enum.detectability: Detecting that an item of interest exists.
meta:enum.unawareness: Individuals being unaware of the processing of their data.
meta:enum.non-compliance: Deviating from policy, regulation, or best practice.

meta:enum.foundation-models: Layer 1. The core models that provide the agent's capabilities.
meta:enum.data-operations: Layer 2. Storage, retrieval, and processing of the agent's data.
meta:enum.agent-frameworks: Layer 3. The frameworks and tools that build and run agents.
meta:enum.deployment-and-infrastructure: Layer 4. The infrastructure on which agents are deployed and scaled.
meta:enum.evaluation-and-observability: Layer 5. Evaluation, monitoring, and observability of agent behaviour.
meta:enum.security-and-compliance: Layer 6. The cross-cutting security and compliance controls.
meta:enum.agent-ecosystem: Layer 7. The applications and marketplace where agents deliver value to end-users.

weaknesses.description: The weaknesses, such as CWE classifications, that this threat exploits.

relatedVulnerabilities.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the vulnerabilities that this threat exploits or that inform it.

attackPatterns.title: Attack Patterns
attackPatterns.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the attack patterns by which this threat is carried out.

attackTrees.title: Attack Trees
attackTrees.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the attack trees that decompose this threat.

abuseCases.title: Abuse Cases
abuseCases.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the abuse cases that illustrate how this threat can be exercised.

killChainPhase.description: The phase of the cyber kill chain at which this threat operates.

behaviors.title: Behaviors
behaviors.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the declared behaviors that constitute the intent of this threat, drawn from the behavior taxonomy.

affectedAssets.title: Affected Assets
affectedAssets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the kinds of assets this threat affects.

relatedBusinessObjectives.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the business objectives this threat endangers.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this threat.

threatScenario.title: Threat Scenario
threatScenario.description: A specific realization of a threat, in which an actor seeks to cause harm. The scenario references the threat it realizes, supplies the actor by reference to a party, and carries the scenario-specific traits and risk assessment, such as motivation, intent, access level, likelihood, and impact.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the threat scenario elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the threat scenario.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the threat scenario.

threats.title: Threats
threats.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats that this scenario realizes. A scenario realizes one or more threats.

actor.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party that is the actor in this scenario. Named adversary groups are organizations and abstract attacker classes are personas.

threatProfile.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a reusable threat profile that describes the durable capabilities of the actor.

motivation.title: Motivation
motivation.description: The motivations of the actor in this scenario.

meta:enum.destruction: Motivated by the desire to destroy.
meta:enum.disruption: Motivated by the desire to disrupt operations.
meta:enum.reputation: Motivated by reputational gain or harm.

intent.title: Intent
intent.description: The intent of the actor in this scenario.

meta:enum.accidental: The harm is caused without intent.
meta:enum.opportunistic: The actor takes advantage of an opportunity as it arises.
meta:enum.targeted: The actor deliberately targets the subject.
meta:enum.persistent: The actor pursues the target persistently over time.

accessLevel.title: Access Level
accessLevel.description: The level of access the actor is assumed to have in this scenario.

meta:enum.internal: Internal access, such as that of an ordinary user.

riskAttributes.title: Risk Attributes
riskAttributes.description: The security and privacy attributes that this threat would compromise if realized.

affectedAssets.title: Affected Assets
affectedAssets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the assets affected in this scenario.

relatedRisks.title: Related Risks
relatedRisks.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the risks that this scenario contributes to.

relatedVulnerabilities.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the vulnerabilities exploited in this scenario.

threatProfiles.title: Threat Profiles
threatProfiles.description: Reusable threat profiles, each describing the durable characteristics of an actor that may pose a threat.

threatProfile.title: Threat Profile
threatProfile.description: A durable, actor-level characterization of the capability to pose a threat, capturing attributes that persist across scenarios. Scenario-specific attributes, such as motivation, intent, and access level, belong on a threat scenario rather than on this profile.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference this threat profile elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the threat profile.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the threat profile.

sophistication.title: Sophistication
sophistication.description: The level of technical sophistication an actor characterized by this profile is assumed to possess.

meta:enum.minimal: Minimal sophistication, relying on readily available tools and techniques.
meta:enum.advanced: Advanced skills consistent with a persistent and well-resourced actor.
meta:enum.expert: Expert skills consistent with the most capable actors.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: The level of resources an actor characterized by this profile is assumed to have available.

skillSet.title: Skill Set
skillSet.description: The specific skills an actor characterized by this profile is assumed to possess.

attackVector.title: Attack Vector
attackVector.description: The conditions under which an attack is possible, following the convention of common vulnerability scoring systems.

type.title: Type
type.description: The context by which an attack is possible.

privileges.title: Privileges
privileges.description: The level of privileges required to carry out the attack.

userInteraction.title: User Interaction
userInteraction.description: Whether the attack requires interaction from a user other than the attacker.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: Whether a successful attack affects resources beyond the vulnerable component.

meta:enum.unchanged: The attack affects only the vulnerable component.
meta:enum.changed: The attack affects resources beyond the vulnerable component.

attackPattern.title: Attack Pattern
attackPattern.description: A description of the common attributes and approach of an attack, typically aligned with CAPEC. Techniques are aligned with MITRE ATT&CK.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the attack pattern elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

capecId.title: CAPEC Identifier
capecId.description: The Common Attack Pattern Enumeration and Classification (CAPEC) identifier for this attack pattern.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the attack pattern.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the attack pattern.

prerequisites.title: Prerequisites
prerequisites.description: The conditions that must hold for the attack to be possible.

techniques.title: Techniques
techniques.description: The techniques used to carry out the attack, typically aligned with MITRE ATT&CK.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this attack pattern.

examples.title: Examples
examples.description: Real-world examples of the attack pattern.

technique.title: Technique
technique.description: A specific technique used to carry out an attack, typically aligned with MITRE ATT&CK.

id.title: Identifier
id.description: The identifier of the technique, such as a MITRE ATT&CK technique identifier.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the technique.

tactic.title: Tactic
tactic.description: The tactic that the technique supports, such as a MITRE ATT&CK tactic.

procedure.title: Procedure
procedure.description: The specific procedure by which the technique is carried out.

attackTree.title: Attack Tree
attackTree.description: A decomposition of an attacker goal into sub-goals and techniques, expressed as a flat collection of nodes. Each node lists its child nodes by reference together with an operator that says how those children combine, so the tree is expressed without nesting. One node is the root, representing the attacker goal.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the attack tree elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the attack tree.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the attack tree.

root.title: Root BOM Reference
root.description: Optional reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the root node within nodes, representing the attacker goal. When omitted, the root is the node that no other node lists as a child.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.description: The nodes of the attack tree. Each node references its child nodes, which are themselves entries in this array, forming the hierarchy without nesting.
