attackTreeNode.title: Attack Tree Node
attackTreeNode.description: A node in an attack tree, representing an attacker goal, sub-goal, or technique. A node references its child nodes and carries an operator that says how those children combine. The hierarchy is expressed by these references rather than by nesting.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the node elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The goal, sub-goal, or technique that the node represents.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the node.

operator.title: Operator
operator.description: Specifies how the nodes listed in 'children' combine to achieve this node. A value of 'and' requires every child node; a value of 'or' requires any single child node. For example, a node whose children are a phishing node and a credential-stuffing node uses 'or' when either alone achieves it, or 'and' when both are needed together. A node with no children is a leaf and omits this property.

meta:enum.and: All child nodes are required to achieve this node.
meta:enum.or: Any one child node is sufficient to achieve this node.

children.title: Children
children.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the child nodes that decompose this node. Each child node is a separate entry in the same nodes array, so the hierarchy is expressed without nesting. A child node may be shared by more than one parent.

attackPattern.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the attack pattern that this node represents.

weakness.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the weakness that this node exploits.

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: The relative cost to an attacker of achieving this node.

skill.title: Skill
skill.description: The skill level required to achieve this node.

detectability.title: Detectability
detectability.description: How readily an attempt to achieve this node can be detected.

meta:enum.low: The attempt is difficult to detect.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this node.

abuseCase.title: Abuse Case
abuseCase.description: A description of how an adversary can misuse the system to achieve a malicious goal. An abuse case is the security counterpart of a use case and reuses its flow structure.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the abuse case elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the abuse case, typically expressed as a malicious goal.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative describing how the system can be misused or abused.

abuser.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party that abuses the system, typically an attacker persona.

realizes.title: Realizes
realizes.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats that this abuse case realizes.

preconditions.title: Preconditions
preconditions.description: The conditions that must hold before the abuse case can begin.

mainFlow.title: Main Flow
mainFlow.description: The ordered steps the abuser follows.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the assets or flows targeted in this abuse case.

detectionOpportunities.title: Detection Opportunities
detectionOpportunities.description: The signals, logs, or controls that present an opportunity to detect the abuse.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this abuse case.

exploitability.title: Exploitability
exploitability.description: The degree to which a threat can be exploited in practice.

level.title: Level
level.description: The maturity of available exploitation capability.

meta:enum.theoretical: Exploitation is theoretically possible but has not been demonstrated.
meta:enum.weaponized: A weaponized exploit is in use.

skillRequired.title: Skill Required
skillRequired.description: The skill level required to exploit the threat.

indicators.title: Indicators
indicators.description: The observable indicators associated with a threat.

compromise.title: Indicators of Compromise
compromise.description: The indicators that a compromise has occurred.

attack.title: Indicators of Attack
attack.description: The indicators that an attack is under way.

signatures.title: Signatures
signatures.description: The detection signatures associated with the threat.

signature.title: Signature
signature.description: A detection signature for a threat.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of signature.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of what the signature detects.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: The confidence level of a detection by this signature.

trustBoundary.title: Trust Boundary
trustBoundary.description: A security annotation marking an architectural boundary as one across which trust changes. It references a boundary defined in a blueprint, where the zones, type, and crossing requirements already live, and adds only the trust differential and the threats and controls at that boundary.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the trust boundary elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

boundary.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the blueprint boundary that this trust boundary annotates.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the trust boundary.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the trust boundary.

trustLevel.title: Trust Level
trustLevel.description: The trust level differential across the boundary.

meta:enum.untrusted: The boundary separates an untrusted zone.
meta:enum.semi-trusted: The boundary separates a semi-trusted zone.
meta:enum.trusted: The boundary separates a trusted zone.
meta:enum.highly-trusted: The boundary separates a highly trusted zone.

threatsAtBoundary.title: Threats At Boundary
threatsAtBoundary.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats that could exploit this boundary.

controlsAtBoundary.title: Controls At Boundary
controlsAtBoundary.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls in place at this boundary. Intended primarily for referencing externally defined controls. Where the control and boundary are declared together, prefer the control's appliesTo property.

killChainPhase.title: Kill Chain Phase
killChainPhase.description: A phase of the cyber kill chain.

meta:enum.delivery: Transmitting the payload to the target.
meta:enum.installation: Installing malicious software or establishing a foothold.
meta:enum.command-and-control: Establishing a command and control channel.
meta:enum.actions-on-objectives: Achieving the goals of the attack.

attackPathStep.title: Attack Path Step
attackPathStep.description: A single step in an attack path, describing one action in the progression of an attack.

name.title: Name
name.description: A short name for the step.

description.title: Description
description.description: The action the adversary performs in this step.

technique.description: The technique used in this step, typically aligned with MITRE ATT&CK.

attackPattern.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the attack pattern applied in this step.

killChainPhase.description: The phase of the cyber kill chain at which this step operates.

source.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the element or zone from which this step originates.

destination.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the element or zone this step reaches.

boundaryCrossed.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the trust boundary this step crosses.

exploits.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the weaknesses or vulnerabilities this step exploits.

mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that detect or prevent this step.

attackPath.title: Attack Path
attackPath.description: An ordered sequence of steps that an adversary follows to progress from initial access toward an objective, crossing elements and trust boundaries. An attack path complements an attack tree: the tree decomposes a goal into alternatives, whereas the path expresses the temporal progression of a single attack, including lateral movement.

name.title: Name
name.description: A human-readable name for the attack path.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the attack path.

objective.title: Objective
objective.description: The attacker objective that the path works toward, such as exfiltrating data or achieving code execution.

actor.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the adversary that follows this path.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: The ordered steps of the path, from the initial action to the objective. The order of the array is significant.

realizes.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats that this path realizes.

relatedRisks.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the risks that this path contributes to.

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.description: A collection of use cases that describe how actors interact with the system to achieve specific goals.

useCase.title: Use Case
useCase.description: A structured description of how one or more actors interact with the system to achieve a specific goal, including the primary flow, alternative paths, and exception scenarios.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name or title of the use case, typically expressed as a verb phrase that captures the goal from the actor's perspective (e.g., 'Place Order', 'Reset Password').

description.title: Description
description.description: A detailed narrative of the use case, including the actor's goal, the scope of the interaction, and any relevant business rules or constraints that govern its execution.

actors.title: Actors
actors.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the actors that participate in this use case, representing anyone or anything that initiates or interacts with the described flow.

preconditions.title: Preconditions
preconditions.description: Conditions that must hold true before the use case can begin execution, such as required system state, authenticated sessions, available resources, or prior completed use cases.

postconditions.title: Postconditions
postconditions.description: Conditions that will be true after the use case completes successfully, describing the expected system state, data changes, notifications sent, or side effects produced.

mainFlow.title: Main Flow
mainFlow.description: The primary sequence of steps representing the most common or expected path through the use case, from the triggering action to the successful outcome.

alternativeFlows.title: Alternative Flows
alternativeFlows.description: Variant paths that branch from the main flow under specific conditions, representing valid but less common ways to achieve the use case goal or a modified outcome.

exceptions.title: Exceptions
exceptions.description: Error or failure scenarios that may interrupt the main or alternative flows, including the triggering condition and how the system responds to recover or terminate gracefully.

successCriteria.title: Success Criteria
successCriteria.description: Measurable or observable criteria that determine whether the use case has been completed successfully, used for validation, acceptance testing, and stakeholder sign-off.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the requirements that are implemented, validated, or addressed by this use case.

businessObjectives.title: Business Objectives
businessObjectives.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the business objectives this use case supports.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Supplementary information, clarifications, open questions, or implementation guidance that provides additional context beyond the formal use case structure.

step.title: Step
step.description: A single discrete action within a use case flow, performed by a specific actor, that advances the interaction toward the use case goal.

number.title: Number
number.description: The sequence number indicating the ordinal position of this step within its flow, starting at 1.

description.title: Description
description.description: A clear, action-oriented statement describing what the actor does in this step and the expected system response, written in the form 'The [actor] [action]'.

actor.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the actor who initiates or performs this step, which may be a human user, an external system, or the system under design.

flow.title: Flow
flow.description: An alternative sequence of steps that branches from the main flow under a specific condition, representing a valid variant path through the use case.

name.title: Name
name.description: A short, descriptive name identifying this alternative flow (e.g., 'Payment Declined', 'Guest Checkout').

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative explaining the purpose and context of this alternative flow, including how it differs from the main flow and under what circumstances it applies.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: The specific condition, decision point, or triggering event that causes execution to diverge from the main flow into this alternative path.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: The ordered sequence of steps that comprise this alternative flow, following the same structure as main flow steps.

exception.title: Exception
exception.description: An error or failure scenario that may interrupt the normal execution of a use case, including the condition that triggers it and how the system responds.

name.title: Name
name.description: A short, descriptive name identifying this exception scenario (e.g., 'Session Timeout', 'Insufficient Funds', 'Service Unavailable').

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative describing the nature of this exception, its potential causes, and its impact on the use case and the actors involved.

condition.title: Condition
condition.description: The specific error condition, system state, or external event that triggers this exception during the execution of the use case.

handling.title: Handling
handling.description: The recovery strategy or response the system takes when this exception occurs, such as retrying the operation, rolling back changes, notifying the user, or escalating to support.

useCaseAssertions.title: Use Case Assertions
useCaseAssertions.description: A list of assertions describing how a component relates to use cases defined in the BOM.

items.title: Use Case Assertion
items.description: An assertion linking one or more use cases to a component, specifying the nature of the relationship.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the assertion elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.

assertionType.title: Assertion Type
assertionType.description: The type of assertion being made about the relationship between the component and the use case.

meta:enum.extends: The component adds optional or conditional behavior to the use case, augmenting its primary flow with additional capability.
meta:enum.implements: The component implements or realises the use case, providing the primary capability that makes the use case functional.
meta:enum.inhibits: The component is known to interfere with or block the correct execution of the use case.
meta:enum.not-applicable: The use case does not pertain to this component. The evaluator has reviewed the use case and determined it is irrelevant.
meta:enum.not-assessed: The relationship between the component and the use case has not yet been evaluated.
meta:enum.other: A relationship that does not fit into the other predefined assertion types.
meta:enum.participates-in: The component participates in the use case but is not solely responsible for its implementation.
meta:enum.supports: The component provides enabling infrastructure for the use case without directly implementing or participating in it.
meta:enum.triggers: The component initiates or kicks off the use case without being responsible for its implementation.
meta:enum.validates: The component validates, tests, or verifies the correct execution of the use case.

useCaseRefs.title: Use Case References
useCaseRefs.description: A list of BOM references linking to use case objects defined in the BOM.

description.title: Description
description.description: Additional context or clarification regarding the assertion.

vulnerabilities.title: Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerabilities identified in components or services.

vulnerability.title: Vulnerability
vulnerability.description: Defines a weakness in a component or service that could be exploited or triggered by a threat source.

bom-ref.title: BOM Reference
bom-ref.description:
An identifier which can be used to reference the vulnerability elsewhere in the BOM. Every `bom-ref` must be unique within the BOM.
Value SHOULD not start with the BOM-Link intro 'urn:cdx:' to avoid conflicts with BOM-Links.

id.title: ID
id.description: The identifier that uniquely identifies the vulnerability.

source.description: The source that published the vulnerability.

references.title: References
references.description: Zero or more pointers to vulnerabilities that are the equivalent of the vulnerability specified. Often times, the same vulnerability may exist in multiple sources of vulnerability intelligence, but have different identifiers. References provide a way to correlate vulnerabilities across multiple sources of vulnerability intelligence.

id.title: ID
id.description: An identifier that uniquely identifies the vulnerability.

source.description: The source that published the vulnerability.

ratings.title: Ratings
ratings.description: List of vulnerability ratings. Consumers should consider ratings in prioritization decisions; source ratings may differ and aid prioritization.

weaknesses.description: The weaknesses, such as CWE classifications, that give rise to this vulnerability.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the vulnerability as provided by the source.

detail.title: Details
detail.description: If available, an in-depth description of the vulnerability as provided by the source organization. Details often include information useful in understanding root cause.

recommendation.title: Recommendation
recommendation.description: Recommendations of how the vulnerability can be remediated or mitigated.

workaround.title: Workarounds
workaround.description: A bypass, usually temporary, of the vulnerability that reduces its likelihood and/or impact. Workarounds often involve changes to configuration or deployments.

proofOfConcept.title: Proof of Concept
proofOfConcept.description: Evidence used to reproduce the vulnerability.

reproductionSteps.title: Steps to Reproduce
reproductionSteps.description: Precise steps to reproduce the vulnerability.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: A description of the environment in which reproduction was possible.

supportingMaterial.title: Supporting Material
supportingMaterial.description: Supporting material that helps in reproducing or understanding how reproduction is possible. This may include screenshots, payloads, and PoC exploit code.

advisories.title: Advisories
advisories.description: Published advisories of the vulnerability if provided.

created.title: Created
created.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the vulnerability record was created in the vulnerability database.

published.title: Published
published.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the vulnerability record was first published.

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the vulnerability record was last updated.

rejected.title: Rejected
rejected.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the vulnerability record was rejected (if applicable).

credits.title: Credits
credits.description: Individuals or organizations credited with the discovery of the vulnerability.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.description: The organizations credited with vulnerability discovery.

individuals.title: Individuals
individuals.description: The individuals, not associated with organizations, that are credited with vulnerability discovery.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: The tool(s) used to identify, confirm, or score the vulnerability.

components.title: Components
components.description: A list of software and hardware components used as tools.

services.title: Services
services.description: A list of services used as tools. This may include microservices, function-as-a-service, and other types of network or intra-process services.

analysis.title: Impact Analysis
analysis.description: An assessment of the impact and exploitability of the vulnerability.

mitigatingControls.title: Mitigating Controls
mitigatingControls.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that prevent or reduce the exploitability or impact of the vulnerability. Substantiates justifications such as protected_by_mitigating_control, protected_at_perimeter, and protected_at_runtime.

response.title: Response
response.description: A response to the vulnerability by the manufacturer, supplier, or project responsible for the affected component or service. More than one response is allowed. Responses are strongly encouraged for vulnerabilities where the analysis state is exploitable.

meta:enum.update: Update to a different revision or release
meta:enum.rollback: Revert to a previous revision or release

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Detailed description of the impact including methods used during assessment. If a vulnerability is not exploitable, this field should include specific details on why the component or service is not impacted by this vulnerability.

firstIssued.title: First Issued
firstIssued.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the analysis was first issued.

lastUpdated.title: Last Updated
lastUpdated.description: The date and time (timestamp) when the analysis was last updated.

ref.title: Reference
ref.description: References a component or service by the objects bom-ref

versions.title: Versions
versions.description: Zero or more individual versions or range of versions.

version.title: Version
version.description: A single version of a component or service.

range.title: Version Range
range.description: A version range specified in Package URL Version Range syntax (vers) which is defined at https://github.com/package-url/vers-spec

status.title: Status
status.description: The vulnerability status for the version or range of versions.

affects.title: Affects
affects.description: The components or services that are affected by the vulnerability.

advisory.title: Advisory
advisory.description: Title and location where advisory information can be obtained. An advisory is a notification of a threat to a component, service, or system.

url.title: URL
url.description: Location where the advisory can be obtained.

severity.title: Severity
severity.description: Textual representation of the severity of the vulnerability adopted by the analysis method. If the analysis method uses values other than what is provided, the user is expected to translate appropriately.

scoreMethod.title: Method
scoreMethod.description: Specifies the severity or risk scoring methodology or standard used.

meta:enum.other: Another severity or risk scoring methodology

impactAnalysisState.title: Impact Analysis State
impactAnalysisState.description: Declares the current state of an occurrence of a vulnerability, after automated or manual analysis.

meta:enum.resolved_with_pedigree: The vulnerability has been remediated and evidence of the changes are provided in the affected components pedigree containing verifiable commit history and/or diff(s).
meta:enum.exploitable: The vulnerability may be directly or indirectly exploitable.
meta:enum.false_positive: The vulnerability is not specific to the component or service and was falsely identified or associated.
meta:enum.not_affected: The component or service is not affected by the vulnerability. Justification should be specified for all not_affected cases.

impactAnalysisJustification.title: Impact Analysis Justification
impactAnalysisJustification.description: The rationale of why the impact analysis state was asserted.

meta:enum.code_not_present: The code has been removed or tree-shaked.
meta:enum.code_not_reachable: The vulnerable code is not invoked at runtime.
meta:enum.requires_configuration: Exploitability requires a configurable option to be set/unset.
meta:enum.requires_dependency: Exploitability requires a dependency that is not present.
meta:enum.requires_environment: Exploitability requires a certain environment which is not present.
meta:enum.protected_by_compiler: Exploitability requires a compiler flag to be set/unset.
meta:enum.protected_at_perimeter: Attacks are blocked at physical, logical, or network perimeter.
meta:enum.protected_by_mitigating_control: Preventive measures have been implemented that reduce the likelihood or impact of the vulnerability, or both. The controls may be referenced using the mitigatingControls property.

rating.title: Rating
rating.description: Defines the severity or risk ratings of a vulnerability.

source.description: The source that calculated the severity or risk rating of the vulnerability.

score.title: Score
score.description: The numerical score of the rating.

severity.description: Textual representation of the severity that corresponds to the numerical score of the rating.

vector.title: Vector
vector.description: Textual representation of the metric values used to score the vulnerability

justification.title: Justification
justification.description: A reason for rating the vulnerability as it was

vulnerabilitySource.title: Source
vulnerabilitySource.description: The source of vulnerability information. This is often the organization that published the vulnerability.

url.title: URL
url.description: The url of the vulnerability documentation as provided by the source.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the source.

affectedStatus.description: The vulnerability status of a given version or range of versions of a product. The statuses 'affected' and 'unaffected' indicate that the version is affected or unaffected by the vulnerability. The status 'unknown' indicates that it is unknown or unspecified whether the given version is affected. There can be many reasons for an 'unknown' status, including that an investigation has not been undertaken or that a vendor has not disclosed the status.

meta:enum.affected: The version is affected by the vulnerability.
meta:enum.unaffected: The version is not affected by the vulnerability.
meta:enum.unknown: It is unknown (or unspecified) whether the given version is affected.

weaknesses.title: Weaknesses
weaknesses.description: A collection of weaknesses, such as CWE classifications, that classify an underlying flaw.

weakness.title: Weakness
weakness.description: A classification of an underlying weakness, given either by a Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifier or by a free-text name with an optional description. The exploitability and the affected scope are expressed by the vulnerability or threat that references the weakness, not on the weakness itself.

oneOf.title: CWE Weakness
oneOf.description: A weakness identified by its CWE identifier.

bom-ref.description: An optional identifier which can be used to reference the weakness elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

cweId.title: CWE Identifier
cweId.description: The Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) identifier that classifies this weakness.

oneOf.title: Named Weakness
oneOf.description: A weakness identified by a free-text name, used where no CWE classification applies.

bom-ref.description: An optional identifier which can be used to reference the weakness elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the weakness.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the weakness.

specFormat.title: Specification Format
specFormat.description: Specifies the format. This value shall be "CycloneDX".

specVersion.title: Specification Version
specVersion.description: The version of the CycloneDX specification the BOM conforms to.

serialNumber.title: BOM Serial Number
serialNumber.description: Every BOM generated SHOULD have a unique serial number, even if the contents of the BOM have not changed over time. If specified, the serial number must conform to [RFC 4122](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4122.html). Use of serial numbers is recommended.

version.title: BOM Version
version.description: Whenever an existing BOM is modified, either manually or through automated processes, the version of the BOM SHOULD be incremented by 1. When a system is presented with multiple BOMs with identical serial numbers, the system SHOULD use the most recent version of the BOM. The default version is '1'.

components.description: A collection of components. When a metadata component is present, this array represents the inventory of components associated with that subject, forming a bill of materials. When the metadata component is omitted, the array provides component data for interchange purposes without establishing a compositional relationship.
